events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour.

events.name: New Zealand Symphony Orchestra 2026 Season
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The NZSO’s reputation for artistic excellence attracts many of the world’s leading musicians, conductors, and soloists. In 2026, some of the music-world luminaries include violinists María Dueñas and James Ehnes, trombonist Jörgen van Rijen, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason, and New Zealand piano prodigy Shan Liu.   
Wellington shows are hosted in the Michael Fowler Centre, home of the NZSO. The touring programme will see the orchestra perform over 100 concerts in over 30 communities. Be among over 100,000 people across the motu who experience live orchestra music.

events.id: KRAANIUM - Wellington - ONE NZ SHOW ONLY-154 Vivian Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: KRAANIUM - Wellington - ONE NZ SHOW ONLY
events.venue: 154 Vivian Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
KRAANIUM – AUSTRALIA & AOTEAROA TOUR MMXXVI
with SLAUGHTERCULT (NSW)
For over two decades, KRAANIUM have ruled the slam world with a blood-slick crown, dragging their twisted, groove-soaked brutality across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. Born in Norway in 2001, the band helped define slamming brutal death metal turning obscene speed and perverse groove into an international cult. Six albums deep, signed to Unique Leader Records, and still filthier than ever, Kraanium have shared the stage with everyone from Abominable Putridity to Vulvodynia, leaving nothing but broken necks and grinning corpses in their wake.
Now, for the first time ever, Kraanium are bringing their global infection down under set to unleash Slamchosis across Australia & Aotearoa this April. Expect bone-grinding riffs, relentless blast beats, and a live show so vile it should come with a biohazard warning.
This isn’t a tour, its an autopsy in motion.
The pits will open.
The riffs will hit like hammers.
April 2026. Aotearoa.
KRAANIUM IS COMING.
Mon 6th April 2026
Valhalla - Wellington/Te Whanganui-a-Tara
TICKETS ON SALE NOW AT UTR.CO.NZ

events.id: Practice As a Yogi - 6 Week Daily Yoga Morning Practice-Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Practice As a Yogi - 6 Week Daily Yoga Morning Practice
events.venue: Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The challenging opportunity to practice every day as a yogi in the morning and experience how it can change your life. We come to know our body and ourselves better through the regular daily practice, practising with full awareness and experiencing the effect of yoga on our body, mind and soul.
Going earlier to bed and not eating too much in the evening are the prerequisites to not giving up after a few days/weeks, though

According to the ancient yoga scriptures, yoga needs to be practised during the period of sunrise, when an immense amount of Prana (cosmic energy) enters our system, nourishing our body, mind, intellect, and soul. Daily practice is crucial if we want to master any skill. You will notice an increased level of energy and positive change in your mood, as well as an improvement in your attention span and concentration. You will experience more inspiration, joy, and love in your daily life.

The Beginners Course teaches the “Sarva Hitta Asanas” from Level 1 - postures that are good for everyone. The Course covers all the foundations you need for an ongoing yoga practice. Loosening your body, the joints, and your mind, the exercises are designed for the complete balance and awakening of energy in body, mind and soul. Experience yoga postures, breath techniques, meditation and relaxation, along with knowledge about basic yoga philosophy and a healthy lifestyle. A wonderful course for new beginners and those looking to deepen their practical experience of yoga.
Some of the many benefits of yoga include: improvement in posture and breathing, increased strength and flexibility, a greater ability to cope with stress, and a feeling of peace and calm.

Monday - Friday 6.30 - 7.30am

Cost: 6 weeks| $171
For this course we require booking. 04-801 7012 or info@yogaindailylife.org.nz

There is no discount for this course. Possibility to join the course any time. We will practice each week a different part from the Level 1. If you could make just one or two weeks, you might like to buy a week pass for $54. Or feel free to contact us.

events.id: Pick Your Own Apples at Molewood Orchard 2026-Molewood Orchard, 17 Mole Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Pick Your Own Apples at Molewood Orchard 2026
events.venue: Molewood Orchard, 17 Mole Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Molewood Orchard in Greytown will be opening its gates to the public on weekends for Pick Your Own apples and pears this harvest season.

Long-time Greytown residents Juliet and Ed Cooke returned to operating the Mole Street orchard in 2022, together with son Wendell. Daughter Merran is responsible for marketing and social media, and son Grayson takes care of the website and visual design. The orchard is now run as an independent family business, with the whole family pitching in to support the 2026 harvest season.

The Cooke family is looking forward to welcoming the public to the orchard for the fourth year running, introducing a new generation to the delight of picking fresh, sun-warmed apples off the tree. If the idea of spending an hour or two wandering down rows of trees laden with fresh, delicious apples appeals, we have got your weekend plans covered!

The orchard is only a short walk or bike ride from Main Street, and parking is available in the orchard grounds. Please bring a bucket, basket or bag for the fruit. You’re welcome to bring a picnic lunch as well to enjoy in the orchard's beautiful picnic area, Molewood Meadows. Sunscreen, sturdy footwear and a hat are a good idea too!

Entry to the orchard is free! Prices per kg will be updated on our Facebook page and website.

events.id: Lavender Magic Flower Farm PYO Dahlias-Lavender Magic, 61 Blakes Road, Mt Holdsworth, Carterton, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Lavender Magic Flower Farm PYO Dahlias
events.venue: Lavender Magic, 61 Blakes Road, Mt Holdsworth, Carterton, Carterton
events.long_description:
Enjoy a weekend escape at Lavender Magic. The lavender has been harvested but the sweet calming scent still lingers. We are offically into the flower season - if you love dahlias then this is the place to visit. Wander through our dahlia picking garden and gather fresh flowers — the perfect gift for friends and family.

Nestled in a charming country garden, Lavender Magic offers breathtaking views of the Tararua Ranges, creating a stunning backdrop for relaxation and discovery. Whether you’re picking flowers, strolling among the lavender, or simply soaking in the peaceful atmosphere, this is the place to unwind and reconnect with nature.

Come see the magic. Lavender Magic — where tranquility blossoms and the stars are brighter.

Lavender Magic is a Lavender farm and a Cut Flower Farm. It has about 5000 lavender plants and grows a range of cut flowers. In season you will find pockets roses, lilies, sunflowers, zinnias.

You will find over 100 dahlias in the display gardens, all labelled so you can see the plants in situ. If you intend on buying tubers, rather than checking out catalogues come see for yourself, the size of the plant, the exact colour and size of the bloom. Your visit may save you money from purchasing something that not quite right.

Since 2007 Lavender Magic has won many awards for the quality of their lavender oil, which can be purchased on site along with balms, creams and sprays in the small farm shop.

We will provide you with everything you need to pick blooms from our cutting garden. Cutting nips, rubber band and bucket. We will let you know which flowers are in season and available to cut. If you like we will turn it into a wonderful bouquet to gift or enjoy.

Our Place Cafe will be operating when available so you can pair the best coffee with your visit. Keep an eye on our socials for updates.

Plan your visit: Lavender Magic is a great place to take time out, relax and recover.
Wear - Good footwear to explore the lavender field and surrounding gardens.
Wear - Sunglasses and slip, slop, slap on if a sunny day as we get full sun.
Bring - BYO food and drink is allowed. Or pick something to eat and a Karamu Coffee from OUR PLACE CAFE who set up when available.
Bring - A rug if you want to sit in the lavender, but not on the ground. Some seating may be available on quiet days.
Bring - A journal or magazine, to ensure you take time out to reflect and relax
Bring - A friend and whanau to share your experience
Bring - A Team of friends, a pack of cards or games and just hang out for awhile
Bring - Your pet, if you are a responsible owner and promise you will pick up its mess and will not let it annoy others.
Bring - A camera to capture some of the magic and create memories.
A toilet is on site.

Bookings are not essential and you can pay at the gate.

Lavender Magic is a scenic drive, about 60 minutes from Upper Hutt and 75 minutes from Palmerston North.

Cut flower bouquets are available or can be ordered in advance to surprise someone special.

If you want something a little more private, we do hire out our property for photography and special events, contact Pauline on lavendermagic@outlook.com

How to get there:
Drive past the pizza place to get to Lavender Magic - Pizza at the Water Mill Bakery is open (Friday Only 5pm-9pm). Other things to do in the Mt Holdsworth area - Fernado Oscar Calligo may have Choripan the Argentinian Food Cart open (Check socials for dets) Paul Melser Pottery (Weekends Only), Jackie Pearson's Fine Art Gallery (Check for details), Rivenrock Mountain Biking Park (Check for details) and DOC walks (24/7) as Holdsworth is the main entrance to the eastern side of the Tararua Forest Park.

Mt Holdsworth, Carterton, Wairarapa is a great place to visit.

Join us over Easter Weekend for loads of family fun!

There's over $3,000 in prizes to be found and heaps of Easter Eggs to be won over the four days.

events.id: The Boy With Wings-Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Boy With Wings
events.venue: Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
A comic, touching, and environmental story using the ageless wonder of puppetry, live music and exquisite hand-crafted sets that magically unfold out of boxes, telling an epic fable of one young man’s life-altering journey set against the backdrop of the miracle of the great migration of Kuaka (Bar-tailed Godwits). As these fragile birds set out on their extraordinary journey from New Zealand to Alaska, watching them take to the sky in awe is Jack-the-Everyman, who is contemplating a journey of his own to seek the ‘treasure’ that will save his dying orchard. Award-winning theatre company ‘Birdlife Productions’ promise laughter, tears and thrilling adventure, enticing us to ask the question, “how do we trust our inner map - how do we find our own wings?” Suitable for ages 5 to 95!

Performers: Bridget and Roger Sanders

events.id: Eat Your Art Out: High Tea-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Eat Your Art Out: High Tea
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Eat Your Art Out brings a creative twist to high tea, inspired by the bold and brilliant works of Toi Art.

Enjoy a menu of classic treats with an artistic edge: from the Sweet William’s Dish, Golden Ratio Scone, Madeleine Moment, and the Mint to Be mousse. Each dish is a playful nod to the world of art, clever, colourful, and made to be shared.

Your high tea includes your choice of tea or coffee. Alcoholic beverages are available for purchase, and dietary options are available on request.

Every purchase helps to support Aotearoa New Zealand’s national museum.

------

Set Menu
(Includes one of each item per person)

Cheery Cherry Curator
A silky milk chocolate crémeux layered with vibrant cherry glaze and topped with a dark chocolate disc creates a perfectly balanced blend of tart and sweet

Mint to Be
Cool mint and sweet strawberry mousse gently resting on a white chocolate base, combining vibrant flavours that are meant to be together

Madeleine Moment
A citrus-infused madeleine paired with silky sambuca namelaka, vibrant green apple gel, and an elegant chocolate garnish delivers golden flavours with a playful twist

Sweet William’s Dish
Mānuka honey ganache nestled in a crisp vanilla tart shell, topped with a chocolate ‘The Dish’ by William De Morgan. A refined take on New Zealand’s liquid gold

The Golden Ratio
A perfectly proportioned cheese scone layered with sundried tomatoes, olives, and fresh basil brings Mediterranean flavours in perfect balance

The Pecking Order
Tender horopito-spiced chicken layered with creamy Tatua mayo and peppery watercress on fresh focaccia fingers, with native pepper leaf adding a bold, warming kick

Still Life with Eggplant
Tangy eggplant and native kawakawa caponata with buffalo bocconcini and fragrant basil pesto on artisanal brioche. A bold sandwich blending native botanicals and classic flavours.

Alcoholic beverages are available for purchase, and dietary options are available on request.
Every purchase helps to support Aotearoa New Zealand’s national museum.

events.id: Foxglove Bar and Kitchen Colouring Competition-Foxglove Bar and Kitchen, 33 Queens Wharf, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Foxglove Bar and Kitchen Colouring Competition
events.venue: Foxglove Bar and Kitchen, 33 Queens Wharf, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sharpen those pencils and pop the imagination caps on!


Our colouring competition is back. Let your little one’s creativity shine and be in to WIN a $100 Star Group Gift Card that can be used at any of our 50+ bars, pubs, and restaurants across the North Island.


How it works:
- Grab a colouring page at a participating venue
- Can your child wow us with the boldest colour mash-up? It's a mini challenge to see who can get the most creative!
- Hand the finished artwork to the staff to enter.


The details:
- Available from 6 April to 19 April at selected venues
- Entries close 19 April
- Two winners per venue
- Age groups: 3-7 years and 8-12 years


Where can we join in the fun, you ask?

events.id: Junior Intro to Sculpting Workshop-Wētā Workshop Group Space, 129 Park Road, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Junior Intro to Sculpting Workshop
events.venue: Wētā Workshop Group Space, 129 Park Road, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get ready to sculpt a creature of your own! Step into the magical hands-on world of Wētā Workshop, where creatures, characters and crazy-cool creations come to life.

Looking for a fun, screen-free activity to spark their imagination these school holidays? Our daily Junior Intro to Sculpting Workshop is the perfect way for young creators to explore the world of sculpture.

In this 60-minute session, children will learn how to shape and sculpt their very own three-dimensional creature using everyday materials like tinfoil and modelling clay. Young makers will create their own imaginative creature from scratch and take home at the end to add any special finishing touches they want. They'll gain new creative skills they can keep exploring long after the workshop ends.

Workshops are kept small to ensure lots of individual attention and support. Whether a budding artist or someone who just loves to make things with their hands, this is a fun and confidence-building creative experience.

Ideal for ages 6 to 11. All materials provided.

Children must be accompanied by an adult. Parents and guardians are FREE.

Our daily Junior Intro to Sculpting Workshop is the perfect way for young creators to explore the world of sculpture.
In this 60-minute session, children will learn how to shape and sculpt their very own three-dimensional creature using everyday materials like tinfoil and modelling clay.
They'll gain new creative skills they can keep exploring long after the workshop ends.

events.id: Pipi's Windy Welly Easter Egg Hunt-280 Lambton Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Pipi's Windy Welly Easter Egg Hunt
events.venue: 280 Lambton Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Pipi, the Kororā/Little Blue Penguin from Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo, has woken up to a bit of an Easter emergency… her eggs have gone missing from her nest!
The Welly wind has been at it again… sweeping Pipi’s eggs along the hill of the Wellington Cable Car.
She’s called in the Easter Bunny for backup, and now they need a team of sharp-eyed little helpers (that’s you!) to track them down.
Hop your way to the Wellington Cable Car this Easter and join the hunt.
Here’s how it works:
Pick up your egg hunt sheet from the ticket box
Keep count of all the hidden eggs you can spot in the terminals and along the track from inside the cars
Don’t forget to BYO pen!
Hand in your best guess to our ticket-box
If you correctly spot the number of Pipi’s missing eggs, you’ll be rewarded with a small Easter treat (while stocks last).
Bring your whānau and your best egg-spotting skills to help Pipi bring her eggs safely home.

Hop your way to the Wellington Cable Car this Easter and join the hunt.

events.id: Advances Yoga Class Level 3 - 4-Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Advances Yoga Class Level 3 - 4
events.venue: Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Level 3 - 4 continues with more advanced postures that build on all the previous exercises learnt, if you have systematically followed them you will find these Asanas easy and energising.

The previous excercise have increased the flexibility of the spine, improved the body's physical condition and heightened awareness to the importance of relaxation. It has been emphasised that each exercise be practised slowly, coordinating movement with the breath, pausing motionless in each position and always with full concentration. Ideal for those who would like to learn the levels of the System YIDL.
Mondays 6 - 7.15pm, $20 or concession card or Monthly pass drop in class, no booking required, for experiences practitioners.

events.id: Easter Pole Weekend 2026-55 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Easter Pole Weekend 2026
events.venue: 55 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Easter Pole Weekend – WELLINGTON, New Zealand
Friday 3rd April – Monday 6th April
16 workshops – Pole Showcase – Pole Photoshoots
*
This Easter we have an exciting four-day Pole Weekend planned at Body Electric Pole Dance Studio!
On Friday 3rd, Saturday 4th and Sunday 5th April we run our workshops. We have invited Body Electric instructors plus guests to create something special for you – there is A LOT of variety to choose from: from Pole Tricks and Flow classes to Choreography and Twerk workshops. You can choose one workshop if you want! Or we would recommend jumping in the deep end and doing several! We have a package of 6 workshops for those of you wanting to be extra hardcore. We also have a special deal of 2 workshops plus a pole show ticket for $95!
*
On Saturday night The Pole Room Easter-themed showcase “Let There Be Light” will impress and entertain with workshop instructor performances plus special guests. $30 tickets from www.eventfinda.co.nz
Monday 6th April, is devoted to pole photoshoots! Book a session with talented pole photographer Dave Giles and get pics of your favourite moves. This shoot is suitable for pole students of all levels. Maree & Giada will be there to help you with poses. $120 for 20 mins (2x 10min sessions) 4 edited images.
*
PRICES
1 workshop - $40
2 workshops, plus a ticket to the pole show - $95
6 Workshops - $210
Pole Show Tickets - $30 through Eventfinda
Pole Photo Shoot with Dave Giles - $120 for 20 min shoot (2x10 min, with room between for a costume change). 4 edited images with the option to purchase more. Help will be given by Giada and Maree for posing.
Please email us to book: info@bodyelectricpole.co.nz
WORKSHOPS (Scroll down for more detailed descriptions of workshops)
*
Friday 3rd April
10.30am Pole Flow, Leg Rotations & Flexible Hips (Ana)
12 noon Belly Dance: Making It Slinky (Pip E-Lysaah)
1.30pm Sensual Awakening: Unleash your Inner Siren (Vixen Temple)
3.00pm Nail Your Inverts (Elisa Puccioni)
4.30pm Feeling the Floor (Karina Shann)
6.00pm Choreography Kickstarter (Maree & Giada)
Saturday 4th April
10.30am Chair Tricks & Choreo (Billie Jean)
12 noon Creative Practices For Freestyle (Rachael)
1.30pm Stripper Flow (Maddie May)
3.00pm Fonji 101 (Terumi Isogai)
4.30pm Tumbles & Drops (Terumi Isogai)
Saturday 4th April, 7.30pm, The Fringe Bar, Wellington
Pole Show featuring Teachers & Guests – “Let There Be Light”
Sunday 5th April
10.30am Booty Electric: Twerkshop (Emma Hopkins)
12 noon Bendy Bunnies: Stretch (Judith Dillon)
1.30pm Hula Hoop 101 (Dylan Daisy)
3.00pm Perfect Pirouettes (Gabrielle McElwee)
4.30pm Handstands with Julia (Julia Bromley)
Workshop details
Friday 3rd April
10.30am Pole Flow, Leg Rotations & Flexible Hips (Ana)
Leg rotations can create different shapes, lines and feeling to our movement. They can elevate our pole flow and are foundational to many transitions and moves. Leg rotations start from the hips. We can make them more comfortable and access more dramatic shapes by conditioning our hip flexors and increasing our range of motion. In this workshop we will learn:
•Conditioning exercises for our hip flexors that you can add to your regular practice
•The foundational movement of leg rotations and how they can elevate and create variety in our pole moves
•A variety of dance elements that use leg rotations, that you can add to your pole flow or routines
(All levels)
12 noon Belly Dance: Making It Slinky (Pip E-Lysaah)
The magic isn’t in the big tricks. It’s in the pause before them.
In Making It Slinky, we’ll dive into the slow moments that define a performance, the entrance, the exit, and every delicious transition in between. This workshop is all about how you arrive, how you linger, and how you leave them slightly unwell. We’ll explore controlled undulations, weighted steps, breath-led movement, and the art of stretching time so the audience forgets to blink. Less rushing. More reigning.
This is signature muscle isolation and control, the kind that makes a simple belly roll wave feel scandalous. We’ll break down my structured approach to belly rolls, arm waves, and wraps ... not just how to execute them, but how to layer and thread them so nothing ever “stops.” Everything melts, coils, and unwraps with intention. You’ll learn how to build tension through transitions, turning the space between movements into something almost indecent. If you’ve ever been told to “do more,” this workshop is about doing less… slower… and making it hit harder.
(All levels)
1.30pm Sensual Awakening: Unleash your Inner Siren (Vixen Temple)
Get out of your head and into your body with Vixen Temple! In this empowering workshop, you'll explore techniques to tap into your inner sensuality and trust your body. Harness your erotic energy to move with seductive fluidity and hypnotic flow.
(All levels)
3.00pm Nail Your Inverts (Elisa Puccioni)
Elisa will guide you into a series of exercises, precise techniques, and insider tips to help you finally master those elusive inverts and leg hangs. Whether it's refining your form, building strength, or gaining more aerial control, we've got you covered! This workshop is ideal for beginners who are starting with their inverts up to intermediate students that want to polish their inverting techniques. Don't forget to BYH (bring your heels!) for an extra challenge and added flair!
(All levels)
4.30pm Feeling the Floor (Karina Shann)
In this workshop Karina will utilize fundamental floorwork moves such as shoulder stands, rolls, duck dives and body lines to guide you through ways that you can accentuate parts of your body, move with intention and captivate the audience. She’ll teach transitions and combos incorporating the fundamentals that you can take away to elevate your floor game. Heels optional but recommended. Socks, leg warmers optional, knee pads necessary.
(Prerequisite – Backwards & forwards roll)
6.00pm Choreography Kickstarter (Maree & Giada)
This workshop is perfect to get you started on your performance journey.
• Practical exercises to unlock your creativity, start building combos for your routines, and practice transitions between moves.
• Talk through how to structure a routine, performance tips and tricks, how to deal with nerves and much, much more!
This workshop is suitable for students of all levels. If you are currently working on a dance for a show, we can help with that. But you don't have to have a performance in mind to get heaps out of this session!
Saturday 4th April
10.30am Chair Tricks & Choreo (Billie Jean)
A burlesque classic – Express! Let’s get our groove on to this banger. We will learn some cute chair tricks and transitions to sass our way through this choreography. Beginner friendly, this dance is suitable for all levels! Plus, progressions for those who want to get upside down.
Wear comfortable clothes, socks or dance heels.
(All levels)
12 noon Creative Practices for Freestyle (Rachael)
Freestyling is a fantastic way to develop your movement style. This workshop will cover various concepts like tempo, sensation, texture, intention. You’ll learn new approaches for practicing freestyling and have fun while gaining a new appreciation for the practice.
(All levels)
1.30pm Stripper Flow (Maddie May)
Learn how to slink around the pole in your own authentic way, focusing on embellishing movements, fluid transitions and musicality!
(All levels)
3.00pm Fonji 101 (Terumi Isogai)
This is an introduction course for fonji related moves. Starting with preparation exercises to get used to the movement. Learn the techniques to execute fonji safely and effectively!
(Prerequisite: Shoulder mount, preferably aerial)
4.30pm Tumbles & Drops (Terumi Isogai)
You climb up the pole, and now what? Learn fun ways to come down the pole!
(Prerequisite: Aerial invert. Leg hang)

7.30pm Pole Show – “Let There Be Light”
Sunday 5th April
10.30am Booty Electric: Twerkshop (Emma Hopkins)
Come along to Emma’s beginner Twerkshop where you will learn basic twerk moves from the floor to on the pole. We will cover different styles of twerking and then put them together for a sexy, bootiful routine. We need to protect our knees while we are popping it like it's hot so please bring kneepads. Sneakers are also recommended.
(All Levels)
12 noon Bendy Bunnies: Stretch (Judith Dillon)
Level up your flexibility this Easter with fun and creative ways to move your body. Whether you've got a dedicated practice or you've never tried stretching before, all levels are welcome - Judith will adapt poses for every level and ability.
In this workshop we will focus on partner assisted stretches and stretching with props (blocks/wall) We will work on breathing into deep stretches, relaxing into passive holds, as well as muscle activation for some fun duo poses.
(All levels)
1.30pm Hula Hoop 101 (Dylan Daisy)
Learn the basics of hula hooping. Start the spin, maintain your core tension and you’re off! Using different body parts to flow and transition make hula hooping a fun and dynamic art form.
After a lifetime of commanding stages and perfecting her craft, Dylan is a fantastic and skilled hoop performer who has become a favourite in the Wellington performing arts scene. Bring a hoop if you have one (We will supply some).
(All levels)
3.00pm Perfect Pirouettes (Gabrielle McElwee)
Keen to learn some smooth ways to turn, flow and transition around the pole?
Want to improve your dance fluidity?
Then this workshop is for you! Pirouettes are a MUST HAVE for any pole dancer, so this workshop will cover pirouette technique, a variety of fun pirouette patterns & transitions which build in level, ending with a short flow sequence. Socks are recommended.
(All levels)
4.30pm Handstands with Julia (Julia Bromley)
Come and get upside down with Julia! This class workshop will focus on the basic foundations of handstands and will give you skills and drills to continue to work on towards your upside-down goals. You will be guided through exercises and drills to maximise strength and technique needed to begin or extend your handstand journey. Julia has been teaching workshops and private lessons for over 7 years in both circus, pole and dance studios and loves seeing students gain the body awareness and technique to attain their goals!
(All levels)
*
Prices:
Single Workshop session: $40 per person
2 workshops, plus a ticket to the pole show - $95
Group package: 6 Workshops - $210
events.description:

events.id: KRAANIUM - WELLINGTON - ONE NZ SHOW ONLY-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: KRAANIUM - WELLINGTON - ONE NZ SHOW ONLY
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
KRAANIUM – AUSTRALIA & AOTEAROA TOUR MMXXVI
with SLAUGHTERCULT (NSW)
For over two decades, KRAANIUM have ruled the slam world with a blood-slick crown, dragging their twisted, groove-soaked brutality across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. Born in Norway in 2001, the band helped define slamming brutal death metal turning obscene speed and perverse groove into an international cult. Six albums deep, signed to Unique Leader Records, and still filthier than ever, Kraanium have shared the stage with everyone from Abominable Putridity to Vulvodynia, leaving nothing but broken necks and grinning corpses in their wake.
Now, for the first time ever, Kraanium are bringing their global infection down under set to unleash Slamchosis across Australia & Aotearoa this April. Expect bone-grinding riffs, relentless blast beats, and a live show so vile it should come with a biohazard warning.
This isn’t a tour, its an autopsy in motion.
The pits will open.
The riffs will hit like hammers.
April 2026. Aotearoa.
KRAANIUM IS COMING.
Mon 6th April 2026
Valhalla - Wellington/Te Whanganui-a-Tara
TICKETS ON SALE NOW AT UTR.CO.NZ

events.venue: Wairarapa Services & Citizens Club, 20 Essex Street, Masterton, Masterton
events.long_description:
Australia’s Hottest Six-Packs are heading to NZ for one night only! Ladies, grab your besties and get ready for the ultimate girls’ night out as Sydney Hotshots return in 2026 with an all-new, high-energy production guaranteed to bring the heat. Expect explosive choreography, cheeky charm, jaw-dropping physiques, crowd interaction and plenty of surprises. Perfect for hen's nights, birthdays, work parties or simply a well-deserved night out, this fully choreographed show delivers non-stop fun from start to finish. Doors open 7:00PM with the show kicking off at 7:30PM. Ticket options include General Admission ($40), VIP Silver reserved second row ($65) and VIP Gold reserved front row ($80). Group bookings are available for parties of 10+, and Companion Card holders are encouraged to contact the venue to confirm acceptance and accessibility, with companion tickets available via media@sydneyhotshots.com.au
. Tickets are transferable but non-refundable — and with shows known to sell fast, secure yours now and get ready for an unforgettable night with Sydney Hotshots.

events.id: SELENE - Wellington-1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: SELENE - Wellington
events.venue: 1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A story about the goddess and the dark side of the moon.
A young girl is watching the moon landings on repeat, entranced by those astronaut’s weightlessness.
A bunch of teenagers are swimming under a lunar eclipse, lost in what might happen next.
A mis-matched couple are watching a horror film at a drive in cinema, about to step into all the next stuff.
SELENE is a radical explosion of an ancient myth.
It’s about how we grow up defined by our bodies.
It’s about the light sides of us and the dark sides of us.
And it’s about the stuff inside us. All the wild stuff inside of us.
A new sibling show to the hugely acclaimed HELIOS, the UK's "masters of storytelling" (ThreeWeeks) Wright&Grainger return to Aotearoa with SELENE - an intimate theatre experience with cinematic score and striking storytelling. Told by and made with Megan Drury.
Presented by Wright&Grainger with Theatre@41 Monkgate. NZ tour produced by A Mulled Whine Productions.
–
“This company truly are the best at storytelling” ★★★★★ - ThreeWeeks
“Wright&Grainger have a beautiful gift for taking Ancient Greek stories and hauling them into the 21st century, translating the touching and keeping the human truth intact.” ★★★★ - The Wee Review
"Through rich and evocative storytelling, Drury masterfully reveals Pandia’s world. A lyrical narrative, paired with a cracking score … we plunge into lakes, howl at the night sky, speed across the countryside on the back of a moped, dance with abandon at raves and visit the surface of the moon itself. SELENE is joyous, funny, heartfelt and life-affirming." ★★★★★ - FringeFeed
“Holds us in a space of emotional tension between the domestic and the epic, the quotidian and the cosmic … deeply affecting, funny, and heartbreaking” - Flat City Field Notes review for HELIOS
events.description:
A mis-matched couple are watching a horror film at a drive in cinema, about to step into all the next stuff.
SELENE is a radical explosion of an ancient myth.
It’s about the light sides of us and the dark sides of us.
And it’s about the stuff inside us.
A new sibling show to the hugely acclaimed HELIOS, the UK's "masters of storytelling" (ThreeWeeks) Wright&Grainger return to Aotearoa with SELENE - an intimate theatre experience with cinematic score and striking storytelling.

events.id: Mushrooms and other fungi of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush--41.267300, 174.759000-Facebook
events.name: Mushrooms and other fungi of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush
events.long_description:
Fungi are an essential part of the biodiversity and ecology of Aotearoa. Geoff Ridley will discuss the importance of fungi drawing from our knowledge of Ōtari Wilton’s Bush.
Located in Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. Gold coin donation. No need to book, just turn up!
This seminar is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual seminar series. This year, they are on the first Tuesday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.
Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

events.id: Flight Club - School Holiday Science Workshop-Space Place at Carter Observatory, 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Flight Club - School Holiday Science Workshop
events.venue: Space Place at Carter Observatory, 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
3… 2.. 1… Blast off! Tamariki learn all about the forces of flight at Space Place’s 4-hour school holiday workshop featuring rocket experiments, vortex cannons and beyond.

From making everyday objects fly with a leaf-blower, to observing what happens when you put a marshtronaut (marshmallow-astronaut) into a vacuum chamber, tamariki will come home brimming with new knowledge about how Gravity, Lift, Resistance and Thrust can be used to make cool things happen!

Suitable for ages 8 – 12, the workshop rounds off with a planetarium film.

Tamariki can be dropped-off in the morning and picked-up afterwards; caregivers are not required to stay for the duration of the workshop. Tamariki should bring a packed lunch.

7, 8, 14, 15 April | 9am – 1pm | Space Place | Ages 8-12

In this advanced four-hour course, tamariki dive into the multi-layered process behind game development, including programming, 3D modelling, game art, animation, and heaps more.

This course is great for tamariki who have already completed 3D Game Design or a similar course, but we welcome novices looking to learn game design from scratch.

events.name: Hindi Language Classes
events.venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
events.long_description:
Learn Hindi language in 8 to 12 weeks. Travelling to India? Hindi will help you explore and give you invaluable experience of incredible India.

The lesson is approximately 90 mins long one on one teaching, and I teach anyone according to your ability. Can you please let me know how much do you know about the Hindi language and why do you want to learn.

Example: are you planning to travel to India? Or just interested in learning a new language?
We teach basic writing, reading and speaking in 6 sessions, and also teach more advanced levels if anyone wants to learn more. Classes are available everyday. Day and time can be arranged according to our availability.

If the time and cost of these lessons are prohibiting you from learning the language then please let me know and I can definitely work something out for keen Hindi learners.

Email: hindiclass@xtra.co.nz
Txt: 02102697038

events.id: Intro to 2D Traditional Animation-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Intro to 2D Traditional Animation
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
This is a beginner's course in 2D animation where tweens & teens will learn the time-honoured principles of hand-drawn animation and how to apply them to their own animation project. Delivered over two morning sessions from 10 am - 1 pm on Tuesday & Wednesday, 7-8 April. Students may work on a project incorporating their own ideas, or if you'd rather, be assigned a project from your tutor.

This is a bring your own device course, and any device, iPad or laptop you already own (except a mobile – it's really not big enough to do good animation on) can be used. The school does not provide any digital equipment. It's important that the app/programme is installed on the student's device before the course begins, and that they have had a play on it to get used to how it works. This is so that the student is at least a little familiar with their chosen software so that it's enough to begin learning animation techniques on day one. Recommended 2D Animation software, including free options, is included in the course listing on our website.

events.id: School Holiday Workshop: Monster Me-The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: School Holiday Workshop: Monster Me
events.venue: The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
School Holiday Workshop: Monster Me
Create wild, monstrous self-portraits inspired by Jason Greig—ink, print, and transform yourself into fierce, funny, or furry creatures!

About this Event

Drop-off your kids for a fun school holiday workshop.

Inspired by the exhibition Blood is Thicker by Jason Greig, in this printmaking workshop we will create wild, expressive self-portraits with a monstrous twist. Drawing on Greig’s werewolf family wall, we’ll transform ourselves into strange, spooky, or silly creatures. Using printmaking techniques, we’ll ink and print bold portraits full of personality, experimenting with exaggerated features to become something completely different. Are you fierce, furry, fanged, or funny? Turn yourself into a monster and bring your alter ego to life on paper!

Create something special these school holidays at Paraparaumu Library! Tamariki are invited to draw portraits of the people (or pets!) they love – from whānau and friends to teachers and furry companions.

Tamariki can take part in fun, guided art workshops (registration required), or drop into our Makerspace craft station to create at their own pace – no booking needed. Caregivers are warmly welcome to join in too and create alongside their tamariki.

events.long_description:
The delightful words and pictures of award-winning Wellington author Ruth Paul (Lion Guards the Cake, Stomp, Anahera, I Am Jellyfish) have thrilled young readers for many years. Now all the fun is coming to the stage in a brand- new play for children.

With your HELP! Ruth and favourite characters LION, JELLYFISH, T-REX and KIWI will join forces against the MISH-MASH MONSTER to stop it eating their stories!

Romping through the April school holidays, these fun-loving characters sing songs by Ruth Paul and Charlotte Yates, with costumes by WoW veteran Fifi Colston and stage direction by Jacqueline Coats (The Adventures of Tahi & Kowhai, Party Pigs!) in a celebration of creativity, imagination and collaboration.

Tues 7 – Sun 19 April (no shows Friday 10 April)
Tues – Sat 11.30am & 1.30pm, Sunday 12 & 19 April 1.30pm & 3.30pm
Q&A with Ruth and the cast after the 1.30pm show Thursday 9 April
$20pp, $70 for a family pass (4 tickets), aged 2 and under free
Running time: 50 minutes

The delightful words and pictures of award-winning Wellington author Ruth Paul (Lion Guards the Cake, Stomp, Anahera, I Am Jellyfish) have thrilled young readers for many years.
Now all the fun is coming to the stage in a brand- new play for children.
With your HELP!
Ruth and favourite characters LION, JELLYFISH, T-REX and KIWI will join forces against the MISH-MASH MONSTER to stop it eating their stories!
Romping through the April school holidays, these fun-loving characters sing songs by Ruth Paul and Charlotte Yates, with costumes by WoW veteran Fifi Colston and stage direction by Jacqueline Coats (The Adventures of Tahi & Kowhai, Party Pigs!)

events.id: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This beginner level class is designed for students who are new to jewellery making, with everyone following the same guided project. It starts on Tue 7 April and runs for 8 weeks till Tue 26 May, 5.30pm - 8pm.

Our popular weeknight classes are a welcoming and hands-on way to get started, offering a lively, friendly studio environment and plenty of support along the way.

The stone setting process teaches you many fundamental techniques in one project. You’ll begin by learning basic ring-making and stone-setting skills in copper or brass, including measuring, texturing, shaping, cutting and soldering.

From there, you’ll make a practice ring set with a cabochon stone (a stone with a flat base and smooth, domed top) in a classic bezel setting. Once you’re confident, you can choose to progress to working in silver to create a second ring using a stone you love, or continue developing a more ambitious design in copper and brass.

The class fee includes copper, brass and two gemstones. Silver is available for purchase in the studio if you’d like to work with precious metal for your second stone set ring.

You’ll have a beautiful selection of beginner-friendly stones to choose from, in a wide range of colours.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time, please email us to arrange this ;)

events.id: SELENE-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: SELENE
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
A story about the goddess and the dark side of the moon.

A young girl is watching the moon landings on repeat, entranced by those astronaut’s weightlessness.
A bunch of teenagers are swimming under a lunar eclipse, lost in what might happen next.
A mis-matched couple are watching a horror film at a drive in cinema, about to step into all the next stuff.

SELENE is a radical explosion of an ancient myth.
It’s about how we grow up defined by our bodies.
It’s about the light sides of us and the dark sides of us.
And it’s about the stuff inside us. All the wild stuff inside of us.

A new sibling show to the hugely acclaimed HELIOS, the UK's "masters of storytelling" (ThreeWeeks) Wright&Grainger return to Aotearoa with SELENE - an intimate theatre experience with cinematic score and striking storytelling. Told by and made with Megan Drury.

Presented by Wright&Grainger with Theatre@41 Monkgate. NZ tour produced by A Mulled Whine Productions.

–

“This company truly are the best at storytelling” ★★★★★ - ThreeWeeks

“Wright&Grainger have a beautiful gift for taking Ancient Greek stories and hauling them into the 21st century, translating the touching and keeping the human truth intact.” ★★★★ - The Wee Review

"Wonderfully rich storytelling blends myth with a deeply human experience" ★★★★★ - The Barefoot Review

"A tender, myth-tinged coming-of-age story." ★★★★ - The List

"Through rich and evocative storytelling, Drury masterfully reveals Pandia’s world. A lyrical narrative, paired with a cracking score … we plunge into lakes, howl at the night sky, speed across the countryside on the back of a moped, dance with abandon at raves and visit the surface of the moon itself. SELENE is joyous, funny, heartfelt and life-affirming." ★★★★★ - FringeFeed

"Magnetic - luminous without sentimentality, fierce without sacrificing vulnerability" - Theatre Travels

“We finish with full hearts, like half-drunk teens leaping into the moonlit lake together, the world open and full of possibility, and tears in everyone’s eyes.” - Flat City Field Notes
events.description:
A story about the goddess and the dark side of the moon.
A mis-matched couple are watching a horror film at a drive in cinema, about to step into all the next stuff.
SELENE is a radical explosion of an ancient myth.
And it’s about the stuff inside us.
Told by and made with Megan Drury.

Fungi are an essential part of the biodiversity and ecology of Aotearoa. Geoff Ridley will discuss the importance of fungi drawing from our knowledge of Ōtari Wilton’s Bush.

events.id: Champion Brass — New Zealand Brass Band Championships 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Champion Brass — New Zealand Brass Band Championships 2026
events.long_description:
More than 1000 competitors blow into Wellington in April to take part in one of the oldest music competitions in the world. The annual National Brass Band Championships attract competing bands from across Aotearoa, Australia, and beyond. Together, they represent the spirit of community music-making at its pinnacle. This year, 40 bands, including one travelling from Tonga, will compete across four grades. 
The Championships are more than just a competition. They are an annual institution with a huge sense of camaraderie between competing musicians. It is a celebration of everything that is great about live music and therefore attracts a wide audience of supporters. 
The highlight of the week is a gala performance from the National Band of New Zealand. The 12 April concert features top musicians selected from competing bands plus guest soloists. Other events are free and open to the public. This includes solo, chamber, and full-band performances, the Champion of Champions concert, and a masterclass with the principal trombonist of the Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra, Jorgen Van Rijen.

Together, they represent the spirit of community music-making at its pinnacle.
The Championships are more than just a competition.
The highlight of the week is a gala performance from the National Band of New Zealand.

events.id: The Pogues — Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Pogues — Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s been 35 years since The Pogues were in New Zealand, and 40 years since the release of their most famous album. Now is your chance to hear them live singing songs from their iconic 1985 album ‘Rum, Sodomy & the Lash’. Not only will you get the full album, but there will also be other much-loved favourites. Hear A Pair of Brown Eyes, Dirty Old Town, A Rainy Night in Soho, and plenty more. 
Helping bring this re-creation to life are original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer and Spider Stacy. There is also a special lineup of acclaimed guest vocalists, Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn. Together they’ll present a soul-stirring celebration of one of modern music’s most enduring legacies. 
The Pogues formed in 1982 and fused punk with traditional Irish music. They originally called themselves Pogue Mahone. This phrase is an anglicisation of the Irish for póg mo thóin, meaning “kiss my arse”.

events.id: F*ck the Small Talk: Wellington #3-15 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: F*ck the Small Talk: Wellington #3
events.venue: 15 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sign up here: https://luma.com/ftst3wellington
F*ck the Small Talk has traveled the world from Berlin, Melbourne, London, Lisbon, and Mexico City, to Lisbon and is now coming to Wellington!

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?
FTST is a one-of-a-kind social experience where strangers come together for an afternoon of meaningful, fun, and deep conversation. It’s also just a different & cool way of meeting new people by getting a bit out of your comfort zone and skipping the small talk!
Join us for games, talks, and connections with friends you haven’t met yet. Join if you get annoyed after being asked what you do, where you are from, and how long you have been in Wellington every single time you meet someone. If you feel that there are better questions in life then this is the place for you.

HOW DOES IT WORK?
We start with a short & sweet intro talk followed by a few light-hearted group games to warm up. Don't worry, nothing too awkward! After that, we split you up into small groups and start with the conversation rounds (with 3-4 people). We'll have awesome question cards that will guide the flow of the conversation. You’ll never have to speak in public if you don’t want to!

HOW TO JOIN?
Take 2 minutes to fill out our form

https://luma.com/ftst3wellington
Our events are limited to 30 handpicked guests to ensure a cozy atmosphere. We prioritize a balance in gender and cultural diversity, making each gathering a unique blend of perspectives! If you're invited you'll receive an invite with a special link via our ticketing platform Luma

HOW MUCH IS IT?
The cost to attend is NZD 23 (early llama) - NZD 25 (general llama) and includes the following:
3 hours of facilitated and curated activities
Light snacks to keep the energy going (only if outdoors)
A space to meet new people in the least boring way possible
Supporting our mission to slowly eliminate small talk around the world
Also includes those pesky ticket fees!

WHERE IS IT?
We host our events in awesome cafes, rooftops, and bars across the city. We only reveal the location to ticket holders 24 hours before the event.

WHAT THEY SAY
“Great way to connect with people in a more meaningful way. Get to the heart of the matter, dive straight into what really counts!” - Matthias

“This is the kind of activity humanity needs every weekend. Thank you for all.” - Sonya

“F*ck the Small Talk changes the narrative from "Where are you from?" to "When was the last time you laughed uncontrollably?" and I think that's beautiful.” - Monika

“I loved F*ck the small talk, I was a little bit nervous at first but they are pros at making you feel comfortable with the rest of the group, the activities are really fun and I enjoyed every second (consider that the one who's saying this is very, very shy). Give it a try!” - Brenda

"it’s a wonderful experience, it’s like being a kid again, when you were at the park, playing with other kids, and just decided you were going to be best friends for the next hours. Maybe you’ll never see those kids again. But that hour was amazing because you both opened up and trusted each other." - Sara

“Lively, interesting, introspective, completely open and no judgement. Everyone that joined was ready for great conversation and helped with different perspectives. So much fun! I can't wait for the next one! “ - Yaz

Check out our 100+ Google reviews: https://goo.gl/maps/TpZy77bfPFkGoikn7

FAQ
• Can I come alone?
Of course, in fact, we encourage it! 99% of people do.

• I signed up but never got an invite. Why?
We usually get a high volume of sign-ups yet we like to keep it cozy & only about 30 people. We believe this is a good number to ensure an awesome experience! Please try again next time if do not receive an invite - no hard feelings

• Why do you charge?
We value our time and put a lot of love, planning, and effort into creating & designing these experiences.
This is our project/business and what we give to the world and we think everything should be a fair exchange! However, money should never be the reason for people not to attend. If you can’t afford it simply get in touch with us

• I'm digging this! Can I get involved somehow?
Sure thing, send us a message!

• Can I bring my cute dog/llama?
It depends on where the event is! Message us to make sure. But it's always a YES in our hearts.

• Can I get a refund if I can't make it?
Unfortunately, we do not offer refunds or ticket transfers to another event date if you cannot attend with a notice of less than 5 days notice. BUTTTT feel free to pass on your ticket to a friend, provided they also sign up for the form
--

𝘾𝙤𝙢𝙚 𝙘𝙪𝙧𝙞𝙤𝙪𝙨, 𝙡𝙚𝙖𝙫𝙚 𝙘𝙤𝙣𝙣𝙚𝙘𝙩𝙚𝙙.
* Only 30 spots available
* Location is only revealed to ticket holders!
* Age guide, 22-40 years old (only a reference, never a limit
)

This event is a no-phone experience. Keep them tucked in your pocket

We started in Berlin back in 2019 out our frustration of being asked 'where are you from?' 40 times in one week. Now there are chapters in 11 cities around the world including Mexico City, Zurich, Lisbon, London, Berlin and Vancouver.

--

events.long_description:
Swing into the golden age of jazz at the Swing Jazz Jam - a toe-tapping evening of classic grooves and spontaneous musical magic!
Featuring piano, guitar, double bass, and drums, this open jam invites musicians and jazz lovers alike to soak up the rhythm and energy of timeless swing ?
Whether you’re here to play or just to sway, come down to Undercurrent on Wednesday 8th April at 8pm.
Koha entry - all welcome.

events.id: First World War Memorial Walk-Bolton Street Cemetery, Bolton Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: First World War Memorial Walk
events.venue: Bolton Street Cemetery, Bolton Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
In honour of Anzac Day, embark on a self-guided journey around the memorials dedicated to the 18 servicemen who valiantly fought in the Great War, many of whom never returned home. Discover their touching stories and take a moment to pause, read, reflect, and honour their memory.

This self-guided activity is available from dawn to dusk. Simply pick up a map and locate the white crosses next to their graves to uncover each soldier's unique narrative.

Maps are available at both entrances to the Bolton Street Cemetery, inside the Bolton Street Cemetery Museum, or you can download a digital version by copying and pasting this link: https://wellingtongardens.nz/assets/PDF-Brochures/J018060-WorldWarOneMemorial-Brochure.pdf

events.id: Health and Safety Essentials for Small Business-Business Central,Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Health and Safety Essentials for Small Business
events.venue: Business Central,Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Health and Safety Essentials for Small Business
This course will give you the oversight you need to ensure that your business health and safety systems and processes are up to date.

About this Event

The purpose of this course is to give you the oversight you need to ensure your business health and safety systems and processes reflect a best-practice approach. Each session comes with practical templates for you to use in your business to comply with the Health & Safety at Work Act 2015. It also supports your understanding of the core elements of a health and safety system.


Key Audience:
This course is suitable for people who own a small business and want to ensure they comply with health and safety best practice requirements. It is also good for those businesses without a health and safety committee and anyone who is required to be involved in health and safety in a small business.


Learning outcomes
Understand the core elements of a health and safety system.
Understand the key duty holders and their respective duties under the Act.
Understand essential health and safety components and how they relate to your legal duties.
Understand practical ways to increase the effectiveness of your health and safety system.

If you would like to register with Business Central directly and receive an invoice for payment, please contact our training team via .

Places are limited, so register today.


Course details
Duration: One day, in person.
Time: 9am - 4pm


Course includes
Workbook, arrival tea or coffee, morning tea, lunch and afternoon tea.

To view our Terms and Conditions click here.

events.id: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - MIRAMAR-Miramar Central School, 38 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - MIRAMAR
events.venue: Miramar Central School, 38 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Introducing our new updated “Ancient Runes Puzzle Box”. Keep your treasures safe in the coolest way possible. Only you will know the special code to release the catch hidden under the mechanical Iris! This new improved version has 2 code wheels that you can use to set your own unique code, as well as 2 code pins that are stored on the box but have to be inserted correctly to activate the mechanism. If everything is set a hidden push button will then activate a solenoid that releases the catch.

Powered by 4 AAA batteries (provided), you’ll get to solder the components, screw and glue all the intricate pieces together and unwrap the puzzle to learn how it works. And, of course, you'll get to take it home! No previous experience is required and you’re welcome to bring a grown up if you want to.

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $135 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.id: Advanced Game Design (ages 8-10)-Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Advanced Game Design (ages 8-10)
events.venue: Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington
events.long_description:
Dive into game development these April school holidays with Nōku te Ao Capital E!

In this advanced four-hour course, tamariki dive into the multi-layered process behind game development, including programming, 3D modelling, game art, animation, and heaps more.

This course is great for tamariki who have already completed 3D Game Design or a similar course, but we welcome novices looking to learn game design from scratch.

Tamariki are encouraged to bring a small packed lunch and a water bottle.

At Te Taiwhanga Arapāho MediaLab, Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central.

Places are limited and bookings are essential for each tamariki that wishes to take part.

Book now: https://www.capitale.org.nz/event/advanced-game-design-8-10/

events.id: String Bean Puppets: Once In a Full Moon-ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: String Bean Puppets: Once In a Full Moon
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Be captivated these school holidays by the gorgeous puppet show, Once in a Full Moon, performed by Anna Bailey of String Bean Puppets.

Deep in the bush, a lonely kiwi looks up at the sky and mistakes the full moon for a giant egg. Can kiwis fly? Can moons fall from the sky? Can wishes come true? This magical string puppet adventure introduces the audience to an intimate encounter with a few of Aotearoa New Zealand's special native creatures.

This show will be held in the Visitor Centre Foyer and is suitable for all ages; normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street.

events.name: Everybody Streets | Fundraising Lunch
events.venue: Everybody Eats Wellington,60 Dixon Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Everybody Streets | Fundraising Lunch
Help our students raise funds for Kaibosh and Everybody Eats. Enjoy a casual lunch highlighting local Asian street food.

About this Event

Everybody Streets is a fundraising event collaborating with Kaibosh and Everybody Eats. It is a casual lunch dining highlighting local asian street food from different countries (Indonesia, Philippines, Thailand, Korea, Sri-Lanka). Coordinated by Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand's 2nd Year Bachelor of Culinary Arts and Business Students.
Important Note: Tables are arranged per order. If you purchase tickets separately you need to email to advise which other ticket holders you wish to be seated with.
Date: Wednesday 8th April
Price: $50 per person
Booking Time: 12:00 pm
Dietary Requirements: Our menu is set. Please notify us in advance if you have any dietary requirements and allergens. Please be aware that the menu includes meat, soy, dairy, gluten and nut components.


The estimated time to enjoy the full Asian Street Food dining experience is 2 hours. We invite you to sit back, relax and enjoy our students' creations.
Come and join our Everybody Streets and enjoy the wonders of local asian street food!

events.id: Otis Prescott-Mason, solo piano-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Otis Prescott-Mason, solo piano
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Programme:

Mozart - Sonata in D major, K.311

Liszt - Étude in Db major, ‘Harmonie du soir’, S.139, No.11

Chopin - Barcarolle, Op.60

Biography:

Otis Prescott-Mason is one of New Zealand’s leading young pianists. He currently studies in Wellington under Dr Jian Liu and, in the United States, under Professor Boris Berman.

In 2025 Otis was awarded 3rd prize at the National Concerto Competition in Christchurch, performing Prokofiev’s 3rd concerto with the Christchurch Symphony. In 2022 he was awarded 1st prize, Best Performance of a Classical Sonata, and the Audience Choice Award at the Lewis Eady National Piano Competition. In the same year he was awarded 1st prize at the PACANZ National Piano Competition. Otis was also awarded 1st prize at the 2020 New Zealand Junior Piano Competition.

Now finished with undergraduate studies at the New Zealand School of Music in Wellington, Otis plans to pursue graduate study abroad in the near future.

He currently studies in Wellington under Dr Jian Liu and, in the United States, under Professor Boris Berman.

events.name: Yoga Nidra - Deep Relaxation Technique
events.venue: Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Also called "The deep sleep of the Yogi". Yoga Nidra is an efficient method for stress relief and has an immense rejuvenating effect.
Yoga Nidra is the Yogic Way of Sleep or 'Psychic sleep'. It is a traditional yogic technique of deep relaxation, usually practiced while lying on the back.
Yoga Nidra is a technique for getting rid of everyday stress and finding inner peace and relaxation.

Cost: $10

events.name: Sarva Hitta Asanas - 6 Week Yoga Beginners Course
events.venue: Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Starting: 8 April 2026
Wednesdays 5.45 - 7.15pm (once a week)
Possibility to join in
9 week course for $150/*$130 conc. for full time students and holders of services community card

For this course we require booking via our booking system.

For this course we require booking via our booking system.
IMPORTANT NOTE: In order to book a course, you will first need to purchase a course membership plan. Go to Memberships and select Buy Membership. Select the appropriate membership plan to purchase the course membership. Then come back here to the course timetable and book the course.

If you need an advice, please contact us via email: info@yogaindailylife.org.nz or then via phone: 04-801 7012


The Beginners Course teaches the “Sarva Hitta Asanas” from Level 1 - postures that are good for everyone. The Course covers all the foundations you need for an ongoing yoga practice as well as plenty of time for guided relaxation. Loosening your body, the joints, and your mind, the exercises are designed for the complete balance and awakening of energy in body, mind and soul. Experience yoga postures, breath techniques, meditation and relaxation, along with knowledge about basic yoga philosophy and a healthy lifestyle. A wonderful course for new beginners and those looking to deepen their practical experience of yoga.

events.id: Plant-Based Craft Club-Toi Poneke Arts Centre,Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Plant-Based Craft Club
events.venue: Toi Poneke Arts Centre,Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Plant-Based Craft Club
Haere mai planty weavers all across the spectrum of craft! This club is for plant craft lovers who want to make alongside other plant craft lovers. BYO project, some kai to share if you would like (not expected), and your lovely plant enthusiastic self.

This is a space for you if you want to develop your skills in community through weaving together with any plants and techniques you please - think cordage/baskets/mats/containers in Harakeke, Ti Kouka, Willow, Ivy, etc etc! This space will be facilitated by MicroMill tutors who are keen to help you think through your projects/practices and weave threads of connection and collaboration across craft spaces. And who knows - maybe we can have some informal wee foraging outings between club sessions too? The sky is the limit.

Appropriate tīkanga for working with and around various plants will be shared at the beginning of each session. By attending you have a responsibility to respect this tīkanga while in the space.

There will not be any formal instruction, club costs are intended to cover the venue and create a supported space for us to learn through doing, together.

Sign up on micromill.org

events.id: Quiz Night-Duke of Wellington, 29 Waterloo Quay, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Quiz Night
events.venue: Duke of Wellington, 29 Waterloo Quay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Every Wednesday is quiz night at one of Wellington's best venues - Duke of Wellington!

Kicking off at 6pm, the quiz offers five rounds of big-screen trivia that's not too hard, not too easy, but includes content that relates to you and your crew! So if you've got some street smarts, a good attitude, and a dash of luck, you'll be a good chance on the night.

Add to the mix some bonus games, great food & drink, a stack of prizes, great banter and laughs aplenty, it's the place to be on a Wednesday for a rollicking good time!

events.venue: The Thistle Inn, 3 Mulgrave St, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Thistle Inn presents: Wednesdays with Munro

Join us for Wednesdays with Munro, a four-month live music series celebrating a rich mix of genres, moods, and musical traditions.
Held on the first Wednesday of each month at 7pm, this intimate program showcases the Michael Munro Band in a different themed performance for each night:


Wednesday 8th April – Michael Munro Band performing ‘Retro-Rock Turned Lounge’. A 5-piece combo to kick off the ‘1st Wednesday of the month’ program.

Wednesday 6th May – Michael Munro Band performing an evening of ‘Latino and Bossas’.

Wednesday 3rd June – Michael Munro Band performing a French music-evening ‘Bon Soir'

Wednesday 1st July – Michael Munro Band performing ‘King of Swing'.


Relax, enjoy great food and drinks, and experience a new musical journey each month at the Thistle Inn!

events.id: Kauri Theatre Presents: Catch Me If You Can the Musical-Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Kauri Theatre Presents: Catch Me If You Can the Musical
events.venue: Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Seeking fame and fortune, precocious teenager Frank Abagnale, Jr., runs away from home to begin an unforgettable adventure. With nothing more than his boyish charm, a big imagination and millions of dollars in forged checks, Frank successfully poses as a pilot, a doctor and a lawyer – living the high life and winning the girl of his dreams. When Frank's lies catch the attention of FBI agent Carl Hanratty, Carl pursues Frank across the country to make him pay for his crimes.

Based on the incredible true story and hit film, Catch Me If You Can is the high-flying musical comedy about chasing your dreams and not getting caught. Nominated for four Tony awards, including Best Musical, this delightfully entertaining show was created by a Tony Award-winning "dream team," with a book by Terrence McNally (The Full Monty, Ragtime) and a swinging score by Marc Shaiman and Scott Wittman (Hairspray).

Content: Catch Me If You Can includes depictions of alcoholism, includes prop guns, and loud noises (i.e., jet engines and gunshots).

When Frank's lies catch the attention of FBI agent Carl Hanratty, Carl pursues Frank across the country to make him pay for his crimes.
Based on the incredible true story and hit film, Catch Me If You Can is the high-flying musical comedy about chasing your dreams and not getting caught.

events.id: Quiet Days-Home of Compassion, 2 Rhine St, Island Bay, Wellington 6023, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Quiet Days
events.venue: Home of Compassion, 2 Rhine St, Island Bay, Wellington 6023, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The theme of this Quiet Day is Joy!
Eastertide provides us with the opportunity to reflect on the life of Jesus and celebrate the resurrection.
Where is God turning our grief into joy? What does it mean to live into the joy of the resurrection? How might we embody joy in our lives? We will spend time gently reflecting on the joy of the resurrection and the hope of Eastertide.
10:30-2.30pm at Chapel.

events.id: THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington-St. James Theatre,83 Courtenay Pl, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington
events.venue: St. James Theatre,83 Courtenay Pl, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington
The Pogues make their long-awaited return to NZ stages with a special 40th-anniversary celebration of their classic album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash!

The tour brings together original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer, and Spider Stacy, alongside a stellar lineup of guest vocalists Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn. They’re joined by musicians Holly Mullineaux, Jordan O’Leary, Fiachra Meek, Jim Sclavunos, Pete Fraser, Daniel Hayes, and Ian Williamson, performing the album in full plus other fan favourites.

Released in 1985, Rum, Sodomy & the Lash remains a cornerstone of alternative music — fusing traditional Irish folk with punk and poetic storytelling. The album features some of The Pogues’ most celebrated songs, including A Pair of Brown Eyes, Dirty Old Town, and A Rainy Night in Soho.

Returning for their first NZ tour in over 35 years, The Pogues promise an unforgettable night honouring one of the most influential albums in modern music.

The tour brings together original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer, and Spider Stacy, alongside a stellar lineup of guest vocalists Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn.
They’re joined by musicians Holly Mullineaux, Jordan O’Leary, Fiachra Meek, Jim Sclavunos, Pete Fraser, Daniel Hayes, and Ian Williamson, performing the album in full plus other fan favourites.
Released in 1985, Rum, Sodomy & the Lash remains a cornerstone of alternative music — fusing traditional Irish folk with punk and poetic storytelling.

events.id: The Pogues - Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary-St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Pogues - Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary
events.venue: St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bringing the spirit of Bluesfest to stages across New Zealand, Bluesfest Tours are proud to present one of next year’s most anticipated Bluesfest artists - The Pogues, who will perform three New Zealand sideshows nationwide, celebrating 40 years of their iconic album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash.

All tickets through Ticketmaster | Access all show tickets from Bluesfesttours.com.au

Now, for the first time in 35 years, The Pogues return to New Zealand to celebrate the 40th Anniversary of their seminal album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash — performed in full alongside beloved favourites.

Featuring original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer and Spider Stacy, this special line-up unites acclaimed guest vocalists Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn, bringing one of folk-punk’s most influential albums back to life. Ranked among Rolling Stone’s 500 Greatest Albums of All Time, the 1985 classic features ‘A Pair of Brown Eyes’, ‘Dirty Old Town’, and ‘A Rainy Night in Soho’.

The Pogues are just one of the many world-class artists performing as part of Australia’s Bluesfest 2026 line-up, who have collectively sold tens of millions of albums, earned multiple GRAMMY Awards and Rock & Roll Hall of Fame honours, and defined generations of live music.

The Pogues’ New Zealand tour and Bluesfest return promise a rousing, soul-stirring celebration of one of modern music’s most enduring legacies.

All tickets through Ticketmaster | Access all show tickets from Bluesfesttours.com.au Now, for the first time in 35 years, The Pogues return to New Zealand to celebrate the 40th Anniversary of their seminal album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash — performed in full alongside beloved favourites.

events.id: Daniel Sloss — Bitter-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Daniel Sloss — Bitter
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description: He’s one of the biggest global names in live stand-up comedy. Daniel Sloss is heading to The Opera House to regale a Wellington audience with his edgy brand of comedy. ‘Bitter’ is an opportunity to experience Daniel’s brilliant and devastatingly hilarious delivery. His impact on the comedy scene has been impressive. Now 35, Daniel has been peddling his dark sense of humour for over half his life. At the age of 19, he was the youngest comedian to perform a solo season in London’s West End. Now, on to solo show number 13, he is one of the top ticket-selling comedians on the planet. His extensive tours have taken him to 55 countries and counting.

events.id: Book binding - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for teens & adults-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Book binding - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for teens & adults
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this workshop for ages 12+ you will be guided in assembling your chosen materials for your book, create decorative covers and learn Coptic binding - an elegant and simple binding that is unlimited in versatility.

No special tools or materials are needed and yet a wide spectrum of creative results can be created, from a notebook, sketch book and album to a highly crafted artwork. The books themselves can be made from found items, recycled material or quality art papers and card. You will produce one bound book and one bound album. Once you have Coptic binding in your skill set you can produce your choice of books in any shape or form, for any purpose.

All basic materials supplied.

events.id: Whānau Print Making-ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Whānau Print Making
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us at Zealandia these school holidays for a free, nature-inspired craft event!

Local artist Tayla Hartemink has designed a series of unique stamps featuring beautiful native manu and ika, perfect for decorating cards, and printing fabric and clothing. A limited amount of materials will be provided, but we encourage you to bring your own T-shirts, tote bags, tea towels, or other fabric items to personalise with these stunning designs.

Tayla Hartemink (Tūhoe) is a multi-media artist, illustrator and creative workshop facilitator based in Te-Whanganui-ā-Tara. Tayla’s work is rooted in a deep connection to whakapapa and indigenous storytelling. Her work incorporates graphic design, painting, print-making and traditional motifs. Through her artwork, she hopes to inspire and empower people to connect with Te Ao Māori.

Suitable for all ages— drop in, get hands-on, and make something colourful to take home!

This show event will be held in the Visitor Centre Foyer and is suitable for all ages; normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street.
