events.id: The Silk Road Journey | Fundraising Lunch-Everybody Eats Wellington,60 Dixon Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: The Silk Road Journey | Fundraising Lunch
events.venue: Everybody Eats Wellington,60 Dixon Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Silk Road Journey | Fundraising Lunch
Help our students raise funds for Kaibosh and Everybody Eats. Enjoy a 3-course vegetarian lunch inspired by the legendary trade route.

About this Event

The Silk Road Journey is a fundraising event collaborating with Kaibosh and Everybody Eats. This charity lunch celebrates the vibrant flavours of the Silk Road through a carefully curated 3-course vegetarian menu, where each dish tells a story of cultural exchange and culinary heritage.
In collaboration with Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand, Kiabosh, and Everybody Eats, this event brings people together through food while supporting community dining and sustainability.
Important Note: Tables are arranged per order. If you purchase tickets separately you need to email to advise which other ticket holders you wish to be seated with.
Date: Thursday 9th April
Price: $65 per person
Booking Time: 11:30 am
Dietary Requirements: Our menu is set. Please notify us in advance if you have any dietary requirements and allergens. Please be aware that the menu includes soy, dairy, gluten and nut components.
The estimated time to enjoy the full dining experience is 2 hours. We invite you to sit back, relax and enjoy our students' creations.

events.id: Cooking Classes-Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cooking Classes
events.venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
events.long_description:
Authentic Indian cooking classes in Wellington with panaromic views over Wellington Harbour.

In each class, you will learn 4-5 recipes like: potato stuff roti, roasted eggplant curry, wholemeal, roti, naan; shahi paneer; dhal, samosa; palak paneer; gourmet chickpeas; vegetable korma; gulab jamun; kheer; mango lassi; masala chai, and many more...

The classes start at 6 pm or 10:30 am on weekends and weekdays with a 2-hour session followed by a dinner or lunch afterwards. The ingredients, copies of recipes, organic karma drink will be provided with a full course meal in a banquet setting. Custom made classes available for gluten free, dairy free and any other food intolerance and dietary requirements.The ingredients, recipes, complimentary organic karma drinks will be provided with a full course meal.

There are 2 to 8 people in each class in purpose built cooking studio. Also a private cooking classes available.

The menu for the class can be:

1) Spicy potato stuffed paratha and roti, rajasthani tomato relish, aalu matter or spicy peas- potato curry, roasted cumin yogurt raita and fresh mint- coriander chutney.

2) Shahi Paneer (cottage cheese cubes with spices and rich tomato-cashew gravy), garlic coriander naan, cumin jeera basmati rice and sweet fragrant rice pudding with cardamom. Also I will teach how to make fresh paneer.

3) Roasted eggplant curry, plain whole wheat chapatti or roti, jeera aaloo or potato curry, Tuar dahl (split yellow lentil) and Basmati rice with homemade pickle.

4) Palak paneer (creamy spinach curry with cubes of paneer), jeera rice (basmati rice with fragrant spices, panjabi chhole or spicy chickpeas curry and mango lassi. Also fresh paneer making.

5) Khadi (chickpeas fritters in yogurt and chickpeas flour soupy gravy), Aloo-Gobhi (cauliflower and potato curry),  savoury wraps with herbs and spices, fresh pickle and basmati rice.

The total cost is $130 per person, this includes ingredient cost. (As an alternative we also sell cooking class gift vouchers.The food options are unlimited and the class provides fresh and best ingredients)
For booking a class please contact indiancooking@xtra.co.nz

Just bring your taste buds! and Enjoy!!

events.id: Rise & Shine: Start before you’re ready, back yourself and take the leap.-1 Whitmore Street-All Events In
events.name: Rise & Shine: Start before you’re ready, back yourself and take the leap.
events.venue: 1 Whitmore Street
events.long_description:
Rise & Shine: Start before you’re ready, back yourself and take the leap.
Kick-Arse Meetup for Wellington Business Women Hosted by Patrice Green, with Viv Conway. Powered by Wellington Beds and BNZ.

About this Event

Rise & Shine: Kick-Arse Meetup for Wellington’s Women in Business powered by Wellington Beds and BNZ.

Join us for on Thursday 9th of April, at 6:00pm for an exciting in-person event designed for Wellington Women in Business.
Step into the dynamic and driven world of Rise and Shine, where the spirit of Wellington’s businesswomen comes alive with energy, ambition, and enterprise.
Hosted by Patrice Green and powered by Wellington Beds, this series is dedicated to supporting women who are building, leading and creating change in business.
We’re excited to connect you with Viv Conway, co-founder of Girls Get Off, at the beautiful BNZ Wellington Partners Centre, Level 1, 1 Whitmore Street. The entrance is on the corner of Featherston and Ballance Street.

When you know what you want and need, everything changes.

Join Patrice as she hosts Viv Conway this November to take the chaos out of 2026 and design a year that feels as good as it looks.
Viv is part of a new generation of founders building bold, high-growth businesses and challenging traditional ideas about women’s products and markets.
In this conversation with Patrice, Viv will share the story behind Girls Get Off and what it has taken to grow a brand in the women’s sexual wellness space. She will talk about the realities of launching and scaling a business in a category that has often been misunderstood or overlooked, and the resilience required to keep moving forward when the path is not always straightforward.
Her journey is a powerful example of what can happen when women identify gaps in the market, back their ideas, and build communities around products that truly serve women.
Expect an honest conversation about entrepreneurship, resilience, community building and what it takes to create something new in a space where the rules are still being written.

What to expect:
6.00pm - Arrival
✨ 30 - minute keynote with Viv Conway
🔥 15 - minute fireside chat with Patrice Green - a candid conversation about entrepreneurship, leadership, and building a bold brand
🎤 15 - minute interactive Q&A - your chance to ask Viv your questions
🥂 7.30 - 8pm - Networking drinks and nibbles - connect with like-minded women and expand your network

This is a women-only event for ambitious, curious, and courageous women. Whether you are in corporate, running your own business, or planning your next move, this is the room you want to be in.
Expect to leave with insights, encouragement, and new connections.
Bring a friend or come solo. We’ll make sure you don’t sip your drink alone.
Seats are limited, so secure your spot and join us for another inspiring Rise and Shine event.

About our events:
Each event is a vibrant venue for visionary growth, where trailblazing speakers share their paths and insights, paving the way for others to follow. It’s not just a meeting, it’s a movement fueled by ambition and the collective power of women who are ready to lead.
These sessions are designed to inspire, challenge, and equip you with the tools to take your business and personal growth to new heights. With a focus on forging lasting connections that grow into business opportunities, ‘Rise and Shine’ is a kick-arse community ready to welcome the next trailblazer - YOU. Get ready to be inspired, to inspire, and to turn every interaction into a stepping stone for success.
Beyond networking, this is a platform for vibrant, interactive conversations. Each event spotlights a trailblazing guest, introduced and conversed with by none other than Patrice herself. But here’s the twist: the floor opens to you, offering a chance to engage directly and ask questions.
At this meetup, you'll have the opportunity to have networking drinks and nibbles while sharing your experiences, ideas, and challenges with fellow businesswomen. It's a perfect occasion to gain valuable insights, learn from each other's successes, and build meaningful connections.
This Kick-Arse Meetup, powered by Wellington Beds and BNZ who are all about celebrating the achievements of women in business and fostering a supportive community.

events.id: Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh-Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh
Join Sri Ganesh for an inspiring Ayurveda talk on how food & senses shape health. Learn 6 tastes, conscious eating, balancing your dominant

About this Event



Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh

What you feed your body and mind today shapes your health tomorrow.
Join us for an inspiring and eye-opening lecture with renowned Ayurvedic teacher and bestselling author Sri Ganesh, as he guides us through the powerful connection between physical and mental food—and how they shape our well-being.
In this talk, you’ll discover:
The two types of food we consume: what we eat with our mouths, and what we absorb through our senses.
How to make choices that support both body and mind—using Ayurveda’s six tastes as your guide.
Practical tools to shop wisely, cook from the heart, and eat consciously.
How to align your food and lifestyle with your presently dominant element (PDE) to prevent or heal imbalances at any age.
Whether you’re curious about Ayurveda, seeking to improve your health, or looking to deepen your relationship with food—this lecture will leave you empowered, informed, and inspired.
Sri Ganesh is the author of nine books, including the cookbooks 'Food is my medicine' and 'Food from my village’.




Event followed by a plant-based dinner and dessert, all included in your ticket!



Meet Your Host: Sri Ganesh

Sri Ganesh, the founder of Vaidya’s Ayurveda Village in Kerala, India, is an internationally recognised Ayurveda practitioner, inspirational speaker, and the author of eight widely read health books published in nine languages across the United States, Europe, and India.
Born into a traditional family in South India, Sri Ganesh began his education in Ayurveda in the early years of his life with his grandmother, a well-respected Ayurveda practitioner and poison therapist in their village. He later expanded his knowledge in the subjects of Mana-shastra (psychology), Vedanta (philosophy), Dhyana (meditation), Kalari Marma Chikitsa (Kerala martial art therapy), and Yoga Chikitsa (yoga therapy) through traditional schools in South Indian villages known as gurukulams. Sri Ganesh also holds a bachelor’s degree from Mahatma Gandhi University and a master’s degree in Human Resource Development (HRD).
Drawing on his traditional knowledge and over two decades of experience working as an Ayurveda practitioner around the world, Sri Ganesh has travelled extensively throughout Europe and the United States as an ambassador of Ayurveda, sharing his ancestors’ wisdom through talks, columns, and books. Over the years, Ganesh has delivered hundreds of inspirational talks and has written eight books in the field of health, which have been translated and published in Germany, Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the Netherlands, the United States, India, and Brazil.



Agenda


🕑: 06:00 PM - 06:15 PM
Arrive to Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:15 PM - 07:15 PM
Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh
Host: Sri Ganesh

🕑: 07:15 PM - 08:15 PM
Plant-based dinner and dessert!

events.id: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey-175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
events.venue: 175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey

“Āio” speaks to peace and inner calm. In this immersive evening, pūrākau, taonga pūoro and waiata are woven together with the healing vibrations of crystal singing bowls to create a space of deep listening and restoration.

The organic tones of traditional Māori instruments — including kōauau, pūtōrino, porotiti and pūtātara — blend with the resonance of crystal bowls, chimes and ukulele, inviting you to slow down, soften, and reconnect with your wairua and your body. Through story, sound and vibration, this is a gentle journey inward.

After the sound journey, share in a nourishing plant-based dinner and dessert, included in your ticket — a warm way to close the evening together.


What to bring:

Yoga mats, blankets and cushions will be provided but you are welcome to bring extra for comfort!



Meet your hosts & musicians: Smita, Peter, and Alexandra

Smita (Ngāi Te Rangi) was born and raised in Aotearoa. From a young age she was drawn to music as a means for reflection and self-expression. During her music studies at university, she was introduced to taonga pūoro, and this sparked a personal journey of reconnection to te ao Māori and her own whakapapa. Following university, she lived in an ashram for several years where she was introduced to the wonderful practice of bhakti yoga. She now lives with her husband in the Wellington region, where they regularly participate in kirtan (mantra music meditation) at Bhakti Lounge.

Peter is Pākehā, with grandparents from England and Ireland, and has been on a four-year journey learning the art of taonga pūoro — the traditional Māori musical instruments of Aotearoa. Guided by the wisdom and generosity of expert makers and players, he has learned both to craft and to play these unique instruments. For Peter, taonga pūoro offer a way to connect deeply with sound, story, and the natural world, and he is excited to share their voices as part of this sound bath experience.

Alexandra is a sound healing practitioner devoted to nurturing healing in both mind and body. She believes that sound vibration holds the power to expand consciousness and restore harmony and balance in our lives. Her journey into sound healing began with the realization of how deeply sound vibration was affecting her psyche—making her more mindful of the music she listens to and the intention it carries. Along the way, she has drawn inspiration from artists such as Deva Premal, The Flying Mystics, Stevin McNamara, and the Bhakti Lounge Kirtan. Their music has profoundly influenced her own sound healing practice, inspiring her to explore sound as a tool for transformation, connection, and inner peace.

https://bit.ly/eventTickets

events.id: Whānau Print Making-29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Whānau Print Making
events.venue: 29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us at Zealandia these school holidays for a free, nature inspired craft event!
Local artist Tayla Hartemink has designed a series of unique stamps featuring beautiful native manu and ika, perfect for decorating cards, and printing fabric and clothing.
A limited amount of materials will be provided, but we encourage you to bring your own T-shirts, tote bags, tea towels, or other fabric items to personalise with these stunning designs.
Tayla Hartemink (Tūhoe) is a multi-media artist, illustrator and creative workshop facilitator based in Te-Whanganui-ā-Tara. Tayla’s work is rooted in a deep connection to whakapapa and indigenous storytelling. Her work incorporates graphic design, painting, print-making and traditional motifs. Through her artwork she hopes to inspire and empower people to connect with Te Ao Māori.
Suitable for all ages— drop in, get hands-on, and make something colourful to take home!
This event will be held on the 9th and 10th of April, 10am-3pm in the Zealandia Visitor Centre Foyer. Normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street.

events.id: Theatresports™: Exhibition Match-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Theatresports™: Exhibition Match
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
Since time immemorial (aka the late 80s), kiwis up and down the country have loved the thrills, the spills, and the spontaneous chills of Theatresports™, the competitive, fast-paced improv classic. And for many teens, Theatresports™ has been their first taste of comedy, improv, and the stage, with many alumni of youth programmes going on to become household names.

And now... Theatresports™ is back in the capital! This Thursday, 9 April, we are thrilled to bring you a one-off exhibition match to whet your appetite for the improv to come.

In Theatresports™, two teams fearlessly take the stage and with no scripts, they take turns performing scenes, playing games, telling stories and creating characters entirely spontaneously. For this exhibition match two scratch teams will face off: game by game, story by story, scored by our judges and inspired by you, our audience. Who will be victorious? Who will be ridiculous? Who will receive the first 5-point score from the judges?

We are also thrilled to announce the return of the Pōneke Theatresports™ Youth Programme for high school students taking place in term 2 and culminating in a season of performances in early July at BATS Theatre. The exhibition match is followed by a Q&A session for anyone interested in our youth programme or in improvisation in general who wants to find out more.

events.name: The Music Of Django Reinhardt
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Les Tripes (French for 'The Guts') Present:
The Music of Django Reinhardt

Dynamic woodwind virtuoso Oscar Lavën joins forces with swing guitarist specialists Adrian Jensen & James Quick, and double bass raconteur Daniel Yeabsley to present a homage to the unique music of guitarist Django Reinhardt and violinist Stephane Grapelli. Together they will create hard-working interpretations of the infectious, foot-stomping, film noir music known in 1940s Paris as Hot Club.

Thursday April 9th.

9pm Downbeat. Free Entry, Koha Encouraged.

Poster art by the marvelous Justine.

events.name: Ultra New Zealand 2026
events.venue: 97 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
ULTRA NEW ZEALAND - FULL LINE UP ANNOUNCED
A-Z
ALISON WONDERLAND
THE CHAINSMOKERS
DJ SNAKE
DARREN STYLES
MARLO
MISS MONIQUE
NICO MORENO
OLIVER HELDENS
ZEDD
+ MANY MORE
ULTRA Worldwide has just announced the inaugural ULTRA New Zealand, which will take place at Wellington Waterfront in Wellington on Friday, April 10, 2026. In celebration of this historic debut edition, ULTRA New Zealand will transform the country’s capital into a multi-stage experience featuring world-class electronic music talent. ULTRA New Zealand will be held at an idyllic waterfront location, promising to channel the signature spirit of ULTRA’s flagship Miami festival and deliver a landmark moment for both ULTRA Worldwide and New Zealand’s live music landscape.

ULTRA Worldwide’s arrival on the Wellington Waterfront brings a sense of grand spectacle to the capital - an event launch that will create an unprecedented atmosphere for New Zealand. Set against the harbor’s sweeping backdrop, ULTRA New Zealand will be an electronic music playground that elevates Wellington City onto the global stage of dance music culture.

ULTRA New Zealand is part of the ULTRA Worldwide family, which continues to cement its status as the world’s most international music festival brand. Boasting active events on all six inhabited continents, including the flagship Ultra Music Festival in Miami, the global festival brand is heralded for delivering an unparalleled combination of top-tier electronic talent, cutting-edge technology and large-scale productions.

https://ultranewzealand.com/

events.id: ULTRA New Zealand-Wellington Waterfront-Wellington NZ
events.name: ULTRA New Zealand
events.venue: Wellington Waterfront
events.long_description:
It’s entertained fans across six continents. Now, for the first time, ULTRA Music Festival is coming to New Zealand. Arguably one of the most recognisable brands in electronic dance music, ULTRA is coming to Wellington for an exclusive stop-off. Known for delivering an epic combination of electronic music talent and impressive stage productions, the festival will take place along the capital’s picturesque waterfront on Friday, 10 April 2026. 
The one-day festival will span three world-class stages, with a fourth dedicated to local drum and bass artists. Along with Wellington’s calm autumn weather, ULTRA is expected to attract up to 20,000 people from across the country, as well as Australia and beyond. 
There are some major international artists confirmed including Alison Wonderland, NGHTMRE, Pendulum and Ray Volpe. You can also expect to see The Chainsmokers, DJ Snake, Zedd, Darren Styles, Nico Moreno, Oliver Heldens, Marlo, & Miss Monique.

events.name: Ringlets EU/UK Fundraiser at San Fran, Wellington
events.venue: 171 Cuba Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ringlets are soon heading off. March & April allows them just enough time to pack up an international driving permit, some muesli bars, and a set of guitar strings (can also be used as floss if you don’t mind a little blood) into a polka-dot bindle. May will then see them embark on a five week long tour of Europe & the United Kingdom. They also have a number of bookings later into the year, so they think they’ll stay on.
You can help them out: these two final headline shows – a shameless shake of the tip jar – in Wellington on April 10th, and Auckland on April 11th will be a chance for you to send them off in style. In the bands hometown of Auckland there will also be a garage sale (anything that didn’t fit into the bindle will be flogged off) alongside some actually high-quality merch and small trinkets exclusive to these fundraiser shows (Merch available in both cities).
Presented by Junk Mail & 100% Good
Ringlets live at San Fran
Friday 10th April - R18
With special guests Dropper, Cruelly & Girls Factory
Tickets from ringlets.co.nz

events.id: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition-11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition
events.venue: 11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for another fun night of social dancing with DJ Rafa, playing the grooviest Mambo, Salsa, and Bachata tracks! Emphasis on the MAMBO
Tonight we celebrate the Palladium era of Latin dance! Known as “the home of the Mambo,” the Palladium Ballroom was America’s most important venue for the development of Latin music and dance throughout the 1950s.
Mambo music and dance were co-created at the Palladium where Puerto Rican, Cuban, Dominican, Italian, Jewish, Irish, and African American patrons danced nightly to music from the “Big Three” Latin orchestras: Tito Puente, Tito Rodríguez, and Machito.
You can expect lots of Mambo and all-around good vibes!
$12 entry
DJ Rafa
Something classy or flashy (totally optional)
BYO drinks (but please keep them off the dance floor)
Salsa Magic's Friday Fiestas are held on the second Friday of every month and are a staple in the Wellington Latin dance community. All levels of experience and styles are welcome!

Wellington… are you ready to finish your night with BIG energy vibes?

Signature Socials is back with another absolute stunner — and this one is built for the true after-hours crew.

David Chouman (AUS) | Ashwynz| Halo

From pounding techno grooves to euphoric trance lifts and full-power psy drops — this is the after party that keeps the pulse racing till the lights come on.

Underground vibes. Heavy bass. No holding back.

events.id: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - CROFTON-Crofton Downs Primary School, Chartwell Drive, Crofton Downs, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - CROFTON
events.venue: Crofton Downs Primary School, Chartwell Drive, Crofton Downs, Wellington
events.long_description:
Introducing our new updated “Ancient Runes Puzzle Box”. Keep your treasures safe in the coolest way possible. Only you will know the special code to release the catch hidden under the mechanical Iris! This new improved version has 2 code wheels that you can use to set your own unique code, as well as 2 code pins that are stored on the box but have to be inserted correctly to activate the mechanism. If everything is set a hidden push button will then activate a solenoid that releases the catch.

Powered by 4 AAA batteries (provided), you’ll get to solder the components, screw and glue all the intricate pieces together and unwrap the puzzle to learn how it works. And, of course, you'll get to take it home! No previous experience is required and you’re welcome to bring a grown up if you want to.

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $135 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.id: Printing With Chine Colle - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Teens-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Printing With Chine Colle - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Teens
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Welcome to mixed media collage, the printmaker's way. Chine Colle is a printing process in which a thin sheet of paper is printed on and, at the same time, mounted on a sturdier sheet, creating a subtle, delicate backdrop to the printed image. Expanded on, this can include contrasting papers, graphic images, select colour shapes and fabric. The mission is just to have fun creating together in the fabulous Inverlochy print studio.

In this one-day workshop for ages 12+, students will:

- Practice traditional and individual Chinese collage techniques
- Explore the use of delicate papers more responsive to ink and hand printing
- Test and practice mixed media chine colle with images, papers and fabric
- How effective Chinese Collage is as a method of adding colour to prints
- Making and using a damp pack
- Recipes for traditional glues

Bring a water bottle and a packed lunch to enjoy in the historic Inverlochy house during break time.

events.id: Whānau Print Making-Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne Ecosanctuary,29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Whānau Print Making
events.venue: Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne Ecosanctuary,29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Whānau Print Making
Join us at Zealandia these school holidays for a free, nature inspired craft event!

Local artist Tayla Hartemink has designed a series of unique stamps featuring beautiful native manu and ika, perfect for decorating cards, and printing fabric and clothing.

A limited amount of materials will be provided, but we encourage you to bring your own T-shirts, tote bags, tea towels, or other fabric items to personalise with these stunning designs.

Tayla Hartemink (Tūhoe) is a multi-media artist, illustrator and creative workshop facilitator based in Te-Whanganui-ā-Tara. Tayla’s work is rooted in a deep connection to whakapapa and indigenous storytelling. Her work incorporates graphic design, painting, print-making and traditional motifs. Through her artwork she hopes to inspire and empower people to connect with Te Ao Māori.

Suitable for all ages— drop in, get hands-on, and make something colourful to take home!

This event will be held on the 9th and 10th of April, 10am-3pm in the Zealandia Visitor Centre Foyer. Normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street.

events.id: Goya Live at The Dowse with RadioActive-45 Laings Road, New Zealand 5010,45 Laings Rd, Hutt Central, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Goya Live at The Dowse with RadioActive
events.venue: 45 Laings Road, New Zealand 5010,45 Laings Rd, Hutt Central, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Goya Live at The Dowse with RadioActive
Goya Live at The Dowse with RadioActive.FM
Friday 10 April, 3-4pm
Free
RSVP appreciated via

Wellington noise-punks Goya take their name from 19th century Spanish painter, Francisco Goya, whose dark imaginative work spanned romanticism, madness and the horrors of war. Goya’s lyrics take aim at the inherent social-pathology of our consumerist lives in the final days of a dying a capitalist system.

Blood is Thicker pairs the heavy metal-inspired works of rocker artist Jason Greig with the darkly sublime works of old masters, including several pieces by Francisco Goya. Creating the effect of a séance across time, with themes of art, music, social criticisms, darkness and chaos, this exhibition provides the perfect backdrop for a live performance in the gallery by Goya.

Join us to hear from curators Chelsea Nichols and Aaron Lister, followed by the live performance by Goya, which will be broadcast live by RadioActive.fm.

events.long_description:
ULTRA NEW ZEALAND - PHASE ONE LINEUP
A-Z
THE CHAINSMOKERS
DJ SNAKE
DARREN STYLES
MARLO
MISS MONIQUE
NICO MORENO
OLIVER HELDENS
ZEDD
PHASE TWO LINEUP DUE EARLY 2026
ULTRA Worldwide has just announced the inaugural ULTRA New Zealand, which will take place at Wellington Waterfront in Wellington on Friday, April 10, 2026. In celebration of this historic debut edition, ULTRA New Zealand will transform the country’s capital into a multi-stage experience featuring world-class electronic music talent. ULTRA New Zealand will be held at an idyllic waterfront location, promising to channel the signature spirit of ULTRA’s flagship Miami festival and deliver a landmark moment for both ULTRA Worldwide and New Zealand’s live music landscape.
ULTRA Worldwide’s arrival on the Wellington Waterfront brings a sense of grand spectacle to the capital - an event launch that will create an unprecedented atmosphere for New Zealand. Set against the harbor’s sweeping backdrop, ULTRA New Zealand will be an electronic music playground that elevates Wellington City onto the global stage of dance music culture.
ULTRA New Zealand is part of the ULTRA Worldwide family, which continues to cement its status as the world’s most international music festival brand. Boasting active events on all six inhabited continents, including the flagship Ultra Music Festival in Miami, the global festival brand is heralded for delivering an unparalleled combination of top-tier electronic talent, cutting-edge technology and large-scale productions.
FINAL TICKETS AVAILABLE AT -> https://ultranewzealand.com/
Stay tuned for additional lin

events.id: The Gumboot Day Express-Paekakariki-Facebook
events.name: The Gumboot Day Express
events.venue: Paekakariki
events.long_description:
�� We’re excited to announce a special collaboration between Steam Incorporated and Feilding and District Steam Rail Society for 2026 — introducing The Gumboot Day Express!
Join us as we steam our way to Taihape, the Gumboot Capital of the World, for one of the most iconic and entertaining days on the Kiwi calendar. Saturday 11th April Only!
Tickets available at www.steaminc.org.nz/book-train-trip/tgde/
Hauled by Ab 608 “Passchendaele”, this heritage journey takes in the spectacular scenery of the North Island Main Trunk — including the stunning Rangitīkei viaducts — before arriving right in the heart of the Gumboot Day festivities.
Expect a fantastic day of small-town charm, live entertainment, markets, and of course, the famous gumboot throwing competition — all wrapped around a classic steam-hauled rail experience.
This is a joint venture between two passionate heritage rail organisations, and we’re proud to be working together to bring something special to the region.
Seats are now available, and we’d love to have you onboard!
Find out more and book via Steam Incorporated's website!
www.steaminc.org.nz/book-train-trip/tgde/

We’re excited to share that Pistang Pilipino, our biggest annual Filipino cultural celebration in Aotearoa is returning this year! Even more special, Wellington will be hosting Pistang Pilipino for the first time in 10 years through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, bringing together Filipino families, players and communities from all over New Zealand.
And this year, we’re adding something NEW and HISTORIC…
The FIRST‑EVER Filipino Pickleball Tournament in Pistang Pilipino history! A fun, competitive, and community‑driven event.
Calling all Filipino Pickleball Players!
This 11th April of 2026, we’re starting things off with the First Filipino Pickleball Fundraising Tournament! All proceeds will go towards supporting Pistang Pilipino 2026 through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, helping us make this celebration bigger and better for everyone.

events.id: Family Shiny - Akatarawa Forest-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Family Shiny - Akatarawa Forest
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Registrations open 20th March 2026
What to expect
This is a “Family Shiny” trip into the Akatarawa Forest.
Non members are welcome to join us but must register to obtain details of start time and location.
Vehicles must have a low ratio gearbox and must not be a 4wd car, station wagon or van, and if a soft top must have a roll cage.
Visitor Registration- $20.00 per driver
There is no trip fee for this trip however non-members pay a “Day Membership Fee” of $20. This is payable in Cash to the Trip Leader on the day.
The trip should be relatively easy as it needs to cater for people just starting out and all types of 4wd vehicles – but a good way to see if you want to get into 4wding and a chance to meet some of our club members (if you are unsure if your vehicle is suitable just ask).
No Dogs Allowed sorry
Once you have completed 3 Family Shinys you can join the club but can only participate in Family Shiny or Touring Class trips. Once you have also done a Training Day and had your vehicle inspected by one of our approved inspectors you can come along on our other more adventurous trips. To find out more on how to join our club go to https://ccvcwellington.wildapricot.org/page-1725861.
Like our Facebook page to find out about more trips and club nights.

events.id: Lions Premier League 2026-Liardet Street Park, Berhampore, Wellington 6023, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Lions Premier League 2026
events.venue: Liardet Street Park, Berhampore, Wellington 6023, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Lions Premier League 2026 is set to become the flagship event of the SLSC calendar — a high-energy, franchise-style T20 tournament inspired by the biggest leagues, but grounded in club pride, community, and competition.
Six franchises. Six owners. One champion.
This tournament brings together the best of our club — past, present, and emerging talent — in a format designed to entertain, challenge, and elevate Lions cricket to the next level.
---
The Format
• 6 franchise teams
• T20 format
• Each team must field a minimum of 8 SLSC players, with up to 3 contracted non-club players
• Played in a festival-style atmosphere with crowds, food, energy, and atmosphere
---
The Franchises
• Ella Beauties
• Mirissa Mission
• Rajarata Rambos
• Yala Night Chasers
• Colombo Seven XI
• Sigiriya Fresh Bros
Each franchise will be led by an owner who builds their squad through the Lions Premier League Auction.
---
Team Ownership & Auction
Each team is owned by an individual or group for $500, which includes:
• $250 franchise ownership fee
• $250 in auction credits to recruit players
---
Lions Premier League Auction Night:
6 March 2026
A marquee evening where strategy, nerves, and big calls shape the destiny of each franchise.
---
Tournament Dates
April 3 - Round Robin
April 11 - Finals Day
April 12 - Reserve Day
---
Prizes & Awards
•
Champions: $1,500
•
Runners-Up: $1,000
•
Most Valuable Player: $200
•
Best Batter: $150
•
Best Bowler: $150
---
More Than Cricket
The Lions Premier League is about:
• Bringing the cricket community together
• Creating opportunities for players to shine
• Uncovering future Premier talent
• Delivering a professional, entertaining cricket & community event
Expect a fantastic couple of days with joyful crowds, great food, and an atmosphere to cherish.
---
Powered by SLSC
The Lions Premier League is proudly powered by SLSC and represents the next chapter in our club’s journey — bold, inclusive, competitive, and forward-looking.
More announcements coming soon — fixtures, owners, player signings, sponsors, and matchday experiences.
Mark your calendars.
Pick your franchise.
The Lions are ready to roar.

--- Tournament Dates April 3 - Round Robin April 11 - Finals Day April 12 - Reserve Day --- Prizes & Awards • Champions: $1,500 • Runners-Up: $1,000 • Most Valuable Player: $200 • Best Batter: $150 • Best Bowler: $150 --- More Than Cricket The Lions Premier League is about: • Bringing the cricket community together • Creating opportunities for players to shine • Uncovering future Premier talent • Delivering a professional, entertaining cricket & community event Expect a fantastic couple of days with joyful crowds, great food, and an atmosphere to cherish.

events.venue: Wellington Zoo - Manchester Street, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo is turning 120 years old this April, and we want to celebrate this milestone with all our Te Whanganui-a-Tara Wellington whanau!
Our official birthday celebration weekend will be held on 11-12 April, and you’re all invited!
Pre-book your tickets to our birthday weekend now and go in the draw to win an amazing prize pack, including:
- 1x family Zoo Crew membership for one year (2x adults and up to 3x kids)
- 1x Close Encounter for 4x people
- $50 Rex Tremendous voucher (can be redeemed at Banana Bar too)
- 2 x tickets to do Wellington Chocolate Factory's Original Chocolatier Experience
- A retail prize pack from Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo and Iko Iko
Zoo Crew members, since you already have a membership, you don't need tickets. Just make sure you scan your pass when you arrive at the Zoo to go in the draw!

events.id: Elsewhere in the Building - Improv with WIT-Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Elsewhere in the Building - Improv with WIT
events.venue: Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Elsewhere in the Building is a friendly improv format that happens in different places within an overarching location suggested by the audience, creating a world of multiple scenes.

This is the first of a series of monthly shows WIT will be holding at Thistle Hall, the next will be Friday 1 May (Micetro), then on 5 June and 3 July (shows tba).

The Wellington Improvisation Troupe, aka WIT, is a member-based community theatre group and registered charity, teaching and performing in Wellington since 2003. Our theatre is made up on the spot, usually inspired by suggestions from the audience and each other. WIT players share a love of storytelling and work together as a team to inspire the audience, we’re sometimes serious, often hilarious and always totally unpredictable.

Pay-what-you-can tickets from $5, and door sales. Entrance to the Upstairs Hall is in Arthur St, or through the exhibition space entrance in Cuba Street.

Our event will feature open Scott Court retailers and local stalls offering a variety of hot food, including chop suey, as well as baked goods such as Fudge, Liquorice, Nougat, cupcakes, cakes, cookies, choc filled fried bread, and brownies.

Come join us and enjoy our local community.

events.id: Find Your Tribe-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Find Your Tribe
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
It’s a lonely world. Internet, parties, couples, work. But how do we find… the one? Y’know, that BFF that can make the world a warmer place to live in?

Following the advice of lonely hearts experts everywhere, join Best On Tap as we twist those old rom-com tropes and find our Tribe! We’ll take your special interest and turn it into a reason, a season or a lifetime of friendship.

Expect love, laughter, misunderstanding, heartbreak, soul searching, and, if we’re all lucky, a life-long (platonic) soul mate.

“...Best on Tap lives up to its mission to root its improv in human experiences that truly reflect the lives we live and observe. From tahi to tekau, they have developed skills that ensure their spontaneous shows will always be engaging.”
Theatreview

events.id: NZ & OZ Rock Experience - Live at Abandoned Taproom Petone-Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: NZ & OZ Rock Experience - Live at Abandoned Taproom Petone
events.venue: Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
NZ & OZ Rock Experience - Live at Abandoned Taproom Petone
The NZ/Oz Rock Experience is the sound of Downunder!

Aussie. Kiwi. Absolute classics.

The NZ/Oz Experience is a high-energy celebration of the songs that shaped Australia and New Zealand’s rock history — performed live by charting NZ and Australian musicians who know how to bring a crowd with them.

From the hooks of The Mockers and The Exponents, to the pub-rock punch of IThe Angels and Australian Crawl, through to favourites from Dragon, INXS and Th' Dudes — this is the soundtrack of stadiums, summer festivals and late-night dancefloors on both sides of the ditch.

It’s loud in the right places, melodic where it matters, and built for singing along. Whether you were there the first time around or just love great live rock, this is a night of nostalgia, energy and songs everyone knows.

If you know the words… you’re coming.

events.id: Our Ancestors panel talk: With Tusiata Avia, Gareth Watkins and Flora Feltham-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Our Ancestors panel talk: With Tusiata Avia, Gareth Watkins and Flora Feltham
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We're very excited to be hosting Tusiata Avia, Gareth Watkins and Flora Feltham for a panel discussion on the theme of 'Our Ancestors' on Saturday 11 April, 11am - 12pm. This panel is part of our Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui series of events happening over the weekend of 11 and 12 of April to celebrate the recent reopening of Te Matapihi.
This event will be held in the beautiful Ngā Pou Ruahine space on the second floor. This is a free event and all are welcome. We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited, so please arrive early to ensure a seat.
Meet our esteemed panellists...
Tusiata Avia:
Tusiata Avia MNZM is a celebrated New Zealand poet and children's author. Among her many awards, her 2021 collection 'The Savage Coloniser' won the Mary and Peter Biggs Award for Poetry at the Ockham New Zealand Book Awards. She was honoured in the 2020 Queen’s Birthday list.
Gareth Watkins:
Gareth Watkins is a hugely respected LGBTQIA+ historian and a prominent voice and recorder of LGBTQIA+ history, especially that of the Wellington area.
events.description:
We're very excited to be hosting Tusiata Avia, Gareth Watkins and Flora Feltham for a panel discussion on the theme of 'Our Ancestors' on Saturday 11 April, 11am - 12pm.
This panel is part of our Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui series of events happening over the weekend of 11 and 12 of April to celebrate the recent reopening of Te Matapihi.
This is a free event and all are welcome.
Among her many awards, her 2021 collection 'The Savage Coloniser' won the Mary and Peter Biggs Award for Poetry at the Ockham New Zealand Book Awards.

events.id: THE BLUE SUMMER EP RELEASE W/ SERENNA-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: THE BLUE SUMMER EP RELEASE W/ SERENNA
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
After slaving away at a leisurely pace for a year in Lyall Bay, dynamic Wellington rock five-piece The Blue Summer finally has something to show for their toil. Engineered by scene veteran Peter Jamieson and mixed by their own Alan Beere, The Blue Summer delivers five searing rock tracks with wisps of the gothic, the chill of post-punk and the lingering hint of funk. Witness them perform the EP along with older and newer material at their spiritual home of Valhalla on Vivian Street on Friday the tenth of April.
Joining them is Serenna - a gothic rock band recently resurrected after a fifteen-year hiatus, and now complete with some luminaries of the darker local scene.
Doors open at 8pm. Punctuality is encourated. Tickets on UTR for $10 + BF.

events.id: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition-Dancetopia - Dance Studio,11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition
events.venue: Dancetopia - Dance Studio,11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition
Join us for another fun night of social dancing with DJ Rafa, playing the grooviest Mambo, Salsa, and Bachata tracks! Emphasis on the MAMBO 💃

Tonight we celebrate the Palladium era of Latin dance! Known as “the home of the Mambo,” the Palladium Ballroom was America’s most important venue for the development of Latin music and dance throughout the 1950s.

Mambo music and dance were co-created at the Palladium where Puerto Rican, Cuban, Dominican, Italian, Jewish, Irish, and African American patrons danced nightly to music from the “Big Three” Latin orchestras: Tito Puente, Tito Rodríguez, and Machito.

You can expect lots of Mambo and all-around good vibes!

💰 $12 entry
🎧 DJ Rafa
👗 Something classy or flashy (totally optional)
🍹 BYO drinks (but please keep them off the dance floor)

Salsa Magic's Friday Fiestas are held on the second Friday of every month and are a staple in the Wellington Latin dance community. All levels of experience and styles are welcome! 🙌

events.venue: Valhalla,154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Slam! The Ultimate After Party feat. David Chouman ( Aus), Ashwynz & Halo
Signature Socials Proudly Present's


Wellington… are you ready to finish your night with BIG energy vibes? 🔥


Signature Socials is back with another absolute stunner — and this one is built for the true after-hours crew.


🔊 Featuring


David Chouman (AUS) | Ashwynz| Halo


From pounding techno grooves to euphoric trance lifts and full-power psy drops — this is the after party that keeps the pulse racing till the lights come on.


📍 Valhalla – Wellington
📅 FRIDAY, 10 April
🎶 Underground vibes. Heavy bass. No holding back.


If you know, you know.
If you don’t… it’s time to find out.


Get ready to SLAM. 💥


About our headliner, David Chouman:


Hailing from Melbourne, David Chouman is a rising force in the modern dance scene, known for explosive productions and performances. With a release catalog spanning Reaching Altitude, Nocturnal Knights, and CLUBWRK, his sound has been championed by global icons like MaRLo, Giuseppe Ottaviani, and Aly & Fila.


Fresh off a massive performance at Dreamstate Melbourne and the successful launch of his own 'Chew On This Live' brand, David is now taking his high-octane sound across to New Zealand. Known for creating moments that make the dancefloor feel truly alive, David Chouman arrives in Wellington to prove why he is one of the most exciting names in the new generation of Trance and Techno.




R18 | ID Complulsory

events.id: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
UPGRADED TO MEOW NUI DUE TO OVERWHELMING DEMAND
Handsome Tours are proud to present The Mountain Goats!
Veteran indie-rock storytellers The Mountain Goats will make their long-awaited return to Australia and New Zealand in April 2026, with performances in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth, Auckland and Wellington. This eagerly-anticipated tour represents the band's first visit to Australia in 9 years (2017) and their first trip to New Zealand in 16 years (2010).
Fronted by prolific songwriter and novelist John Darnielle, The Mountain Goats are revered the world over for their sprawling catalogue of twenty-three studio albums released between 1994 and 2023. This tour announcement coincides with the band today unveiling album number twenty-four, 'Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan' - to be released 7th November 2025 on Cadmean Dawn Records (the band’s own label).
Produced by the Mountain Goats’ multi-instrumentalist Matt Douglas, Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan is a full-on musical that stands as the most conceptually detailed and musically elaborate project in the band’s ever-expanding catalogue. It features appearances from The Replacements’ Tommy Stinson, harpist Mikaela Davis, musical theatre royalty Lin-Manuel Miranda (Tony Award-winning ‘Hamilton’, Disney’s ‘Moana’ & ‘Encanto’), and the band’s new bassist Cameron Ralston.
Don’t miss one of indie rock’s most enduring and beloved bands as they make their long-overdue return to our shores!

events.id: Ride the Cyclone-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Ride the Cyclone
events.venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Darkly funny, wildly imaginative, and surprisingly heartfelt, Ride the Cyclone is the viral musical phenomenon that has taken the world by storm.
In this hilarious and exhilarating story, the lives of six teenagers of the St. Cassian Chamber Choir are changed forever after they board a doomed rollercoaster ride.
Thrown into the abyss, they awake to find an ominous mechanical fortune teller that offers them the chance to compete for the prize … to return home in one piece.
Now confronted with their darkest desires, shameful secrets and each other, this “supremely witty … and just plain delightful” (New York Times) musical makes a uniquely uplifting and outrageous case for what makes a life worth living.

events.id: Helmet 2026 | Wellington-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Helmet 2026 | Wellington
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Helmet has built a reputation as one of the most commanding forces in the underground scene. Born at the dawn of the rage-filled 90’s, Helmet fiercely holds space for multiple generations of alternative fans seeking the vital energy of hostile riffs and inventive metronomic groove. With a refusal to fit neatly into a genre, Helmet’s music creates an atmosphere best known live; part industrial, part hip-hop, part existential sermon.
"Helmet stripped away the excess, dialled in the precision, and redefined what heaviness could sound like without succumbing to clichés." Pitchfork
Formed in 1989 in NYC by Page Hamilton, Helmet has been crushing stages for over three decades. In that time they have only come to Aotearoa / NZ a handful of times, most recently in 2023 during their sold out Best of Helmet 1989-2023 tour, with 13th Floor declaring “a complete evening of essential music from one of heavy music’s most essential bands.” With 35 years of rocking behind them, Helmet are at the top of their game.
Don’t miss Helmet in New Zealand next April supported by New Way Home.
Tickets on sale Thursday 2 October, 12pm

events.id: URGE BLACK-154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: URGE BLACK
events.venue: 154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Unleash your inner dark side at URGE BLACK. The pulsating rhythms of liberation meet the sultry allure of leather-clad revelry. This isn’t just a dance party; it’s a sensorial odyssey that transcends age and convention. Prepare to be captivated, challenged, and ultimately liberated in an atmosphere where every shimmy and shake is an act of personal revolution.
URGE doesn’t just throw parties; we craft transformative experiences that celebrate the raw, unbridled essence of gay culture. Leather, fetish, uniform, gear, sports kit, etc.
Let’s dance and celebrate our authentic selves!
URGE tips:
Doors open at 9:00 PM.
Door sales are limited and $60 (cash only) if the event has not sold out prior.
Peak times: Queues can form if everyone arrives at once. We work to get you in quickly, so please be patient and courteous to our staff.
Dark Room: Located out back. Explore it if you’re feeling adventurous!
Your DJs:
Winner Mick
DJ K-OTIK
Everett Wayne
Event Rules:
This is a safe space: have fun, be kind, spread love, and look out for each other.
Follow instructions: Listen to staff and volunteers—they’re there to keep everyone safe. Ignoring their directions may result in removal from the event.
ZERO tolerance for harassment: This includes racism, transphobia, homophobia, sexism, misgendering, xenophobia, ableism, and fatphobia.
CONSENT IS KEY: Consent must be explicit and enthusiastic and can be withdrawn at any time.
- - - - SEE YOU ON THE DANCE FLOOR - - - -

events.name: 1st Filipino Pickleball Fundraising Tournament
events.venue: Ākau Tangi Sports Centre, 72 Kemp Street,72 Kemp St, Kilbirnie, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
1st Filipino Pickleball Fundraising Tournament
Kia ora, mga kababayan!


We’re excited to share that Pistang Pilipino, our biggest annual Filipino cultural celebration in Aotearoa is returning this year! Even more special, Wellington will be hosting Pistang Pilipino for the first time in 10 years through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, bringing together Filipino families, players and communities from all over New Zealand.

And this year, we’re adding something NEW and HISTORIC…
The FIRST‑EVER Filipino Pickleball Tournament in Pistang Pilipino history! A fun, competitive, and community‑driven event.

Calling all Filipino Pickleball Players!

This 11th April of 2026, we’re starting things off with the First Filipino Pickleball Fundraising Tournament! All proceeds will go towards supporting Pistang Pilipino 2026 through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, helping us make this celebration bigger and better for everyone.

Tournament Categories:

Singles
• Men’s Intermediate
• Men’s Advance
• Women’s Intermediate
• Women’s Advance

Doubles
• Men’s Intermediate
• Men’s Advance
• Women’s Intermediate
• Women’s Advance

Mixed Doubles
• Intermediate
• Advance

Entry Fees

$40 – Base fee (includes your first category)
+$20 – For each additional category you join
Play more, enjoy more, support more!

Scan the QR code on the poster or click the registration link to secure your spot. Limited slots per category!


Let’s make history together, support Pistang Pilipino, and celebrate our Filipino pride on and off the court.

Salamat po!

events.id: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa: POSTPONED-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa: POSTPONED
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
About 1 in 5 New Zealand adults experience a common mental illness each year. Understanding how to talk about mental health and help someone in need are important skills. This certified Mental Health First Aid course, will teach you the skills and knowledge to recognise and respond to someone experiencing mental health challenges. Course fees: If you are a NZ citizen/Permanent Resident Visa the course fee is $199 (evidence of eligibility required). Others pay $499.

Description
Mental Health First Aid is an evidence-based workshop which gives people the skills and confidence to have supportive conversations with their whānau, co-workers and community and help guide them to professional help if needed.

You will learn to recognise common mental health challenges, how to provide initial help to someone experiencing a mental health challenge using a practical, evidence-based action plan, and how to respond in a crisis situation. During the workshop you will have the opportunity to practice new skills in a safe environment.

You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the reduced course fee. A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Mental health challenges covered in the course include:

Depression
Anxiety
Psychosis
Substance use problems
Mental Health Crisis Covered:

Suicidal thoughts and behaviours
Non- Suicidal self-injury
Panic attack
Traumatic events
Severe effects from Alcohol or other substance use
Aggressive behaviours
People who attend Mental Health First Aid training can complete a short online assessment to be accredited as a Mental Health First Aider. You must attend the full two days to be able to do the online assessment for accreditation. The accreditation is valid for three years.

If you choose not to be accredited, you will receive a certificate of participation if you attend the full course. You will also receive a workbook as part of your course fee.

A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Prerequisites
You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the reduced course fee.

A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

events.id: Leaf Library-Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Leaf Library
events.venue: Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington
events.long_description:
Create art from leaves and lightboxes these April School Holidays with artist Dr Gabby O’Connor.

To accompany her exhibition Rau Whārangi at Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, local artist Dr Gabby O’Connor invites tamariki and whānau to take part in a hands-on, drop-in art and ecology experience during the April school holidays.

Tamariki can create leaves from recycled items and use lightboxes to explore the art of shadows.

Digital images of the artworks created during the session may be included in the Rau Whārangi exhibition video, which plays alongside Gabby O’Connor’s installation at Te Matapihi.

Suitable for all ages. Free, donations appreciated.

Held at Nōku te Ao Capital E, Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central.

events.id: Te Papa Whānau Day-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Te Papa Whānau Day
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This whānau day invites you to explore our deep connections within te taiao (the natural world).

ORO ATUA Wānanga Rongoā Puoro | 11am-12.30pm
A wānanga for all whānau, communities, and ages. Experience Jerome and Ruiha’s whānau practice of Taonga Pūoro Māori (Māori musical instruments) as Rongoā (healing), by coming together to help navigate the challenges of modern life.

Mauri Ora Art Workshop | 10am-2pm (drop-in)
Make your own artwork inspired by nature and pūrākau (Māori stories) with artist Ryan Cassidy. Learn te reo Māori and New Zealand Sign Language as you explore the patterns of the natural world.

Scale of the Universe Interactive | 10am-2pm (drop-in)
Navigate the visible and invisible through the interactive visual tool Scale of the Universe. Explore the scale of different organisms and objects, from the smallest particles to the largest galaxies.

Learn About Lichens | 10am-2pm (drop-in)
Are they fungi, algae, or bacteria? Join Fungi of Aotearoa author Liv Sisson to learn all about the lichens that live among us!

Free face painting by Daizy Designs | 10am-2pm (drop-in)

Gelli plate printing with Lara Shepherd | 10am-2pm (drop-in)
Join Te Papa’s resident evolutionary biologist, Lara Shepherd, to make prints using native plants.

Putting the 'Art' in 'Earth' tours | 11am and 1pm
These tours expand Te Papa's art floors, building dynamic connections between art-making and the environment.

Slow Looking | Times TBC
It’s International Slow Art Day! Take a closer look at a painting in these fun sessions. Local high school students will guide you – the longer you look, the more you’ll see!

events.id: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Kua raranga tahi tātou he whariki mō āpōpō
Together we weave the mat for future generations

Nau mai haere mai, all are welcome to attend Ōtari Raranga wānanga (classes).

These classes run approximately fortnightly.

No experience required and all materials provided. You will walk away with woven taonga at the end of every class.

During the first 2 classes, beginner students will complete a variety of putiputi (flowers).

We provide harakeke for you if it is your FIRST class and we will teach you how to harvest for yourself during this time and what to harvest for your next class.

After that, if you would like us to harvest for you, purchase the add-on - "Please harvest for me" for an additional fee.

Moving forward, you will start making kōnae (2 corner kete - basket), kono (4-sided kete), tapawhāroa and more.
You will need to harvest 8-10 rau for yourself and start building your own tool kit.

After completing 6 or more beginner classes (close together), you may be ready to move up to our intermediate classes. Please talk with our kaiako and kaiāwhina if you are interested.

With regular attendance, you will:
- Learn tikanga (protocols) and karakia for hauhake (harvesting) and preparing harakeke
- Build your mātauranga (knowledge)
- Use kupu Māori appropriate to raranga practice
- Develop your pūkenga (practical skills)
- Create taonga (treasures) for your whānau
- Build hononga (connections) within our raranga rōpū (group)
- Have the skills to practice at home.

What’s included in your beginner's ticket?
- Three-hour weaving class with experienced kaiako
- Harakeke is provided for your first class, and then you should hauhake (harvest) for yourself or click the "please harvest for me" option in addition to your ticket at least two days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share

Led by Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera) , Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri), and/or Anna Lees (Te Āti Awa, Kāi Tahu).
On occasion our raranga rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), will teach and we often have support from our kaiāwhina.


Important information
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia
- For tikanga purposes, beginner ticket sales close two days before wānanga, giving us time to hauhake (harvest) for you
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets
- We welcome children aged 10-14, but they should be accompanied by an adult with a ticket

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers
For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit our website: www.otariraranga.co.nz

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with the Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits, please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com or visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information

events.id: 🇳🇿🇩🇪Kiwi Kölsch Service!🇩🇪🇳🇿-Level 1, 66 Tory Street, New Zealand 6011,Lone Star, 66 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: 🇳🇿🇩🇪Kiwi Kölsch Service!🇩🇪🇳🇿
events.venue: Level 1, 66 Tory Street, New Zealand 6011,Lone Star, 66 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
🇳🇿🇩🇪Kiwi Kölsch Service!🇩🇪🇳🇿
Kölsch Service is BACK!! But this time with a kiwi twist to celebrate FRESH HOP SEASON!! Frischë Dösen Kölsch is made with fresh Wakatū hops, straight off the bine in Nelson, which we'll be serving the traditional way on Saturday 11th of April - just like in Köln.

What's Kölsch service?
It's kinda like beer yum cha but there's only one thing on the menu - 200ml glasses of Kölsch bier! 🍺
We keep track of your tab by marking the edge of your coaster and when your glass is empty - voilà, another glass appears! 🪄 If you're done, just pop your coaster on top of your glass to indicate no more.
You can order food and non-alcoholics the same way, just take a seat and everything will come to you. 🪑

🇳🇿Special KIWI Menu!🇳🇿
We'll be serving up distinctively kiwi snacks - like classic kiwi dip with Proper Crisps, Marmite & Cheese Garlic Flatbreads plus our usual offerings of Cheese Rolls and Cheerios and delicioius pizzas.

To make sure you get a table, book here: https://www.meandoses.co.nz/bookings


We'll be running Kölsch service from 12pm - 5pm on Saturday April 11th, come along and get the Köln experience! 🍻🥨🇩🇪

events.id: The Poetry Corner: The Poets of Aotearoa, hosted by Kate De Goldi-65 Victoria Street, New Zealand 6011,Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: The Poetry Corner: The Poets of Aotearoa, hosted by Kate De Goldi
events.venue: 65 Victoria Street, New Zealand 6011,Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Poetry Corner: The Poets of Aotearoa, hosted by Kate De Goldi
Join us for a huge celebration of poetry in Aotearoa, hosted by New Zealand Reading Ambassador, celebrated writer Kate De Goldi. Come along from 1-2pm to hear poets Jenny Bornholdt, Harry Ricketts, Janis Freegard, James Brown, Tim Jones, Ashleigh Young and Sudha Rao read their work.

This event is part of our Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Weekend, and will be held on the bleachers (near the Harris Street entrance). For full details of all events happening:

https://wcl.govt.nz/explore-te-matapihi

Full details of our speakers:

Former Poet Laureate Jenny Bornholdt

Poet, academic, biographer, editor, anthologist, critic, literary scholar and cricket writer Harry Ricketts

Multi-award-winning poet, novelist and short story writer Janis Freegard

Editor and poetry teacher at the International Institute of Modern Letters at Te Herenga Waka—Victoria University of Wellington James Brown

Sir Julius Vogel Award winner for Best Collected Work and the 2010 recipient of the NZSA Janet Frame Memorial Award for Literature, Tim Jones

Co-founder of both The Cuba Press and Mākaro Press, novelist, children’s author, poet , songwriter and NZ Book Award winner Mary McCallum

Winner of the Windam-Campbell Prize from Yale University and the Royal Society Te Apārangi Award for General Non-Fiction, acclaimed poet Ashleigh Young is also an editor at Te Herenga Waka University Press.

And Sudha Rao. Sudha trained in classical South Indian dance and established Dance Aotearoa New Zealand. She completed an MA in Creative Writing at the International Institute of Modern Letters and published her first collection of poems, On Elephant’s Shoulders in 2022.

All are welcome to attend this free celebration of poetry.

events.id: The Wizard of Oz-Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: The Wizard of Oz
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
The Wizard of Oz
The classic story by Frank L Baum is given the wacky Pantoloon pantomime treatment with hilarious results! This fun-packed story is guaranteed to have you cheering Glinda the Good Fairy, booing the evil Wicked Witch of the West and rolling in the aisles with laughter. Follow the brave foursome along the yellow brick road to find out if the Scarecrow gets a brain, the Tin man gets a heart, the Lion gets some courage – and if Dorothy finds her way back home! With fabulous costumes and hilarious comedy, this is a treat for all the family.

With well-known upbeat songs, traditional panto one-liners and gags this is definitely a show for the whole family to enjoy! Click your heels and follow the yellow brick road all the way to see this magical, musical pantomime!

events.id: Historic Postcard Walk: Before and After - Guided Walk-Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Historic Postcard Walk: Before and After - Guided Walk
events.venue: Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join the Friends of the Garden for an easy 60-minute walk in the Main Garden to reflect on the past hundred years of the Botanic Garden through the views of 20th century postcards. Donal Duthie, a head gardener in the Wellington Botanic Garden in the 60s and 70s, collected many valuable postcards of the Garden.

Meet at the Founders Entrance, Glenmore Street.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)

This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.

Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.id: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Waenganui (Intermediate)-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Waenganui (Intermediate)
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ma te mōhio ka roa, ma te ora ka mōhio
Through learning there is life, through life there is learning

Nau mai haere mai, welcome to Ōtari Raranga Wānanga - Waenganui

Our kaiako will have made it clear to you when you can advance to this level from beginners.

If you are new to Ōtari but have experience please reach out before committing.

Projects are planned in advance of each wānanga to help us move forward together as a rōpū and awhi one another while working on similar taonga.
Please check your emails beforehand so you know what to bring.

Waenganui Wānanga are an important step toward joining our Tuakana Rōpū, who will often be attending these wānanga.
While they work on their own taonga projects, they are a valuable source of support for learners—happy to kōrero, share advice, and offer inspiration along the way.

Projects may include new closes for your kete timata, kete waikawa, puritanga, pīkau and pōtae.
We will teach you preparation, preservation and storage of harakeke - important especially over winter months. Additionally, we will induct you to the Ōtari Pā Harakeke when ready.

With regular attendance, you will deepen the mātauranga gained from beginner classes and share your learnings with others. (Refer to below for a reminder)

What’s included in your intermediate ticket?
- Three+ hours weaving class with our tuakana weavers and raranga rangatira.
- Limited access to tools, as you are expected to have your own kete mahi (tool kit).
- You are expected to bring your own harakeke.
- We encourage you to harvest for yourself, but if you are unable to harvest for class, we have ‘Please harvest for me’ options in addition to your ticket at least three days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share


Led by our raranga Rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), with Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri) and / or Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera)


Important information:
- Waenganui Rōpū are expected to harvest for themselves, but if something unexpected pops up we have add-on services available during the ticketing process. A minimum of 3 days notice is required.
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia.
- At our discretion, tickets are fully refundable or transferable.
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class.
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets.

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit www.otariraranga.co.nz

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community.
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- All donations over $5 made to The Trust are tax-deductible
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com
- Visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information

events.name: FIESTA! Latin American Show & Flavours
events.venue: Newtown Community Centre - Te Whare Hapori o Ngā Puna Waiora,7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
FIESTA! Latin American Show & Flavours
✨ You’re invited to FIESTA!- Latin American Show & Flavours! ✨

Wellington — one night, one stage, one Latin experience.

FIESTA! is where the city comes together to feel the rhythm, taste the flavours, and live the energy of Latin America in a fully immersive night at the Newtown Community Centre.

Leading the night is Cumbiasonica, bringing explosive live cumbia and irresistible Latin grooves that turn the audience into part of the show — expect singing, dancing, and a packed dance floor from the first beat.

The experience continues with the bold and hilarious Teti Queen, plus an interactive salsa session by Salsa Therapy — designed for everyone, from first-timers to dance lovers, with prizes that keep the energy high.

Featuring special performances by Estudio de Samba Wellington and Club de la Comedia, adding rhythm, colour and personality to a night built to move and connect people.

Explore the flavours of Latin America with Argentine, Peruvian and Brazilian food, alongside traditional snacks and drinks by Latino Foods NZ.

This is not just a show — it’s a shared experience. A space where cultures connect, people meet, and Wellington’s Latin community comes alive.

Family-friendly and open to all — ¡Todos bienvenidos!


✨ Tickets:
https://events.humanitix.com/fiesta-latin-american-show-and-flavours


Find us!

https://www.facebook.com/LatinEventsWelly

https://www.instagram.com/latineventswelly

events.venue: Newtown Community Centre Theatre, Columbo Street and Rintoul Street, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
FIESTA! - Latin American Show & Flavours is a vibrant cultural event happening on Saturday 11 April 2026, from 6:00 to 9:00 PM, at the Newtown Community Centre in Wellington, bringing together live music, dance, comedy, and Latin American food to celebrate the richness, energy, and diversity of Latin culture in one unforgettable evening. Fiesta is a Family Friendly Event.

Get ready for a high-energy night with Cumbiasonica, whose infectious cumbia grooves and Latin rhythms will have everyone singing, dancing, and moving together all night long. Their live shows are known for big energy, catchy choruses, and an atmosphere that invites the whole audience to join in.

The evening also features the hilarious Teti Queen, delivering bold comedy and Latin charm, plus vibrant performances and a short salsa class with Salsa Therapy, perfect for both beginners and dance lovers. Special guests: Estudio de Samba Wellington and Wellicomedia.

Complete the experience by tasting Latin America with Argentine, Peruvian, and Brazilian flavours, alongside traditional Latin snacks and drinks provided by Latino Foods NZ.

Tickets:

Early Bird $20 (till12th March)

Standard $25 (till 10th April)

Find us!

https://www.facebook.com/LatinEventsWelly

https://www.instagram.com/latineventswelly

#WellingtonEvents #WhatsOnWellington #CulturalEventsNZ #LiveMusicWellington #LatinCultureNZ
