events.id: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011-All Events In
events.name: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
events.long_description:
Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!
UPGRADED TO MEOW NUI DUE TO OVERWHELMING DEMAND

Handsome Tours are proud to present The Mountain Goats!

Veteran indie-rock storytellers The Mountain Goats will make their long-awaited return to Australia and New Zealand in April 2026, with performances in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth, Auckland and Wellington. This eagerly-anticipated tour represents the band's first visit to Australia in 9 years (2017) and their first trip to New Zealand in 16 years (2010).

Fronted by prolific songwriter and novelist John Darnielle, The Mountain Goats are revered the world over for their sprawling catalogue of twenty-three studio albums released between 1994 and 2023. This tour announcement coincides with the band today unveiling album number twenty-four, 'Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan' - to be released 7th November 2025 on Cadmean Dawn Records (the band’s own label).

Produced by the Mountain Goats’ multi-instrumentalist Matt Douglas, Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan is a full-on musical that stands as the most conceptually detailed and musically elaborate project in the band’s ever-expanding catalogue. It features appearances from The Replacements’ Tommy Stinson, harpist Mikaela Davis, musical theatre royalty Lin-Manuel Miranda (Tony Award-winning ‘Hamilton’, Disney’s ‘Moana’ & ‘Encanto’), and the band’s new bassist Cameron Ralston.

Don’t miss one of indie rock’s most enduring and beloved bands as they make their long-overdue return to our shores!

events.name: Resonant Rave.🪩 Move Sober, Dance Deeper.
events.venue: St Peters Village Hall, Paekākāriki,19 Beach Rd, Paekakariki 5034, New Zealand, Paekakariki
events.long_description:
Resonant Rave.🪩 Move Sober, Dance Deeper.
https://events.humanitix.com/resonant-rave-kuksdam5

✨ A Night for the High Vibe Dancers ✨
Love the energy of a club night — the music, the dancing — but not the hangover?

Craving that connected dance floor feeling? Welcome to Resonant Rave — where it's purely about music, movement, and a welcoming community. 🪩💫

No booze — just pure vibes, connection, and the freedom to be fully you.

Whether you’re sober, sober-curious, or simply want to experience dance in a new, elevated way — this night is for you.

Come dance. Come connect. Come as you are.

Expect bass-laden melodic grooves, warm smiles, and the kind of connection that happens when everyone’s really there.

Move sober. Dance deeper. Let the resonance move through you.

🎵Music Vibe: House: Breaks: Psy Dub: Melodic Techno

🎧 With special guest DJ:

HINERAU-KAHUKURA
A Māori wahine DJ, taught the craft by her late son, DJ Waylow, she brings a fresh and evolving sound shaped by soul, vibration and passion for the decks. From intimate sets to clubs and events across Aotearoa she’s been carving out her own space in the scene over the last 2 years,
Now focused on honing her craft further, she’s only just getting started.

🪩

🎧 As well as your residents Kimba & Codaraan:

KIMBA JADE
Kimba Jade has been lighting up dancefloors for over 20 years with her signature blend of deep, funky, and globally inspired grooves. A master of musical journeying, Kimba weaves bass-driven beats with tribal, melodic, and soulful sounds that move both body and spirit.

With a deep passion for Ecstatic Dance and the power of music to connect and uplift, Kimba brings that same vibrant, heart-opening energy into clubs and bars. Her favourite styles to play are Tech House infused with Indie Dance, Techno, Deep Tech/Minimal, blending rhythms with emotive melodies to create a sound that’s both driving and deeply immersive.

After years of spinning in Sydney and Auckland’s underground scene — from conscious dance floors to late-night sessions — Kimba is now based in Wellington, bringing her signature fusion of rhythm, soul, and bass to local venues. Whether behind the decks at a festival, Ecstatic Dance, or a packed club night, Kimba’s passion is the same: to create an unforgettable atmosphere where freedom, rhythm, and connection collide.

Link to music
https://soundcloud.com/kimba
@djkimbajade
🪩

CODARAAN
Codaraan will be opening the night with music that invites you to move and connect with your body, preparing you perfectly for an evening of dancing. Expect a seductive groove-focused journey that builds slowly, allowing you to find your flow at your pace.

Tickets $20 online and $25 on the door:
https://events.humanitix.com/resonant-rave-kuksdam5
🪩

SET TIMES:
7pm - 8.15pm = Codaraan
8.15pm - 9.30pm = Hinerau-Kahukura
9.30pm - 10.45pm = Kimba Jade

💃 This event is All Ages 😎

Come 🪩 Dance like no-one is watching.

Love Resonant Collective
(Kimberley Jade & Bernie Cruise) ❤️

events.id: Ride the Cyclone-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ride the Cyclone
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Darkly funny, wildly imaginative, and surprisingly heartfelt, Ride the Cyclone is the viral musical phenomenon that has taken the world by storm. Buckle up for the ride of a lifetime, as Ride the Cyclone crashes into Wellington to make its Aotearoa New Zealand premiere at Circa Theatre next April.

In this hilarious and exhilarating story, the lives of six teenagers of the St. Cassian Chamber Choir are changed forever after they board a doomed rollercoaster ride.

Thrown into the abyss, they awake to find an ominous mechanical fortune teller that offers them the chance to compete for the prize … to return home in one piece.

Now confronted with their darkest desires, shameful secrets and each other, this “supremely witty … and just plain delightful” (New York Times) musical makes a uniquely uplifting and outrageous case for what makes a life worth living.

Featuring a deliriously funny, genre-defying score, Ride the Cyclone introduces some of the most remarkable characters in modern musical theatre – brought to life by six of Wellington’s finest musical theatre performers.

★★★★★ "...utterly charming, utterly bonkers, and utterly wonderful.” – ArtsHub Australia

★★★★ “Dark yet camp, nihilistic yet strangely life-affirming, this show is full of surprises and just as brilliantly bonkers as it sounds. This darkly comedic viral musical sensation is a wild ride” – Time Out AU

events.name: LNKF #63: Mal and Mog
events.venue: BATS Theatre,1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
LNKF #63: Mal and Mog
Late Night Knife Fight is the monthly improv battle where three teams face off in a fight for glory, honour, and the audience's favour. The winner returns in the headline spot next month; the losers wail and gnash their teeth.

We'll bring the knives; you bring the judgement.

Headlined by:
🔪 MAL AND MOG (Megan Connolly and Malcolm Morrison)

Contested by:
🔪 THE HISTORY BOY: WILD PLANET (Matt Powell)
🔪 ALPHA? BET! (Steven Youngblood, Guanny Liu-Prosee and Annabel)
🔪 COMICAZZI (Lyndon Hood)

The first rule of Knife Fight is... BOOK EARLY
Monthly (ish) at BATS Theatre
Tix $25 Waged / $15 Unwaged
Upcoming teams, full title history and more at http://knifefight.nz

~~~

ACCESSIBILITY
The Stage is located on the ground floor. The ground floor at BATS is fully wheelchair accessible with a ramp at the north end of the building, which provides direct access to the foyer and bar area, and a wheelchair accessible Box Office counter and bathroom on the ground floor.

If you have accessibility questions or requirements please contact BATS directly on or call 04 802 4175.

The venue's bar Lumen is open from 5pm, doors open 10 minutes before showtime. Seating is not allocated. Latecomers will be admitted but you have to go across the stage - we recommend being on time 🙂

~~~

Late Night Knife Fight (presented by Locomotive) is a joyful proving ground for improvisors creating new work and ensembles, offering development, coaching, and collaboration across companies and styles. Seize the opportunity to catch the next big thing in improv before it takes flight!

http://knifefight.nz

events.long_description:
Late Night Knife Fight is the monthly improv battle where three teams face off in a fight for glory, honour, and the audience's favour. The winner returns in the headline spot next month; the losers wail and gnash their teeth.

We'll bring the knives; you bring the judgement.

The first rule of Knife Fight is... book early!
Upcoming teams, full title history and more at Knife Fight's website.

"Being handed a knife as I enter, albeit a plastic one, I could not help but get the feeling that this was going to be a more hardcore night of improv than I had experienced in a while (Late Night Knife Fight) has real potential to become a Wellington institution." - Art Murmurs

"Fundamentally precious" - Theatreview

Late Night Knife Fight is a joyful proving ground for improvisers creating new work and ensembles, offering development, coaching, and collaboration across companies and styles. An opportunity to catch the next big thing in improv before it takes flight, work developed at LNKF has gone on to full seasons and festivals, with The National Average, Two Dope Teens (Best Improv Nelson Fringe 2018), The History Boy, Magnus Steele, and more in development.

events.id: Waypeople Album Release Tour - Wellington-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Waypeople Album Release Tour - Wellington
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Wellington-based saxophonist and composer Jake Baxendale brings together a remarkable collective of Aotearoa’s leading jazz and contemporary musicians for Waypeople, a luminous new album inspired by Ursula K. Le Guin’s English-language version of the Tao Te Ching. Drawing from jazz, Chinese classical music and improvisation, the project fuses lyrical storytelling with meditative textures and powerful ensemble interplay.

Featuring Chelsea Prastiti (voice), Jia Ling “Jessie” (guzheng), Callum Passells (saxophones), Daniel Hayles (piano), Seth Boy (double bass), and Rick Cranson (drums), Waypeople transforms the timeless Taoist philosophy of harmony, balance, and paradox into sound. The result is a work of shimmering beauty — grounded in jazz but alive with the rhythm of ancient poetry and the pulse of contemporary life.

Tickets $20-30+BF https://events.humanitix.com/waypeople-album-release-tour-wellington/tickets

events.id: The WBS presents: Frank John and Erin Manu featuring Rob Greenfield-Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: The WBS presents: Frank John and Erin Manu featuring Rob Greenfield
events.venue: Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
The WBS presents: Frank John and Erin Manu featuring Rob Greenfield
Wow, you're in for a full night of class entertainment!

Originally from Amsterdam, Frank John brings his technical brilliance to this New Plymouth-based duo, and his Taranaki-born wife Erin Manu provides driving rhythm guitar and has a reputation for her unique style on banjo - described as having 'a kinda swagger' - by Albi from Albi and The Wolves.

Whether it's a sizzling acoustic instrumental or the soulful delivery of a ballad, they capture the essence of guitar and song through the variations of the acoustic genres – folk, blues, country, swing and classical, all wrapped into a neat Kiwi-Dutch package. They perform hidden gems, well-known standards and their own compositions.

Frank and Erin have recorded a compilation of some of their original instrumentals, which will be released this year on CD and online. Each composition is finely crafted and features Rob Greenfield (bass), Ryan John (guitar), Jude Douglas (violin), Liz Merton (violin) and Cindy Muggeridge (keyboard).

For this concert, Frank and Erin will be joined by Robert Greenfield (bass).
In addition, all musicians who recorded on the CD will be performing during this concert.

“Erin has a voice like a crema-rich latte”
– Auckland Folk Festival fan

"Beautifully concise guitar playing, tasty harmonies, well crafted originals and brilliant covers"
– Chris Penman

Frank John and Erin Manu - 'Venice'
Featuring Robert Greenfield and Ryan John:


DOORS:
7.00pm

SHOW STARTS:
8.00pm

ENTRY:
$30 / $25 members

please bring cash to pay for tickets,
or book prior to the day via email to:


Plenty of tasty treats available at the venue, come for a drink (or two) and dinner before the show starts.

www.abandonedbrewery.co.nz/taprooms#Petone

events.id: URGE BLACK-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: URGE BLACK
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Unleash your inner dark side at URGE BLACK. The pulsating rhythms of liberation meet the sultry allure of leather-clad revelry. This isn’t just a dance party; it’s a sensorial odyssey that transcends age and convention. Prepare to be captivated, challenged, and ultimately liberated in an atmosphere where every shimmy and shake is an act of personal revolution.
URGE doesn’t just throw parties; we craft transformative experiences that celebrate the raw, unbridled essence of gay culture. Leather, fetish, uniform, gear, sports kit, etc.
Let’s dance and celebrate our authentic selves!
URGE tips:
Doors open at 9:00 PM.
Door sales are limited and $60 (cash only) if the event has not sold out prior.
Peak times: Queues can form if everyone arrives at once. We work to get you in quickly, so please be patient and courteous to our staff.

events.id: Wellington Burner PICNIC - at CENTRAL PARK PLAY AREA-Brooklyn Rd, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Wellington Burner PICNIC - at CENTRAL PARK PLAY AREA
events.venue: Brooklyn Rd, Wellington
events.long_description:
Kiwiburn Community Picnic @ Central Park (Bi-Monthly Hang)
We’re kicking off our bi-monthly community picnics, and you’re warmly invited to come hang out. Bring yourself, bring a friend, bring a blanket, and kai for low-key good times with burners in the (mild) wild
Weather backup: if the weather turns feral and you’ve got a space where our crew could gather instead, sing out! Community hosts always appreciated.
Where: Central Park Play Area (child-friendly spot)
When: We’ll go from 1pm until we naturally peter out
Bring along:
- BYO picnic (no cooking facilities available at this location)
- Instruments if you’ve got them. Guitars, small drums, anything easy to carry for some light tunes
- Good vibes and curiosity
A few practical bits:
Dogs welcome, but must be on-leash at this spot.
On-street parking available, with easy access to/from SH1.
Public transport: No. 7 bus from Wellington Station with the stop right outside at the park gate.
Central Park tracks are nearby if anyone feels like a short bushwalk side quest (some tracks may be closed, so keep an eye out).
This one’s all about simple connection. Meet new folks, catch up with old ones, lounge in the grass, maybe wander the bush, maybe make a little music.
See you there

events.venue: 111 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The National Brass Band of New Zealand is set to light up Wellington Opera House this April with a world-class Gala Concert you won’t want to miss.
Following the National Contest, the National Band's performance features phenomenal guest artists: Harmen Vanhoorne, Celine Wu, Helen and Glyn Williams, alongside the band’s principal trombone, Aishah Leitner.

events.id: Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Weekend: Oceans of the Mind Discussion Panel-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Weekend: Oceans of the Mind Discussion Panel
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Over the weekend of 11 and 12 of April we will be staging a very special series of exploration events to celebrate the recent reopening of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui. The panel topic for our Sunday 12 April 2pm panel is: 'Oceans of the Mind'.
This event will be held in the beautiful Ngā Pou Ruahine space on the second floor. This is a free event and all are welcome. We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited, so please arrive early to ensure a seat.
Meet our esteemed panellists...
Grant Nalder:
Grant Nadler is our beloved Wellington Harbourmaster. He has devoted most of his working life to the sea and has a treasure trove of fabulous nautical true-life tales to draw on.
Dr Nicole Miller:
A keen explorer of the underwater world, Nicole uses citizen science, visual storytelling, and art to connect people of all ages with their local marine environment. As the founder of Explore Your Coast (https://exploreyourcoast.co.nz/), she is passionate about bringing the underwater world to shore - sparking curiosity, a deeper sense of connection and care, and action to protect and restore marine ecosystems and biodiversity across New Zealand.
The short documentary 'Seaweed, A Love Story', featuring Nicole’s work, is now screening at venues across New Zealand.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.id: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)-39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
events.venue: 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Please note: the event is free but it can only continue if we support the Southern Cross by buying food or drinks

Wellington’s favourite monthly Bachata Latin dance party

Join us for an awesome mix of bachata, focusing on the best sensual tracks and remixes, along with salsa and a smattering of kizomba and zouk. We welcome everyone, from the absolute beginner to the most experienced dancer. We’re all about the joy of social dancing together.
And what makes a free party with great people even better? A free beginners’ bachata lesson with some of Wellington‘s best Latin dance teachers. If you’ve never danced bachata, Latin dance or even any other dance style before then this is the perfect lesson for you. We will have you dancing like a pro in no time!
Bailamos Bachata is hosted free of charge by the Southern Cross. They love the atmosphere we bring but we also need to support them in return. We’re asking everyone attending to purchase soft drinks, food or alcohol from the bar. It’s vital we show our gratitude.
1800-1830 social
1830-1900 free lesson
1900-2100 social
Q&A
Q. What is bachata?
A. Bachata is a popular and fun Latin couples dance. It started in the Dominican Republic, then travelled to Europe and the rest of the world. It now includes a wide range of music styles and dance influences and is still changing and evolving.
Q. I haven’t danced before, is that a problem?
A. Not at all! That’s exactly what the lesson is for – everyone has to start somewhere and we’re a very welcoming crowd for beginners.
Q. Is bachata a difficult dance to learn?
A. Its origins as a party/community dance mean the basics can be learnt quite quickly, so you’ll be enjoying yourself in no time.
Q. Who are the capital bachata collective?
A. We are the combined Wellington dance schools and people teaching bachata and we really want to share this dance with everyone. We love bachata, we love our community and we want to share it with you
￼

The Artisan Craft Market is a premium handmade event that takes place across various locations in the broader Wellington region. This high-quality market is designed to foster a welcoming and friendly atmosphere.

Our vision at Artisan Craft Market is to make the market accessible to those who appreciate shopping locally. events.id: Autumn Textured Pendants/Earrings-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Autumn Textured Pendants/Earrings
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This quick, creative taster class is perfect for a fun outing with friends, a thoughtful gift-making session, or a treat just for you.

In just three hours, you’ll learn to craft a stunning pair of textured metal earrings from scratch. You’ll be guided step-by-step through heating and texturing the metal, then cutting and shaping it with traditional jewellery tools. By the end, you’ll have a beautiful pair of earrings to wear proudly or gift to someone you care about!

You will practice on smaller texture samples to work out what texture and what metal you want to go with, and then move on to creating your earrings in warm copper or yellow brass.

Your class materials pack includes everything you need for the class to make a super cool pair of shiny gorgeous earrings.
Using copper and brass as our main material allows us to have more creative fun and create larger earrings with less expense.

We do have a lovely range of sterling silver sheets that you can purchase as extra if you are committed to working in silver!

We use quality pre-made sterling silver and stainless steel earring hooks and hoops for these taster classes so that you can focus on making the main part of the earrings and know that your findings will be just perfect.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany anyone under the age of 16.

events.name: Sunday Koha Yoga - Heated Flow
events.venue: Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us EVERY Sunday for a 90 minute Heated Flow for koha entry (ie, give what you can)! Proceeds are donated to a different charitable organisation each month that's committed to supporting the wellbeing of Aotearoa.

This weekly class is open to everyone, regardless of yoga experience - haere mai! BYO mat or borrow one from us. Towel hire $3 if needed, and make sure to bring a water bottle!

To book: Download the AWHI app to view the timetable, or simply show up in good time on Sunday!

WHAT IS 'KOHA'?
Koha loosely translates to 'gift' or exchange. In other words, pay what you can for the class!

For more information about our studio, visit our website at awhiyoga.co.nz

events.id: Vintage cake masterclass-Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Vintage cake masterclass
events.venue: Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Step into the charm of classic pâtisserie and learn the art of timeless cake design in our Vintage Cake Masterclass.

In this guided, hands-on session, each guest will receive a 10cm mini chocolate cake — beautifully prepared and ready for decorating. You’ll begin with a live demonstration by Chef Shuchi Bhardwaj, who will walk you through the techniques and piping styles that define elegant vintage cakes.

Then, with expert guidance, you’ll recreate the design on your own cake, icing and piping step by step to achieve a stunning, nostalgic finish.

All materials are thoughtfully provided, including:

• Freshly baked mini chocolate cake
• Buttercream icing
• Decorations and piping tools
• Cake box to take your creation home

Whether you’re a beginner or a baking enthusiast, this interactive class is designed to be relaxed, creative, and enjoyable.

Gluten-free options are available.
$70 per person, including a hot beverage of your choice.

Come for the experience, leave with new skills — and a cake that looks straight out of a vintage bakery window.

events.venue: Grants Bush Reserve, Wallaceville Estate,Wallaceville Estate, Gilruth Rd, Trentham, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Wallaceville Community Open Day
🌞 WALLACEVILLE COMMUNITY OPEN DAY 2026 🌞
🎉 Part of the U-PLAY Holiday Programme! 🎉

📅 Sunday, 12 April 2026
🕑 2:00pm – 4:00pm
📍 Grants Bush Reserve, Wallaceville, Upper Hutt
🎟️ FREE ENTRY

✨ Get ready, Wallaceville! ✨
Join us for an unforgettable afternoon packed with fun, laughter, and vibrant community spirit 💛

Bring your friends, family, and neighbours along to celebrate everything that makes Wallaceville special.

There’ll be bouncy castles, fun activities, face painting, and delicious food and ice cream!

We also have a fire truck fun for the kids (and the young at heart!). It’s the perfect chance to catch up with familiar faces, meet new people, and enjoy the great outdoors at our beautiful Grants Bush Reserve.

So grab your sunhat and sunglasses and join us for a day of connection, community, and good vibes — fun for the whole whānau!

events.id: Amici (Saxophone Quintet)-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Amici (Saxophone Quintet)
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Long-established Amici Ensemble performs each year with varying combinations of instruments. In 2026 this is a quintet with the saxophone adding richness to a string quartet. The performers are Donald Armstrong – violin, Anna van der Zee – violin, Nicholas Hancox – viola, Andrew Joyce – cello, and Simon Brew – saxophone. The string players are members of the NZSO, joined by Simon Brew who is much in demand as a saxophonist.

Since Adolf Sax invented the saxophone in the 1840s, composers have been enchanted with its sonorities, combining the saxophone with other instruments in many idioms. Composers such as Debussy and Ravel incorporated its beautiful sweetness in their music, influencing more recent composers such as Ellen Zwilich and Russell Peterson.

This unusual lineup of saxophone and string quartet, while embracing their classical origins, plunge headlong into the evocative sounds, catchy rhythms and soaring melodies that this combination lends itself to.

Russell Peterson, Quintet for Alto Saxophone and Strings
Max Richter, On the Nature of Daylight (string quartet)
Ellen Taaffe Zwilich, Quintet for Alto Saxophone and Strings
Wolfgang A Mozart, String Quartet K590
Astor Piazzolla, The Four Seasons: Winter and Spring

events.id: Sunday Jazz - James Wylie-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Sunday Jazz - James Wylie
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer, hangs, and jazz, on a Sunday, perfect!

This week featuring local saxophonist, back in town briefly from Greece, James Wylie

A modern look at the saxophone-organ trio tradition through the prism of original compositions by James Wylie and Miles Crayford. Joining them will be long-time collaborator, drummer Mike Jensen.

Miles Crayford and James Wylie have been musical brothers since their teenage years and their influence on each other has been central to their artistic worldview. Since that time, their individual paths have diverged, been enriched and new musical identities have been forged. This meeting will be the first time playing together in more than ten years (and Wylie's first appearance in Wellington in as many years) and will feature a range of newly composed orginal music by both musicians. For this performance Crayford will be performing on Hammond organ and Wylie on tenor saxophone - a nod to their childhood trio which was active in and around Wellington in which Mike Jensen held down the drum chair. This is a rare opportunity to hear this group due to James Wylie being based in Europe for the last 15 years. Not to be missed!

Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY

events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Monk and Munchies
Monk & Munchies is where plant-based eats meet meaningful conversation - with guest speaker, author and monk, Devamrita Swami.

About this Event


Monk & Munchies is where plant-based eats meet meaningful conversation.

This time we’re joined by Devamrita Swami a monk, author, and speaker who travels the globe meeting people and sharing practical wisdom that actually fits into everyday life. Come meet him in person and see what you take from the conversation.
We’ll warm things up with mantra music meditation, live music, voices rising together, and that familiar Bhakti Lounge buzz building in the room.
Stick around later as the mantra picks up and the dancing begins!

Then it’s time to eat.
Think:
BBQ Cauliflower Pizza
Crumbed Zucchini Fries with Yum Yum Sauce
Fresh Salad with Lemon Maple Vinaigrette
Brownie with whipped coconut cream & berry sauce
Sangria mocktail


Good food, live mantra music, a great social scene, and insights that might stay with you long after the night ends.

Pull up, meet some good sorts, and see what happens. 🎶





Agenda


🕑: 05:45 PM - 06:00 PM
Doors Open at Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:00 PM
Mantra Music ~Kirtan

🕑: 06:30 PM - 07:05 PM
Talk with a Monk

🕑: 07:05 PM - 07:30 PM
Mantra Music~Kirtan and Dance

🕑: 07:30 PM - 08:00 PM
Plant-based dinner (the best part of the night!)

🕑: 08:00 PM - 09:00 PM
After Dinner Mantra Music & Dance

events.id: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)-Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant,39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
events.venue: Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant,39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
🔥 Please note: the event is free but it can only continue if we support the Southern Cross by buying food or drinks 🔥


🌟 Wellington’s favourite monthly Bachata Latin dance party 🌟


Join us for an awesome mix of bachata, focusing on the best sensual tracks and remixes, along with salsa and a smattering of kizomba and zouk. We welcome everyone, from the absolute beginner to the most experienced dancer. We’re all about the joy of social dancing together.

And what makes a free party with great people even better? A free beginners’ bachata lesson with some of Wellington‘s best Latin dance teachers. If you’ve never danced bachata, Latin dance or even any other dance style before then this is the perfect lesson for you. We will have you dancing like a pro in no time!

Bailamos Bachata is hosted free of charge by the Southern Cross. They love the atmosphere we bring but we also need to support them in return. We’re asking everyone attending to purchase soft drinks, food or alcohol from the bar. It’s vital we show our gratitude.

1800-1830 social
1830-1900 free lesson
1900-2100 social

Q&A
Q. What is bachata?
A. Bachata is a popular and fun Latin couples dance. It started in the Dominican Republic, then travelled to Europe and the rest of the world. It now includes a wide range of music styles and dance influences and is still changing and evolving.

Q. I haven’t danced before, is that a problem?
A. Not at all! That’s exactly what the lesson is for – everyone has to start somewhere and we’re a very welcoming crowd for beginners.

Q. Is bachata a difficult dance to learn?
A. Its origins as a party/community dance mean the basics can be learnt quite quickly, so you’ll be enjoying yourself in no time.

Q. Who are the capital bachata collective?
A. We are the combined Wellington dance schools and people teaching bachata and we really want to share this dance with everyone. We love bachata, we love our community and we want to share it with you
￼

events.id: SUENA ORO - TE HEKE TŌ TAURA KŌRARI-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: SUENA ORO - TE HEKE TŌ TAURA KŌRARI
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Suena Oro, a te reo Māori/Spanish fusion band from Whakatū/Nelson are bringing their psychedelic sonic experience to Valhalla on their ‘Te Heke Tō Taura Kōrari’ tour.
Whakatu’s fertile music scene has been ignited by Suena Oro’s hypnotic extended jams that touch on folk, reggae, drone-music and other broad genres, all the while keeping experimentation, improvisation and taonga pūoro at the core.
Suena Oro’s original songs draw inspiration from tangata whenuatanga, a rich depth of Spanish heritage and many years of experience at the coalface of various musical forms. The songs are vehicles for political and humanist perspectives riding dance-orientated grooves. Kanikani mai!
Suena Oro will be supported at Valhalla by Whakatū-based live electronic artists Droneeater and Alvaro Moreno. Droneeater - aka Chris Mulholland - performs live te reo Māori dance orientated music. His 2025 single ‘Tō Wairua’ reached number #1 on Radioactive’s Top 11 Charts. Alvaro Moreno, alongside running the record label ‘Nelson Techno Collective’ that released Droneeater’s 2025 EP ‘Ngā Oro Rōria’, performs live techno-inspired music.
Tickets: $10 presale via UTR or $15 at the door.

events.id: Georgia Knight-San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Georgia Knight
events.venue: San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way. After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band.

Naarm-born and sometimes Aotearoa-based, Knight crafts otherworldly pop from autoharp, dusty loops, synth textures, and avant-garde edges. Her 2023 EP Hell on Bent Street established her as a rising force, with appearances at Dark Mofo, Rising, Camp A Low Hum, and shows alongside Blonde Redhead and Mogwai.

It’s been a big 12 months for Georgia. Her independently released debut album Beanpole, described by KEXP as "an evocative set of dimly-lit, hypnotic, psych-tinted pop", earned praise from Sharon Van Etten, Arab Strap, and BBC Radio 6 Music. Critics highlighted her "shadowy, seductive trip-hop textures" (KCRW), "extraordinary vocal range and graveside poems" (The 13th Floor), and "goosebumps"-inducing live performances (BBC Radio 6).

Supporting the album, she was booked for a string of highly coveted festivals including The Great Escape (UK), Golden Plains (AU), Phoenix Central Park (AU), The Others Way (NZ), and Port Noise (NZ).

Now touring her 2025 album Beanpole, recorded with Andrew ‘Idge’ Hehir, Georgia returns with a fuller, immersive live show, intimate, cinematic, and impossible to look away from.
events.description:
Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way.
After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band.

events.id: Emily Kristen Morris & JJ Niemann in Concert!-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Emily Kristen Morris & JJ Niemann in Concert!
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Emily Kristen Morris and JJ Niemann, all the way from NYC, are joining forces for a one-of-a-kind concert experience in New Zealand, proudly presented by Little Joke Productions, performing for one night only.
Emily brings her powerful voice, captivating stage presence, and professional experience from lead roles in ‘Waitress’, ‘Wicked’, ‘Frozen’, and ‘Something Rotten’. She has also performed internationally with symphony orchestras and shares her expertise with millions online as a celebrated vocal coach.

JJ, known for his charismatic performances, exceptional vocals, and impressive Broadway and regional theatre credits, has starred in ‘The Book of Mormon’, ‘Hamilton’, ‘Back to the Future’, and ‘Catch Me If You Can’, and has captivated over 2 million fans online with his engaging performances.

Don’t miss this exclusive and unforgettable night of music and storytelling – grab your tickets now and experience Emily Kristen Morris and JJ Niemann live in concert!

Emily Kristen Morris & JJ Niemann in Concert!

Circa One
Duration: 90 minutes no interval

events.name: Porirua Grand Traverse - 2026
events.venue: Kai Tahi,20 Parumoana Street, Porirua, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Porirua Grand Traverse - 2026
Established in 2006, the Porirua Grand Traverse showcases the region's awesome outdoor resource and active lifestyles. From the Arena 9k Fun Run/Walk, and FS Cycles 13k Fun Bike, through to the 56k Multisport and 48k Duathlon events, to simple Paddle, Mtn Bike and Mtn Run options, this vibrant festival of fitness has something for everyone.

It is a non-profit event organised locally by the Porirua Grand Traverse charitable trust and since day one our goals has remained the same...

All Ages: We're about participation for everyone from retirees to kids!
All Abilities: Options for rookies to experienced endurance junkies!
All Amazing: Showcasing Porirua's hills & harbour, bush & beaches!

events.id: K-Pop Live-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: K-Pop Live
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Bursting with colourful costumes and exciting choreography, this is the ultimate high-energy concert experience. K-Pop Live is a fun-filled one hour show bringing you the music and dance of the biggest K-pop hits. Hear all your favourite hits from Gangnam Style, the song that brought K-pop to the Western mainstream, through to Golden and Soda Pop by Demon Hunters. There’ll also be bangers from Blackpink, BTS, Stray Kids and many more.
The show is directed and choreographed by Rina Chae, the renowned Korean–New Zealand dancer, choreographer, and K-pop industry professional. Rina had a pivotal role in introducing and establishing K-pop dance culture in Aotearoa. She has performed with global icons including Beyoncé and Justin Bieber, choreographed for artists across Australia, the UK, and New Zealand.

events.id: Pottery wheel throwing & hand building - creative arts Holiday program-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Pottery wheel throwing & hand building - creative arts Holiday program
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore the techniques of pottery making including creating a mug using handbuilding processes and making a small bowl using throwing techniques on a pottery wheel. Students will be shown how to centre clay and make a small bowl on the wheel and trim it. Cups and bowls will be glazed by the tutor and ready for pickup approximately 3-4 weeks later depending on drying conditions.

This workshop is limited to three students only to ensure each person has individualised attention.

Suitable for ages 12+.

events.id: Musicking Together: Capoeira for Music therapy week-Evaro, Level 1, James Smith Corner, 65 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Musicking Together: Capoeira for Music therapy week
events.venue: Evaro, Level 1, James Smith Corner, 65 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Musicking Together is a community music circle. It's free, accessible, open to anyone and instruments are provided.

It is run by Sophie Sabri, a registered music therapist and in collaboration with different local musicians each month.
This month we will host Michael Person for a night of capoeira rhythms, songs and dance.
It's is also MUSIC THERAPY WEEK and the theme this year is about Weaving Connections, Te Poutama Hononga.

Come along to meet new people and play music without pressure.
"Musicking Together" is a space to create music together, to dance, to sing or simply listen to others. As long as we are in space, we are musicking.

How to find us? Evaro is located in the James Smith building, at the corner between Manners and Cuba Street. Enter the building and pass Rebel Sports to find the stairs. Go up to level 1 and follow the corridor on the left. You'll find Evaro to your left.

events.id: Jurassic Park-The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Roxy Cinema
events.name: Jurassic Park
events.venue: The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Details
Directed by Steven Spielberg
Starring Jeff Goldblum, Samuel L. Jackson, Joseph Mazzello, Laura Dern, Sam Neill, Richard Attenborough, Wayne Knight, Ariana Richards, BD Wong
Designed to showcase the incredibly creative and ever-changing menu of our playful, fine dining establishment CoCo At The Roxy. EAT THE FILM provides film and food fans with a cult classic film and delicious seasonal and thematic dishes* designed to arrive in-theatre, perfectly timed to specific scenes.

All tickets include several themed dishes and drinks brought to you in the theatre, an experience with more than enough to sate your eyes and appetite.

This menu will be in keeping with the herbivore dinosaurs and will be fully vegetarian.

--- In Steven Spielberg's massive blockbuster, paleontologists Alan Grant (Sam Neill) and Ellie Sattler (Laura Dern) and mathematician Ian Malcolm (Jeff Goldblum) are among a select group chosen to tour an island theme park populated by dinosaurs created from prehistoric DNA. While the park's mastermind, billionaire John Hammond (Richard Attenborough), assures everyone that the facility is safe, they find out otherwise when various ferocious predators break free and go on the hunt. ---

Schedule:
6:00pm – The Roxy main doors open
6:00pm – Themed pre-show upstairs in the Grand Lobby
6:20pm – Cinema doors open
6:30pm – Quick intro
6:35pm – Feature begins

When purchased tickets either select ETF - Alcohol for the Cocktail option or ETF - Non-Alc for the Mocktail version of drinks.

*No dietaries catered for.

Presented in 4K

events.id: Paint and Wine Night - Mohua in Autumn-The Arborist Rooftop Bar, 166 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Paint and Wine Night - Mohua in Autumn
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop Bar, 166 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

events.id: Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening (JBLZE) is coming to The Opera House for a night of rock and roll classics. The landmark tour will let fans experience hits such as ‘Kashmir’ and ‘The Wanton Song’ live alongside deeper cuts like ‘In The Light’ and ‘Boogie With Stu.’ Along with playing the iconic album in its entirety, other Led Zeppelin favourites, including ‘Good Times Bad Times,’ ‘Whole Lotta Love”, and of course ‘Stairway To Heaven’, find their way into the setlist. 
“For me, this isn’t just an album — it’s the album,” declares Jason Bonham. “Physical Graffiti has always been my ultimate Led Zeppelin record, and I set out to honour its 50th Anniversary with 50 unforgettable shows." Jason is the son of Led Zeppelin drummer John Bonham. Since his father’s death, he has performed with the three surviving members of Led Zeppelin on several occasions.

events.id: Children's Creative Arts Holiday Programme 6-10 years-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Children's Creative Arts Holiday Programme 6-10 years
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
The school holiday programme has been designed to challenge and encourage children from 6 years old to 10 years old who love art. The programme is a mixed media experience, used to teach the creative possibilities in the various art genres. Students will have made a variety of art objects by the end of the week and can take them home. All materials are supplied, including smocks to help keep them clean.

Each holiday, the programme changes to add variety for the many returning students.

This programme includes 4 x morning sessions over Tuesday - Friday, 14-17 April. All materials included.

events.id: Monoprinting - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Tweens, Teens Or Ad-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Monoprinting - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Tweens, Teens Or Ad
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Of all the print-making methods, mono printing is the most spontaneous, responsive and versatile, being the medium for everything from abstract expressionism to hyper photographic realism. Simple and direct with the minimum of equipment and media, mono printing is approachable for any age or ability level, yet the creative potential is unlimited.

Bring a water bottle and a packed lunch to enjoy in the historic Inverlochy house during break time.

Two-day workshop on Tuesday & Wednesday, 14-15 April, 10 am - 2 pm daily.

All materials included. Recommended age 12 and above.

events.name: Paint your pet - Creative Arts holiday programme for tweens & teens
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bring in a photo of your or a friend's pet to copy or use as a guide to create the scene. You will learn the basic properties of watercolour paint mediums to help highlight the unique character of the animal you paint.

events.id: School Holiday Workshop: Splat & Sculpt-The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: School Holiday Workshop: Splat & Sculpt
events.venue: The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
School Holiday Workshop: Splat & Sculpt
Create hanging sculptures (Kids Only)

About this Event

Drop-off your kids to create their own art inspired by the sculptural work of George Agius.

We will use our bodies to create moulds and transform them into unique hanging sculptures. Experiment with shapes, textures, and materials as you press, imprint, and cast your forms. Add layers of colour and bring your creations to life and hang them to sway, wobble, and float.

events.id: Wellington Government House Garden tour-Government House Visitor Centre, Hospital Road Entrance, Newtown Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Wellington Government House Garden tour
events.venue: Government House Visitor Centre, Hospital Road Entrance, Newtown Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore Government House gardens with the BARK garden team (90 minute tour)

Government House was opened in Newtown in 1910, and its extensive gardens have been in continuous development since then. As part of the Spring Festival, the Government House garden team is offering guided walks through our gardens. Tours will go ahead regardless of weather. Tours will not enter the main house.

Bookings are essential for the free tour. Places are limited: https://gg.govt.nz/government-house/government-house-wellington/government-house-tours/upcoming

Some parts of our grounds are not mobility friendly. Please contact the Visitor Centre direct if you have any questions regarding accessibility.

Groups wanting to organise a garden tour or house tour can email our Visitor Centre team direct: bookings@govthouse.govt.nz

If you have any questions or need more information, please email us at bookings@govthouse.govt.nz, or call us at 04-382-0837. You will receive a reminder email 24 hours before your tour.

As part of the Spring Festival, the Government House garden team is offering guided walks through our gardens.
Tours will go ahead regardless of weather.
Bookings are essential for the free tour.
Groups wanting to organise a garden tour or house tour can email our Visitor Centre team direct: bookings@govthouse.govt.nz If you have any questions or need more information, please email us at bookings@govthouse.govt.nz, or call us at 04-382-0837.

events.id: Draw It Out Teens Workshop-Drama Studio House, 93 Kelburn Parade, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Draw It Out Teens Workshop
events.venue: Drama Studio House, 93 Kelburn Parade, Wellington
events.long_description:
Draw It Out Teens Workshop

Theme: Learn a tool that helps when feeling stuck
Teens 11-17 get to learn a new tool that can help when working through hard stuff and helps to listen to their own inner voice to move forward.

This single workshop helps teens to use drawing to access their inner resources for when they feel stuck, either with hard feelings or moving forward, because often they have the guidance within, and just need help to bring it out.

No art skills are needed! The tool is called Interactive Drawing Therapy, and uses simple crayons to learn a tool they can take home.

Workshops are held at the Draw It Out studio - Level 2, 105 High street.

Liability disclaimer:
Draw It Out offers creativity for wellbeing. It is not intended as therapy nor does it replace counselling, therapy, or any other medical or clinical professional help. Participants understand it is their own responsibility to manage their mental and physical health during sessions, to opt in and out of activities as they see fit, and make the facilitator aware if further support is required.

events.long_description:
Be among the first to see THE WEED EATERS, the darkly comic, award-winning film already hailed as "the most fun I’ve had at the movies in a long time” (The Spinoff).

As part of THE BIG FORKING TOUR of Aotearoa, we’ll be showing a special advance screening of the film, followed by a live Q&A. Hear more from the creators and cast at this special screening, part of an unmissable 21-stop tour ahead of the movie’s hotly anticipated April 30 theatrical release.

From Tāmaki Makaurau Auckland to Murihiku Southland, Callum Devlin (director/producer), Finnius Teppett (scriptwriter/producer), and actor-producers Alice May Connolly and Annabel Kean will light up cinema screens with their debut feature, which follows four millennial slackers on a secluded New Year’s getaway.

Stumbling across their reclusive host’s long-forgotten stash of ancient cannabis, their holiday high takes a nightmare turn into accidental cannibalism… Soon the unsuspecting stoners’ friendships, survival skills, and dietary preferences are put to the test in a film that wickedly combines the deadly and the deadpan.

It promises to be a memorable night with prizes, surprises and a 6-foot fork! Book now - and see it with some of your best buds.

Classified R13 Violence, offensive language, drug use & content that may disturb.

events.id: Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington-Opera House-All Events In
events.name: Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington
events.venue: Opera House
events.long_description:
Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington
Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening Announces New Zealand as part of the World Tour Celebrating the 50th Anniversary of Physical Graffiti this April 2026

Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening/JBLZE is announcing the final dates to Celebrate the 50th Anniversary of the Led Zeppelin classic, Physical Graffiti and what better way to wrap up the World Tour than with dates down under! The landmark tour will let fans experience classics such as “Kashmir” and “The Wanton Song” live alongside deeper cuts like “In The Light” and “Boogie With Stu.” Along with playing the iconic album in its entirety, other Led Zeppelin favourites including “Good Times Bad Times,” “Whole Lotta Love” and of course “Stairway To Heaven” find their way into the setlist.

“For me, this isn’t just an album—it’s the album,” declares Jason Bonham. “Physical Graffiti has always been my ultimate Led Zeppelin record, and I set out to honour its 50th Anniversary with 50 unforgettable shows. That vision now looks more like 80 shows! My last tour of Australia and New Zealand was nothing short of electrifying, and I know the April tour will raise the bar even higher. I’m counting down the days until JBLZE share our final powerful, celebration of Physical Graffiti together.”

events.long_description:
Our first two sessions of EAT THE FILM | JURASSIC PARK sold out super quick. Should we add a third screening?
Register your interest here!
Eat the Film
Designed to showcase the incredibly creative and ever-changing menu of our playful, fine dining establishment CoCo At The Roxy. EAT THE FILM provides film and food fans with a cult classic film and delicious seasonal and thematic dishes* designed to arrive in-theatre, perfectly timed to specific scenes.
All tickets include several themed dishes and drinks brought to you in the theatre, an experience with more than enough to sate your eyes and appetite.
This menu will be in keeping with the herbivore dinosaurs and will be fully vegetarian.
In Steven Spielberg's massive blockbuster, paleontologists Alan Grant (Sam Neill) and Ellie Sattler (Laura Dern) and mathematician Ian Malcolm (Jeff Goldblum) are among a select group chosen to tour an island theme park populated by dinosaurs created from prehistoric DNA. While the park's mastermind, billionaire John Hammond (Richard Attenborough), assures everyone that the facility is safe, they find out otherwise when various ferocious predators break free and go on the hunt.
Schedule:

6:00pm – Themed pre-show upstairs in the Grand Lobby
6:20pm – Cinema doors open
6:30pm – Quick intro
6:35pm – Feature begins
When purchased tickets either select ETF - Alcohol for the Cocktail option or ETF - Non-Alc for the Mocktail version of drinks.
*No dietaries catered for.
Presented in 4K

events.id: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)-17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
events.venue: 17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
Two titans of the turntables. One night only.
Three-time DMC World Champion DJ QBert is widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential DJs of all time. His dominance at the DMC World Championships in the early ’90s redefined what was possible with two turntables and he is widely credited with transforming the turntable from a device used to play records into a respected, expressive musical instrument. Decades later, his innovation, technical mastery and cultural impact remain unmatched. Seeing QBert live is not just a show, it is witnessing the evolution of hip hop craft in real time.
Sharing the bill is Aotearoa’s own 2002 New Zealand DMC Champion Alphabethead, a shining star of experimental hip hop and razor-sharp turntablism. "With a career spanning international tours, acclaimed projects both solo and in bands Bad Taste and The All Seeing Hand, Alphabethead represents the very best of New Zealand DJ culture.
This is a rare meeting of eras, styles and legacies at the highest level of turntablism. Miss it, and you will be hearing about it for years to come.

events.id: Cameraless Photography & Artist's Book - Creative Arts Holiday Program-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cameraless Photography & Artist's Book - Creative Arts Holiday Program
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore the historic photographic process of the cyanotype, and stitch your prints into a unique, handmade artist’s notebook.

In this three-day workshop, you’ll explore the beautiful 19th-century photographic process of the cyanotype (often called a blueprint), known for its striking Prussian blue tones. In its earliest form, the cyanotype was used to record botanical specimens. On day one of our workshop, you’ll collect plants or foliage from the garden (or bring your own) to create delicate contact prints on Japanese rice paper and watercolour paper. On day two, we’ll plan your book structure and complete your cyanotypes. On day three, we’ll trim your prints and stitch or construct them into a handmade notebook of your own design.

Course dates are Wed-Fri, 15-17 April, 10 am - 2 pm daily. All materials provided.

Recommended age for this course is tweens & teens ages 10 and above.

Ronald McDonald House Variety Concert
TINA CROSS IS BACK!

Back by popular demand! The Kāpiti Variety Concert returns to Southward Theatre on Wednesday 15 April 2026, with shows at 2.30 pm and 7.00 pm.

This lively showcase of local talent will raise vital funds for Ronald McDonald House Charities, which supports families when their child is in a hospital away from home.

events.id: Botanical Insights talk - Women in Botany-Treehouse Visitor Centre, 101 Glenmore St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Botanical Insights talk - Women in Botany
events.venue: Treehouse Visitor Centre, 101 Glenmore St, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this talk the accomplishments of a selection of brilliant, forcefully independent-minded, women botanists of the 18th, 19th and 20th centuries will be reviewed. Their work had far-reaching benefits on, amongst other things, the economic development of their countries, conservation management and higher education. One of the ‘Sisterhood’ received the Nobel Prize. Others gained high office in various learned societies and institutions. Several New Zealand botanists will be highlighted.

Located in the Treehouse Seminar Room, Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā.
Free of charge. You’re welcome to bring along your own ‘brown bag’ lunch to enjoy during the talk.

The speaker is Reg Harris, a volunteer guide [botany] at Wellington Botanic Garden.
Reg delivers botany lectures for diverse audiences, delving into plant interactions, adaptive radiation, tropisms, and more. As a dedicated volunteer guide at Wellington Botanic Garden (WBG), he pioneered collaborations between WBG and Victoria University, organising annual field labs for biology students.

events.id: NZ Tunnelling Company 110th Commemorations-Pukeahu National War Memorial Park, Buckle St, Mt Cook, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: NZ Tunnelling Company 110th Commemorations
events.venue: Pukeahu National War Memorial Park, Buckle St, Mt Cook, Wellington
events.long_description:
Members of the pubic are warmly invited to attend a special commemorative event as descendants of the First World War New Zealand Tunnelling Company are gathering at Pukeahu National War Memorial Park above the Arras Tunnel. 2026 is the 110th anniversary since the Company arrived on the Westen Front. The Tunnelling Company was the first New Zealand unit to arrive on the Front. Their duties included working underground at Arras in northern France. Today, the Arras Tunnel in Wellington is a visible reminder of the story of those who served underground.

We are gathering in the nation’s capital, in a space of national significance, to pay appropriate respect to a group of men whose service and contribution have often not been understood. This will be a very special moment as descendants and supporters gather along with others who share their stories. We are honoured to have RNZE providing practical support and assistance on the day. This event has been made possible by partial funding from Veterans Affairs and many volunteers assisting at their own expense. Descendants are also helping with ongoing costs.

The commemoration is shaping up to be a special cultural blend of Aotearoa New Zealand, including Tangata Whenua, the Seyip Association representing a Tunneller of Chinese descent, as well as Cook Islands, French and Tahitian influences. They all share the Tunnelling Company story, with representatives from these groups participating. Over 50 descendants representing more than 20 Tunnellers’ families will be joining us, travelling from as far afield as Kaitaia and Invercargill.

We are honoured to have the Mayor of Wellington His Worship Andrew Little welcome us to the capital, followed by our keynote speaker Sir Jerry Mateparae GNZM, QSO, KStJ. The French Ambassador to New Zealand H.E. Laurence Beau and RNZE: Colonel Commandant Col. Paul Curry will also speak.

A special guest at the commemoration is Hon. Chris Finlayson KC. Chris was instrumental in the naming of the Arras Tunnel in 2014. Other distinguished guests who will join us include official representatives from the Cook Islands, the United Kingdom, Australia and France. Also attending will be past and present military representatives including Masterton and Wellington RSAs, the Sappers Association NZ, with five historical military vehicles also taking part to help us honour the ASC who were attached to the Tunnellers. Along with the Wellington Mayor, one other Mayor from the North Island and one from the South Island who share the Tunnellers’ story are also joining us. We will have two Tunnelling Company Chaplains with us on the day. Descendant Mons. Bernard Kiely and Waihi Tunnelling Company Chaplain Rev. Christina Syratt.

An important part of this commemoration is the reading out of a Roll of Honour by descendants and supporters. A descendant will also read the names on the Roll of Remembrance which recognises Tunnellers who took their own lives after the war. As far as we are aware, this will be the first time a list like this has been read out at Pukeahu.

The service proper begins at 1.00pm. Those not participating in the march are requested to be seated by 12.45pm. At 12.45pm descendants and supporters will march from Buckle Street to Anzac Square, led in by supporting RNZE personnel, a piper, flag bearer and three historic military vehicles. People who have a relationship to the Tunnelling Company story are invited to join descendants in this short march.
events.description:
Members of the pubic are warmly invited to attend a special commemorative event as descendants of the First World War New Zealand Tunnelling Company are gathering at Pukeahu National War Memorial Park above the Arras Tunnel.
Along with the Wellington Mayor, one other Mayor from the North Island and one from the South Island who share the Tunnellers’ story are also joining us.
We will have two Tunnelling Company Chaplains with us on the day.
An important part of this commemoration is the reading out of a Roll of Honour by descendants and supporters.

A Wine & Food Pairing Night at Cuba St Tavern

Join us at Cuba St Tavern for Perfect Pairs - a special evening celebrating great wine, good food and the people around the table.

For one night only, we’ll be pouring five standout wines from around the world, each paired with a dish designed by our kitchen to bring out the best in every glass.

Guiding the evening is Brandon Nash, from Importer and Distributor Dhall & Nash Fine Wines, who will introduce each wine throughout the night and share a little about the regions, producers and stories behind the pours.

From English sparkling to Central Otago Pinot Noir, this lineup takes guests across some of the world’s most exciting wine regions - all enjoyed in the relaxed, social atmosphere of the Tavern.

Expect a night of great wine, thoughtful plates and good company in the heart of Cuba Street.

events.id: The Weed Eaters - Big Forking Tour-Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Weed Eaters - Big Forking Tour
events.venue: Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Be among the first to see THE WEED EATERS, the darkly comic, award-winning film already hailed as "the most fun I’ve had at the movies in a long time” (The Spinoff).

As part of THE BIG FORKING TOUR of Aotearoa, we’ll be showing a special advance screening of the film, followed by a live Q&A. Hear more from the creators and cast at this special screening, part of an unmissable 21-stop tour ahead of the movie’s hotly anticipated April 30 theatrical release.

From Tāmaki Makaurau Auckland to Murihiku Southland, Callum Devlin (director/producer), Finnius Teppett (scriptwriter/producer), and actor-producers Alice May Connolly and Annabel Kean will light up cinema screens with their debut feature, which follows four millennial slackers on a secluded New Year’s getaway.

Stumbling across their reclusive host’s long-forgotten stash of ancient cannabis, their holiday high takes a nightmare turn into accidental cannibalism… Soon the unsuspecting stoners’ friendships, survival skills, and dietary preferences are put to the test in a film that wickedly combines the deadly and the deadpan.

It promises to be a memorable night with prizes, surprises and a 6-foot fork! Book now - and see it with some of your best buds.

Classified R13 Violence, offensive language, drug use & content that may disturb.

events.id: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
Two titans of the turntables. One night only.

Three-time DMC World Champion DJ QBert is widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential DJs of all time. His dominance at the DMC World Championships in the early ’90s redefined what was possible with two turntables and he is widely credited with transforming the turntable from a device used to play records into a respected, expressive musical instrument. Decades later, his innovation, technical mastery and cultural impact remain unmatched. Seeing QBert live is not just a show, it is witnessing the evolution of hip hop craft in real time.

Sharing the bill is Aotearoa’s own 2002 New Zealand DMC Champion Alphabethead, a shining star of experimental hip hop and razor-sharp turntablism. "With a career spanning international tours, acclaimed projects both solo and in bands Bad Taste and The All Seeing Hand, Alphabethead represents the very best of New Zealand DJ culture.

This is a rare meeting of eras, styles and legacies at the highest level of turntablism. Miss it, and you will be hearing about it for years to come.

events.id: SIKA Sound Journey - KAPITI-Raumati South Memorial Hall. Tennis Court Road, Raumati South.,43 Tennis Court Rd, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: SIKA Sound Journey - KAPITI
events.venue: Raumati South Memorial Hall. Tennis Court Road, Raumati South.,43 Tennis Court Rd, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
SIKA Sound Journey - KAPITI
SIKA Sound Journey - 'Between the Worlds'
Something between an ancient ceremony and a movie soundtrack.

Please bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket. Be prepared to drop into a deep space.
Door sales will be available if there is space. Advanced booking is recommended.

Between the Worlds is an evening Sound Journey shaped by over thirty years of deep ceremonial and immersive sound practice. Blending primal rhythm, atmospheric voice, deep didgeridoo, and field recordings, the journey unfolds organically — shifting from dynamic, embodied energy into vast, cinematic stillness.

This is not passive listening.
It is an experience to be entered.

As sound layers, dissolve, and reform, participants are guided into a liminal space where time softens and inner landscapes awaken. The journey becomes a passage — one that listeners don’t just hear, but inhabit.

Guided by the myth of the Wandering Forest Spirit, Between the Worlds invites a reconnection with self, with nature, and with something older than language. Ceremonial, immersive, and deeply evocative, this is an evening to slow down, listen deeply, and cross into the unseen.
It's like a soundtrack that is crafted to go with your life.

Sika's Sound Journeys are truly unique. They are sonic explorations that feature didgeridoos, drums, flutes, voice and orchestral elements. A collection of foraged sounds from objects he has recorded in nature, add even more textures. Many of his sounds are completely unrecognisable and abstract. Immersed in this cocoon of abstract sound, we can find ourselves in the most surprising places in our mind. In this timeless realm, beyond the normal limitations of rational reality, anything is possible.
An epic adventure deep inside yourself.
Journey to worlds within worlds on incredible sound waves.

Sika creates a safe space to relax deeply and connect with our inner guidance. This Sound Journey begins and ends with a short guided visualisation.
There will be two journeys with an interval. Most people choose to lie down, so bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket and get really comfortable.
A journal is also recommended.

events.name: For a Just Society
events.venue: Rutherford House, 33 Bunny St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Setting about creating an Aotearoa New Zealand that is built on notions of comprehensive, holistic justice, we need to re-imagine how an Aotearoa with a future would look like. There are concepts emerging internationally and nationally that will help us develop a framework for a society built on equity and justice for all.

with Tayla Forward, Boyd Scirkovich, Marilyn Waring, James Belich, Meihana Durie, Carwyn Jones, Gareth Hughes, Frances Hancock, Pania Newton, Bianca Elkington, Clive Aspin, Heather Came, Fleur Fitzsymonds, Rebecca Kiddle and more.

events.id: Joanne McNally — Pinotphile-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Joanne McNally — Pinotphile
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
She’s a comedian boasting stage, television, and podcast success. Joanne McNally heads to The Opera House in Wellington for a new stage show. Pinotphile displays the Irish comic’s wry wit and sense of the absurd. Hailed by Variety as One to Watch, McNally has been keeping herself busy since her last tour by ruining men’s lives (and her own). For Pinophile, she’s still single, still unfertilised, and laden with irreverent, ridiculous and hilarious war stories. Join Joanne for a wild ride as she navigates situationships, revenge, frenemies and rejection. The Irish Examiner describes her trademark style as ‘joyously nuts’. 
You may have come across Joanne as a co-host of the award-winning podcast My Therapist Ghosted Me. She’s also had riotous appearances on Taskmaster, The Jonathan Ross Show and The Big Fat Quiz of Everything.

events.name: 150 Years of Rugby - Wellington College
events.venue: Wellington College (New Zealand),Centennial Memorial, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
150 Years of Rugby - Wellington College
🏉 150 Years Since the First Intercollegiate Rugby Match – Nelson Old Boys, This Is Ours Too! 🔵⚪

In April 2026, Wellington College and Nelson College will come together to celebrate 150 years since the first intercollegiate rugby match in the world — a milestone in the proud history of both schools.

As part of the celebrations in Wellington, we want to make sure Nelson College is strongly represented — and that starts with our own event:

🔵 𝗡𝗲𝗹𝘀𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝗢𝗹𝗱 𝗕𝗼𝘆𝘀’ 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 – 𝗪𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘁𝗼𝗻 𝗕𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗵 𝗙𝘂𝗻𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻

📍 Backbencher GastroPub, Wellington
📅 Friday 17 April 2026
⏰ 5.00pm – 9.00pm

Hosted by the Nelson College Old Boys' Association Wellington Branch, this dedicated Nelson College gathering is a fantastic opportunity to reconnect, share stories, and support our 1st XV ahead of the big match.

Bring your old team. Rally your year group. Spread the word.
These milestone weekends are about more than rugby — they’re about camaraderie and celebrating what it means to be Nelson College men.
We welcome all Nelson College supporters, parents, Old Boys, partners and Nelson College Old Girls!

🎟️ Cost: Free
(Registration encouraged for catering purposes.)
👉 Register here: https://community.nelson.school.nz/WCRugby150

𝗢𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝟭𝟱𝟬𝘁𝗵 𝗖𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗯𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 – 𝗛𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗯𝘆 𝗪𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘁𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲

Wellington College is hosting a superb three-day programme of events across 16–18 April 2026, and Old Boys are warmly encouraged to take part:

⛳ 150th Anniversary Golf Day
📅 Thursday 16 April 2026
A premium team-of-four event including lunch, dinner, and competitions. Play alongside rugby greats such as Christian Cullen, Ian Kirkpatrick, Victor Vito, and Jason Eaton.
🎟️ Register / Purchase tickets: https://www.sporty.co.nz/viewform/423419

💼 Rugby Legends Lunch
📅 Friday 17 April 2026
An afternoon of lunch, beverages, entertainment, and exclusive auction moments. Hear from:
🏉 Marc Ellis
🏉 Dane Coles
🏉 Sir Graham Henry
🎟️ Register / Purchase tickets: https://www.sporty.co.nz/viewform/423419

🏆 150th Anniversary Match Day
📅 Saturday 18 April 2026 - Kick Off 1.30pm
The historic anniversary match between Wellington College and Nelson College, played at the Wellington College grounds — a day not to be missed.
🎟️ Match information & updates: https://www.wellington-college.school.nz/wcrugby150/
If you can't make it in person, SKYTV will be covering the game.

✈️ Travelling to the capital?

A big thank you to Rydges Hotels & Resorts for supporting the celebrations with a 20% accommodation discount for attendees.

🏨 Rydges Wellington City
RZWELL
https://www.rydges.com/private-page/wellington-college-150th-rugby-anniversary-2/

🏨 Rydges Wellington Airport
RZWEAP
https://www.rydges.com/private-page/wellington-college-150th-rugby-anniversary/

Let’s show up in numbers.
Let’s make some noise.
Let’s celebrate 150 years of history — together. 🔵🏉

events.id: Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development-Maungaraki School,Dowse Drive, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development
events.venue: Maungaraki School,Dowse Drive, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development
Teaching has never been more complex or more important. Every classroom is a dynamic mix of neurodiversity, cultural identity, language, trauma, and rapid societal change. Educators are being asked to do more, with less - often caught between policy pressures and what they know is right for children.

This new national workshop from Longworth Education acknowledges that complexity and offers practical, evidence-informed strategies for navigating it.

This is not a one-size-fits-all model. Instead, we explore what it means to teach in ways that are developmentally responsive, neuro-affirming, trauma-aware and culturally sustaining, all while managing the daily realities of the classroom.

Designed for teachers, leaders, SENCOs, learning support staff and anyone working in education, this workshop will support you to:

Better understand the developmental, neurological and relational needs that shape student behaviour and learning

Recognise and respond to complexity, without becoming overwhelmed by it

Strengthen inclusive, identity-affirming practices that work in diverse, real-world classrooms

Learn practical ways to hold onto both rigour and humanity in your teaching

Leave with tools, frameworks and language you can use the very next day

Whether you're new to this way of thinking or looking to deepen your practice, this workshop will meet you where you are and support you to move forward with confidence, clarity and care.

Join us as we mark ten years of Longworth Education’s commitment to child-centred pedagogy and celebrate our recognition as one of HundrED’s Top 100 Global Education Innovations for 2026 and Aotearoa's leading expert in play pedagogy across the school sector.
events.description:
Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development Teaching has never been more complex or more important.
Every classroom is a dynamic mix of neurodiversity, cultural identity, language, trauma, and rapid societal change.
Educators are being asked to do more, with less - often caught between policy pressures and what they know is right for children.
This new national workshop from Longworth Education acknowledges that complexity and offers practical, evidence-informed strategies for navigating it.
This is not a one-size-fits-all model.

events.long_description:
Hutt City Night Market - 16 April 2026
Experience the sights, smells, and flavors of a traditional night market right in the heart of Lower Hutt this autumn. 🍂

The Hutt City Night Market is an international celebration of food, bringing together a delicious mix of cuisines from around the world.

Stroll through bustling stalls, grab a cozy bite (or three), bring your friends and family, stay for a picnic, and enjoy the crisp autumn air in a vibrant community setting.

🕓 4pm – 8pm
🌧️ Every Thursday night, rain or shine (unless there is a thunderstorm or severe weather, in which case the market will be cancelled)
📍 Dowse Square, 45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt (opposite HCC building and next to Dowse Museum)

The food trucks attending each week will be announced by Tuesday, so keep an eye out to see if your favorite is rolling in!

👉 Like and follow Wellington Street Eats on Facebook & Instagram for all updates:
@wellingtonstreeteats

See you there under the autumn skies! 🍁
