events.id: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Orange County post-hardcore trailblazers Saosin will return to Australia in April 2026 for a national headline tour, celebrating 20 years of their iconic self-titled album - playing the album in full as well as the hits. Joining them across all dates is special guests and post-hardcore legends Senses Fail. Tickets on sale Thursday 26th February 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Saosin quickly emerged as a powerful voice in the post-hardcore and alternative rock scenes, gaining immediate underground acclaim from the get-go. Following a shift in vocalists, Cove Reber joined the band and fronted their breakout self-titled album in 2005, which featured genre-defining tracks like "Voices" and "You’re Not Alone". The album's success propelled Saosin into mainstream recognition, earning them a dedicated global fanbase and cementing their status as one of the most influential bands of their era.
Saosin were first noticed by New Zealand fans at the Taste of Chaos tour at St James in 2006 alongside acts Taking Back Sunday, Underoath, Anti-Flag, Thursday and Senses Fail. They continued to come back to these shores in 2010 with Bless the Fall for one Auckland show
Now, 20 years on they’ll perform their self-titled album in full across New Zealand alongside their biggest hits next April.
Since reuniting with vocalist Cove Reber in 2024, Saosin has reignited the fervent energy that defined their most influential years. With Reber back at the helm, Saosin is redefining their future. The chemistry among the original lineup has sparked creative momentum, leading to new material, revitalized performances, and expanded vision. The band is once again a driving force in post-hardcore, proving that while time may pass, authenticity and connection endure. This chapter in Saosin’s journey is both a homecoming and a launching point for what’s next.

Supporting Saosin on this special run is Senses Fail, These guys are also no strangers to New Zealand having been here for the Taste of Chaos tour in 2006.. The band have been called any number of things across their near 25 years as a band – emo, screamo, post-hardcore, metalcore, pop punk. It’s testament to the band’s perpetual evolution as much as it is their steely endurance – a quality which frontman Buddy Nielsen has required bottomless reserves of along the ride. It’s through the songs of Senses Fail that Nielsen has forever made sense of and peace with life’s highs and lows; a journey that began in the leafy suburbs of Ridgewood, New Jersey with the iconic Gold-selling debut album Let It Enfold You, and which has since seen Senses Fail become one of rock music’s most cherished, celebrated names.

Bringing together two giants of their genre, don't miss Saosin and Senses Fail do what they do best - tickets available Thursday 26th February 2026 at 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Tickets on sale.
Saosin 2026 Tour Dates
Sat 18th Apr - Studio The Venue (Auckland)
Sun 19th April - San Fran (Wellington)
Mon 20th April Sydenham Underpass (Christchurch)

events.id: Surf Life's Problems-Kadampa Meditation Centre Wellington, 2 Ardross Avenue, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Surf Life's Problems
events.venue: Kadampa Meditation Centre Wellington, 2 Ardross Avenue, Wellington
events.long_description:
About the Half-Day Course

Learn how to ride the waves of difficulties and problems that are a part of everyday life and see how it is possible to remain calm and happy even in the most difficult of circumstances.

Life is a constant flow of waves. Some are gentle. Others crash in without warning. Just as one problem subsides, another arises. Despite our best efforts, we can feel tossed around, our mood dictated by whatever comes next. Sometimes change happens so quickly or unpredictably that it leaves us feeling powerless and out of control.

What if you could remain calm, steady and confident no matter what arises?
In this transformative half-day course, we will explore practical meditations that build genuine mental strength and resilience. You will learn how to respond to life’s challenges with flexibility instead of fear, clarity instead of confusion, and purpose instead of pressure.

Kapiti Wedding & Events Expo 2026
Get inspired at the Kapiti Wedding & Events Expo 2026, the Kapiti Coast’s must-visit event for anyone planning a wedding, celebration, or special event.

Join us Sunday 19 April, 10am–3pm at Paraparaumu Memorial Hall and explore 40+ of the region’s best wedding and event professionals all in one place. From stunning florals and photographers to venues, celebrants, entertainment, catering, and styling inspiration, you’ll find everything you need to bring your event to life.

Your FREE ticket also unlocks special offers around the Kapiti Coast through our Explore Kapiti programme, giving you discounts at local shops, cafes, and experiences during your visit.

Come along, meet the creatives behind Kapiti’s most memorable events, and start planning with confidence.

Register now for your FREE tickets and join us for a day of inspiration, ideas, and celebration.

events.id: Textured Bracelets - Taster-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Textured Bracelets - Taster
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This lively, hands-on workshop is designed for complete beginners, but it’s also a great opportunity for more experienced students to enjoy a creative session making their own unique bangle!

Everyone will leave with a beautifully finished bracelet of their own preferred texture.

We’ll begin by experimenting on a sample strip of copper, where you’ll practice creating textures using hammers, punches and stamps. Once you’re confident you’ll move on to your main project: a 3mm wide flat strip bracelet in either sterling silver or copper, crafted entirely by you from start to finish. Along the way, you’ll learn essential jewellery making skills including measuring, texturing, soldering, shaping, and finishing.

Students are free to personalise their bangle with textures or stamped designs. This could be a simple hammered finish, decorative patterns, or meaningful words, names, dates, or poetry lines - making your bracelet truly one of a kind.
Beginners will work with the 3mm strip provided, ensuring a rewarding project that’s manageable to complete. Wider metal is much harder to form and solder, so we are learning to walk before we can run ;)

Copper is included in the course fee, if you wish to work in silver you can purchase it on the day.

Experienced students may choose to challenge themselves by making a wider bracelet from flat strip or by creating additional bangles in silver wire. (Please note: extra silver for wider bangles is available to purchase at the workshop for experienced students only, and let us know if you wish to go wider so we can make sure we have the strip in stock).

This workshop is a taster class: perfect for anyone seeking a fun, creative experience with a beautiful result, or for those looking to dip their toes into jewellery making and build foundational skills.

events.id: Jewellery-making in 4 weeks - Sunday Afternoon block, Mixed-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making in 4 weeks - Sunday Afternoon block, Mixed
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This Sunday afternoon block is a short course on jewellery-making. It starts Sunday 19 April and runs for 4 weeks till Sunday 10 May, 2pm - 5pm.

Beginners will practice the basic techniques in copper or brass and then move on to working in silver. An excellent first project for beginners in this class is to create a simple textured ring band - but once you have the basics down, it is fun to create earrings, pendants or other simple items. Learning to cut, heat, form and texture metal - to solder using a jeweller's torch and silver solder, and finally to polish your work up to a beautiful finish: these classes are super satisfying.

These blocks are also a nice way for existing students or more advanced makers to work on more challenging projects and join friendly classes in our well-equipped studio!

All materials you need can be purchased in class - beginners should expect to use around $50 - $70 of metals in this block.

This block is great for teens and kids over 12 or so. NOTE: Anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

Our booking system asks for full payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time please email us to arrange this ;)

events.name: Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
events.venue: Duncan's Brewing Company,4B Sheffield Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
Join us for Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze, where you can sip, glaze and decorate your own custom Tumbler

About this Event


Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
Join us in person for a fun and laid-back afternoon of creativity and good vibes. Whether you're a pottery pro or just starting out, this social is all about raising a glass and enjoying some creativity together. Bring your friends, get your your creativety flowing, and let's make some unique pieces while sharing laughs and good company. Don't miss out on this awesome chance to connect and create!
You'll be glazing your very own a handmade tumbler made my Mud + Woman Pottery.





Pottery Class Terms & Conditions


Bookings: All class bookings are non-refundable 7 days prior to event.
Changes & credits: If something comes up and you can’t make it, just reach out — we’re happy to offer a voucher so you can join another class instead.
The nature of clay: Working with clay is a beautiful but imperfect process. Each piece goes through drying and firing, and sometimes cracking, warping, or breakage can happen despite our best care. It’s all part of the magic (and mystery) of pottery.
Finished pieces: While we do everything we can to look after your creations, we’re unable to guarantee the final result or offer refunds or remakes for pieces that don’t make it through the process.

events.id: Songwriters’ Open Mic at Moon-Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Songwriters’ Open Mic at Moon
events.venue: Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Songwriters’ Open Mic at Moon

The first of the 2026 series of Songwriters’ Open Mic sessions at Moon will be on Sunday April 19 kicking off shortly after the bar opens at 4pm. There will be sessions each month through until November, on the afternoons preceding the Songwriters' Showcase concerts scheduled for the evenings.

These sessions are solely for folk to perform their own original songs. So what’s the drill? Turn up, make yourself known to whoever appears to be in charge, and get your name on the list. Performances will then be in the order called by the organisers - no pre-booking of times, or reserving spots. Sets will be either 2 or 3 songs depending on the level of demand.

So regardless whether you are a “newby” taking your first steps beyond your bedroom, or an “old hand” wanting to test drive new material, or just get some “open wicket practice”, this is the open mic for you. If you fancy getting to play one of the Showcase concerts this is the right place to audition. And it's not just a chance to perform but to meet and get to know other songwriters around town.

Performers at the open mic get entry to the following Songwriters show for $10 (discounted from the regular punters price of $15.

The Poneke Songwriters Collective thanks Moon Bar for supporting these events and continuing to support the local music community

As the cool kids say “be there or be quadrilateral”.

Pic - Random “guy with a guitar”

events.id: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran-San Fran,Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran
events.venue: San Fran,Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran
Orange County post-hardcore trailblazers Saosin will return to Australia in April 2026 for a national headline tour, celebrating 20 years of their iconic self-titled album - playing the album in full as well as the hits. Joining them across all dates is special guests and post-hardcore legends Senses Fail. Tickets on sale Thursday 26th February 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Saosin quickly emerged as a powerful voice in the post-hardcore and alternative rock scenes, gaining immediate underground acclaim from the get-go. Following a shift in vocalists, Cove Reber joined the band and fronted their breakout self-titled album in 2005, which featured genre-defining tracks like "Voices" and "You’re Not Alone". The album's success propelled Saosin into mainstream recognition, earning them a dedicated global fanbase and cementing their status as one of the most influential bands of their era.
Saosin were first noticed by New Zealand fans at the Taste of Chaos tour at St James in 2006 alongside acts Taking Back Sunday, Underoath, Anti-Flag, Thursday and Senses Fail. They continued to come back to these shores in 2010 with Bless the Fall for one Auckland show
Now, 20 years on they’ll perform their self-titled album in full across New Zealand alongside their biggest hits next April.
Since reuniting with vocalist Cove Reber in 2024, Saosin has reignited the fervent energy that defined their most influential years. With Reber back at the helm, Saosin is redefining their future. The chemistry among the original lineup has sparked creative momentum, leading to new material, revitalized performances, and expanded vision. The band is once again a driving force in post-hardcore, proving that while time may pass, authenticity and connection endure. This chapter in Saosin’s journey is both a homecoming and a launching point for what’s next.


Supporting Saosin on this special run is Senses Fail, These guys are also no strangers to New Zealand having been here for the Taste of Chaos tour in 2006.. The band have been called any number of things across their near 25 years as a band – emo, screamo, post-hardcore, metalcore, pop punk. It’s testament to the band’s perpetual evolution as much as it is their steely endurance – a quality which frontman Buddy Nielsen has required bottomless reserves of along the ride. It’s through the songs of Senses Fail that Nielsen has forever made sense of and peace with life’s highs and lows; a journey that began in the leafy suburbs of Ridgewood, New Jersey with the iconic Gold-selling debut album Let It Enfold You, and which has since seen Senses Fail become one of rock music’s most cherished, celebrated names.


Bringing together two giants of their genre, don't miss Saosin and Senses Fail do what they do best - tickets available Thursday 26th February 2026 at 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Tickets on sale.
Saosin 2026 Tour Dates
Sat 18th Apr - Studio The Venue (Auckland)
Sun 19th April - San Fran (Wellington)
Mon 20th April Sydenham Underpass (Christchurch)

WELLINGTON - The Arborist Rooftop Bar
Monthly Topic: The Story Behind the Brand
Hosted by:
This month, we’re diving into The Story Behind the Brand– because people don’t just connect with what you do, they connect with why you do it. Your journey, your lessons, your turning points– that’s where real connection lives. Throughout April, we’ll explore how sharing the human behind your business builds trust, deepens relationships, and turns casual followers into genuine community. Come ready to own your story, speak with confidence, and show up as more than just a business owner– but as the woman behind the brand.
Want to get your ticket for FREE?

now and become part of a wonderful community of women in business who are ready to build their dream life and business.

Our groups value the power of connection, and we believe that together, we can achieve more than we ever could alone. That's why we're committed to fostering a supportive community that empowers each member to reach their full potential. Our groups are focused on connection, inspiring stories, celebrating each other's successes and sharing our vulnerable moments.

events.id: Jewellery-making On Monday Nights - 6 Weeks Block, Level 2+-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making On Monday Nights - 6 Weeks Block, Level 2+
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Our popular weekend block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.

This class is for level 2+ and advanced students. It is not open to beginners. It starts on Mon 20 April and runs for 6 sessions until Mon 8 June, 5.30pm-8pm.

Students must have completed at least two 4-week blocks or a 6-week fabrication block. If you are unsure of your level because you may have done a few different shorter classes, please contact us and we can advise you.

Students will be supported in further developing their jewellery-making skills in the areas that interest them.

Level 2 students learn to solder more competently on their own using chainmaking as a way to develop their fine soldering skills. They may create a simple stone setting or ring alongside this challenge project so they have something to hone their skills that they are familiar with, as well as taking on a new skill. Their project could be a pendant on a handmade chain, a charm bracelet or a more complex bezel setting - for example a teardrop or soft square stone to take on bezels with corners!

Level 3 and above students are encouraged to work on their design skills and try new and challenging techniques.

This class fee does NOT include materials for any student. All metals and stones as well as quality findings and chains are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time please email us to arrange this ;)

events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Tawa, Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.id: Nōku te Ao Wellington Regional Wānanga-Te Wharewaka o Poneke,2 Taranaki Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Nōku te Ao Wellington Regional Wānanga
events.venue: Te Wharewaka o Poneke,2 Taranaki Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Nōku te Ao Wellington Regional Wānanga
Nōku te Ao Wellington Regional Wānanga unites voices to drive action against mental distress discrimination in Aotearoa.

About this Event


Overview
Free to attend – registration essential
Limited spots
Morning tea and lunch provided
Following the success of the Nōku te Ao Symposium in Auckland last year, the Regional Wānanga series He reo tini, he wawata kotahi – Many voices, one goal to end mental distress discrimination continues the momentum to create lasting change for people living with mental distress in Aotearoa.
As the final event in a series of four across the motu, the Nōku te Ao Wellington Regional Wānanga will bring together systems change leaders, mental health professionals, and lived experience advocates to focus on practical actions and tools to end mental distress discrimination at every level.
Join us for a day of powerful kōrero, practical learning, and collective action.



Keynote Speakers
We are honoured to welcome Kerri Butler and Paul Rangiwahia as our keynote speakers.
Kerri Butler’s keynote will explore the stigma and discrimination experienced by people living with mental illness or distress – and how each of us can play a role in challenging it. She’ll share practical tools and everyday actions to help foster greater social inclusion, and highlight why it’s so important to keep this social movement alive, visible, and growing.
In his keynote, Paul Rangiwahia will explore the power of art in normalising mental health conversations, and approaches to wellbeing that focus on small, repeatable actions rather than big motivation or hype.



Workshops
The day will include two interactive workshops. The first workshop, led by John Kingi and Heemi Kapa-Kingi from Soften Up Bro, will challenge the perception that experiencing mental distress is a sign of weakness. Through storytelling and reflection, this workshop will explore how vulnerability can strengthen whānau and communities, and how speaking honestly about mental distress can deepen connection and a sense of identity.
The second workshop will be spearheaded by Hannah Whittaker-Komatsu. This workshop will examine how lived experience functions as a continuous loop of influence, shaping the actions we take to create change for our whānau and communities. Together, we’ll reflect, share, and celebrate the many ways we collectively shape our world, moment by moment.



What you’ll gain
Evidence-based tools to challenge mental distress stigma and discrimination in your workplace and community
Connections with sector leaders and lived experience advocates across Wellington and the wider region.
Inspiration and practical steps to help create lasting change.



About Nōku te Ao
Nōku te Ao is a public awareness movement dedicated to increasing social inclusion and ending discrimination against people with lived experience of mental illness or distress.
Grounded in Te Tiriti o Waitangi and led by tāngata whaiora (people seeking wellness), Nōku te Ao works with lived experience communities, whānau, and kaimahi across Aotearoa – to end stigma and discrimination in homes, schools, workplaces, hospitals, and the media.

events.id: Tony Lee, solo piano-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Tony Lee, solo piano
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for a recital of Scriabin and Rachmaninoff given by Australian pianist Tony Lee.

Scriabin -

Prelude Op. 11, No. I in C major
Prelude Op. 17 No. 5 in F Minor
Prelude Op. 16 No. 1 in B major
Etude Op. 2 no. 1 in C# minor

Rachmaninov -
Sonata No. 1 in D minor, Op. 28

Sydney-born pianist and educator Tony Lee has gained a reputation as one of Australia's finest young pianists. He seeks to transmit the essence of music and his understanding of it through his performances and teaching. Tony was an artist in residence at the Chapelle Musicale Reine Elisabeth in Belgium and worked closely with the renowned Portuguese Swiss pianist Maria-João Pires between 2014-2019.

In 2023, Tony served on the Piano Faculty at the Zhejiang Normal University and Emporia State University Undergraduate Joint Program and now holds the position of Artistic Advisor for the Rising Stars Program at the Sydney Conservatorium of Music.

Tony has performed across the Asia Pacific, Europe, the USA and has appeared as soloist with the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra, Brussels Philharmonic Orchestra, National Ballet of China Symphony Orchestra, Shanghai Philharmonic Orchestra, Lanzhou Symphony Orchestra, Chengdu Philharmonic Orchestra, Guangxi Symphony Orchestra, Samara Philharmonic Orchestra, Queensland Symphony Orchestra, Adelaide Symphony Orchestra, Tasmanian Symphony Orchestra, and New Zealand Symphony Orchestra.

events.id: After 5 with Hnry Stadium-Hnry Stadium,105 Waterloo Quay, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: After 5 with Hnry Stadium
events.venue: Hnry Stadium,105 Waterloo Quay, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
After 5 with Hnry Stadium
After 5 with Hnry Stadium

About this Event

Major events are a big part of what keeps Wellington buzzing — from international sport and concerts to the moments that bring the city together. But what does it take to attract and deliver those events, and why do they matter so much for the capital’s economy and vibrancy?

Join us on 22 April for a special After 5 at Hnry Stadium to hear from Warrick Dent, CEO of Hnry Stadium, and James Fuller, CEO and co-founder of Hnry.

They’ll share insights on the role major events play in Wellington’s future, how the stadium works behind the scenes to bring world-class events to the city, and the story behind the Hnry Stadium naming rights partnership — including how partnerships like this help support the stadium and contribute back to the Wellington community.

You’ll also get the chance to step inside the stadium, with a behind-the-scenes look at some of its member, corporate and function spaces. For those interested, there will also be the option to join a short stadium tour after the discussion.

If you’re interested in Wellington’s economic future, major events, or simply want a rare look inside one of the capital’s most important venues, this is an After 5 you won’t want to miss.
events.description:
After 5 with Hnry Stadium After 5 with Hnry Stadium About this Event Major events are a big part of what keeps Wellington buzzing — from international sport and concerts to the moments that bring the city together.
But what does it take to attract and deliver those events, and why do they matter so much for the capital’s economy and vibrancy?
Join us on 22 April for a special After 5 at Hnry Stadium to hear from Warrick Dent, CEO of Hnry Stadium, and James Fuller, CEO and co-founder of Hnry.
They’ll share insights on the role major events play in Wellington’s future, how the stadium works behind the scenes to bring world-class events to the city, and the story behind the Hnry Stadium naming rights partnership — including how partnerships like this help support the stadium and contribute back to the Wellington community.
You’ll also get the chance to step inside the stadium, with a behind-the-scenes look at some of its member, corporate and function spaces.

events.id: The Antarctic Film Festival - Wellington #2-Government Buildings Historic Reserve, 55 Lambton Quay, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Antarctic Film Festival - Wellington #2
events.venue: Government Buildings Historic Reserve, 55 Lambton Quay, Wellington
events.long_description:
An unforgettable evening of storytelling from the coldest place on Earth. The New Zealand Antarctic Society is proud to invite you to a presentation by Ngawai Clendon and a curated selection of captivating short films from the heart of Antarctica.

The films blend stunning documentary footage, curated by award winning filmmaker Anthony Powell, that reveals the continent’s stark beauty, unforgiving conditions, climate change, and penguin antics with stories captured by inventive and funny entries from the Winter International Film Festival of Antarctica’s 48-Hour Challenge, an annual showcase of creativity and resilience from those who winter-over on the Ice.

Before the films, hear from Ngawai Clendon, who will share her journey to the Ross Sea and the historic huts, along with the process and outcomes of that experience. Ngawai is in her third year at Victoria University of Wellington, studying New Zealand Sign Language, Te Reo Māori, Neurodiversity, and Education, with the goal of becoming a teacher who can communicate in all of Aotearoa New Zealand’s official languages while supporting children on the spectrum. Outside of study, she is a competitive marching girl with SWIS, New Zealand’s champion senior team based in Wellington.

events.long_description:
Presented by: Handsome Tours
Handsome Tours are proud to present Sleaford Mods 2026 Australian & New Zealand Tour
This will be Sleaford Mods first time back to Australian and New Zealand soil since May and June of 2023, where they performed at events like Sydney's Vivid LIVE and Hobart's Dark Mofo.
“We took a year off ’cause I needed to get my nails did, and now we’re itching to get back out there and play live”, says Sleaford Mods' Jason Williamson. “We released a new single Megaton last month, it’s an absolute banger so we’re buzzing to play that live for the first time. Who knows, we might have a few more new tracks to drop by then. Either way, it’s been a while since we toured, and we’re gagging for it.”
Sleaford Mods has partnered with PLUS1 to support War Child. $1.15 inc GST from every ticket sold will help War Child to protect, educate, and stand up for the rights of children affected by war.
www.warchild.org.uk

events.long_description:
THUR Wellington - Empower Her Networking - The Story Behind the Brand
Empower Her, female networking group that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences

About this Event

WELLINGTON - The Arborist Rooftop Bar
Monthly Topic: The Story Behind the Brand
Hosted by:
This month, we’re diving into The Story Behind the Brand– because people don’t just connect with what you do, they connect with why you do it. Your journey, your lessons, your turning points– that’s where real connection lives. Throughout April, we’ll explore how sharing the human behind your business builds trust, deepens relationships, and turns casual followers into genuine community. Come ready to own your story, speak with confidence, and show up as more than just a business owner– but as the woman behind the brand.
We recommend bringing a notebook and pen- there will be space for reflection, ideas, and those “lightbulb” moments you won’t want to forget.
Want to get your ticket for FREE?
Are you a woman in business looking for a supportive community to help you connect, grow, and learn?
As an Inner Circle member, you can attend as many of our networking hubs as you like for FREE! This includes our hubs across Auckland, NZ, Australia and the UK. Enjoy unlimited access to our netwokring events and take full advantage of the community and networking opportunities.
Join the Inner Circle to unlock even more benefits:
Monthly Masterclasses: Learn from industry experts on topics that will elevate your business and mindset.
Accountability Calls: Stay on track with our supportive community and achieve your goals.
Personal Branding Opportunities: Create your own masterclasses and shine as an expert.
Exclusive Discounts: Enjoy special rates on Empower Her events and live workshops.
Vibrant Community: Share your journey, get support, and celebrate your successes with empowering women.
now and become part of a wonderful community of women in business who are ready to build their dream life and business.
Empower Her is a global female networking group, that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences.
Whether you're just starting out with new business ideas or have years of experience under your belt, all are welcome! Empower Her provides a safe space where you can share your business with others, connect with like-minded women in business, and prioritise collaboration over competition.
Our groups value the power of connection, and we believe that together, we can achieve more than we ever could alone. That's why we're committed to fostering a supportive community that empowers each member to reach their full potential. Our groups are focused on connection, inspiring stories, celebrating each other's successes and sharing our vulnerable moments.
Join our free community
The Inner Circle
Follow us on Instagram
Empower Her Co-Founders Victoria and Charlotte are highly passionate and dedicated to empowering female leaders in the business world. By joining Empower Her, you will not only expand your professional network but also gain access to a supportive community of like-minded individuals who are committed to helping each other succeed.Find out more about us and check up our upcoming events on our website.

events.name: Westpac Smarts: Leadership That Lasts
events.venue: Westpac Building,Level 10, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Westpac Smarts: Leadership That Lasts
Westpac Smarts: Leadership That Lasts - Lessons from KP

About this Event

What does effective leadership really look like in 2026?
Join us on 23 April for Westpac Smarts with Kirsten Patterson (KP), Chief Executive of the Institute of Directors New Zealand, for a practical and thought-provoking session on the leadership lessons that matter.
Drawing on years at the coalface of governance and executive leadership, KP will share the insights she’s gained throughout her career — and the essential tools every leader needs in their leadership toolkit to navigate complexity, build trust and drive performance.
Awarded a Member of the New Zealand Order of Merit (MNZM) in 2025 for services to governance and women, KP brings deep experience across governance, law and human resources. From chairing ethical leadership initiatives to serving on climate governance and advisory boards, she works closely with directors and senior leaders shaping New Zealand’s future.
This session will blend insight with practical perspective — offering ideas you can apply immediately, whether you are leading a team, a board, or preparing for your next leadership challenge.
This session is sure to be an engaging and high-value conversation for leaders at every stage and is not to be missed.
Event details:
12:00pm - Networking
12:15pm – Presentation
1:15pm – Wrap-up
Chamber members and Westpac customers: Free entry
Non-members: $10.00 donated to Life Flight Trust through the Westpac Chopper Appeal.

events.name: Rova tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Cream Events
THIS IS THE HOO. ROVA is bringing it home.
After shutting down dancefloors across the UK and Europe, Aotearoa producer & DJ ROVA returns home for THIS IS THE HOO, a full scale NZ tour hitting Dunedin, Wellington, Auckland and Christchurch this April.
Since discovering his passion for music production in 2019, Rova has quickly become one of the fastest growing names in drum and bass. Each release showcases his distinct flair for pushing bass music boundaries, with his sound now turning heads around the globe.
With every show, he creates an electrifying atmosphere, blending new sounds with his signature style. His ability to connect with fans through his hard hitting live sets has led to sell out shows across Europe, Australia and New Zealand, this tour marks a full circle moment.
THIS IS THE HOO isn’t just a tour. It’s a homecoming, a statement, and a reminder that Rova is firmly in his moment.

events.id: Conservation Kōrero: Dark Skies-ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Conservation Kōrero: Dark Skies
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne and the Wellington Astronomical Society have teamed up to bring you an evening of ‘Dark Skies’.

Astronomer Holly McClelland will begin the evening with a talk on the importance of dark skies, both to the natural ecosystem and to human health.
Illustrated with stunning image from the Cretney Observatory near Martinborough, Holly will introduce us to the night sky, so the next time you are looking to the stars you can not only find the Southern Cross, but also the Galactic Kiwi.

Weather permitting, we will then have an hour of telescope viewing from the Zealandia carpark. Alternatively, if the weather isn’t ideal, you’ll get the chance to get an up-close look at some of the incredible astronomical equipment used by the Wellington Astronomical Society. WAS members will be available to answer your questions.

About Holly:
Holly is an astronomer and science communicator. She has a Bachelor of Science in Astronomy from the University of Canterbury, was a senior astronomy guide in Tekapo, leading groups on once-in-a-lifetime stargazing experiences. Holly is also on the council of the Wellington Astronomy Society, helping to make events like this possible.

events.id: Faultline Ultra--Wellington NZ
events.name: Faultline Ultra
events.long_description:
Calling all athletes on foot or bike. National and international competitors are invited to test their endurance at Faultline Ultra. For runners and mountain bikers, it’s a dream come true — the ultimate stamina challenge set against the wildly beautiful Wellington landscape. 
Race options
In April 2026, the hills and trails around Wellington offer six possible routes for athletes to push their limits. Choose from the following lengths depending on your ability and appetite.
6km – Magnitude 6.0
12km – The Tremor
25km – After Shock
53km Ultra – Shake and Bake
103km Ultra – The Worlds End
100 Miler – Insanity
The fault lines around Wellington have pushed and pulled the land to create soaring peaks, rugged ridgelines, and bush-clad hills. Athletes can choose trails that wind through Wellington City, Karori, Porirua, Belmont, the Hutt River Trail, and Kāpiti. Sign up and compete — you’re likely to get the best after-match stories you will ever tell.

events.id: Hidde Van Wee tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Hidde Van Wee tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Lacuna
Lacuna presents Dutch selector Hidde Van Wee live at Meow Nui.
One of the most exciting emerging names out of Europe’s underground, Hidde Van Wee lands in Wellington fresh off his latest Eastenderz release ‘Shamans Vision’. With a sound rooted in minimal house and rolling techno, his sets are driven by deep grooves, crisp percussion, and an instinct for building energy across the dancefloor.
Whether in the booth or in the studio, Hidde delivers a sound that’s tight, hypnotic, and built for late nights - the kind that lock you in and don’t let go.
Supporting on the night is Lacuna resident Jack Ritchie, bringing his signature selection, alongside Auckland-based duo Cash - a high-energy pairing making serious waves with their productions and club-ready sound.
Expect a full night of forward-thinking underground music, dialled-in energy, and a packed dancefloor from start to finish.

events.id: King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s a global phenomenon hailed as the most electrifying celebration of the King of Pop’s music. ‘The King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience’ makes its way to New Zealand — and Wellington — for the first time. If you never had the chance to see Michael Jackson live, this is the closest you’ll ever get. From the famous moonwalk to the spine-tingling vocals, every moment of this production channels the spirit of the King of Pop at his peak. Witness the power of hits like Billie Jean, Thriller, Beat It, Smooth Criminal, and plenty more. 
The star of the show is TJ Cappola. His stunning vocals and commanding stage presence make for a flawless embodiment of Michael Jackson. With sold-out performances across Australia and Asia, thousands have been mesmerised by his performance. Backing TJ is a live band and world-class dancers. The replica costumes and cutting-edge lighting and visual display make this more than just a show — it’s an experience.

events.id: One New Zealand Warriors v Dolphins ANZAC match-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: One New Zealand Warriors v Dolphins ANZAC match
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Make your ANZAC long weekend a winner by cheering on the One New Zealand Warriors at Hnry Stadium. Aotearoa’s favourite NRL team is heading south to give fans outside of Auckland the opportunity to see them play. The Hnry Stadium event is only the fourth time the team has staged the prestigious ANZAC Day game in Aotearoa. It’s going to be a rare chance to see them in action. Australian team the Dolphins come from Redcliffe in Queensland.  They are sure to bring the energy needed to make this an epic battle. One New Zealand Warriors have had a sensational season and are currently in fourth place on the NRL Premiership leaderboard. 
From Hnry Stadium all the way along the waterfront, Wellington will be buzzing for the long weekend. Head on down for top-tier footy, food, and entertainment. It’s a great way to celebrate the spirit of the ANZACs as two countries united in history reignite their trans-Tasman rivalry. Be there when the capital goes Warrior mode.

events.long_description:
NZDG North Island Champs 26 - Buckley Golf Course - Directed by Disc Shop NZ
https://www.pdga.com/tour/event/98221
BOOK THE DATE! 144 player cap - this will sell out!
Registration Details:

General Rego NOW open.

COURSE INFO -
Local shredders Jesse Wilson and Jonny Wake have found and crafted what is potentially NZ's best disc golf - disc golf course. Just over a year old, "Buckley" is an absolute gem of a course that has been expertly integrated into the Buckley (ball) Golf Course in the rural town of Shannon.

Shannon, in the North Island of New Zealand, is just over an hours drive from the capital city Wellington (with international airport) and is just a few kilometres off of the National SH1. Small town hospitality NZ at it's finest!

Wanting to step in and help the NZDG find a venue for the 2026 North Island Champs, they enlisted the help of the event veterans at Disc Shop NZ.

Course managers Jesse and Jonny will also be teaming up with the local disc golf club MTDG to help with logistics. The focus will be on a very well run, yet low key North Island Champs, highlighting a disc golf course that speaks for itself.
3 rounds over 3 days - 54 holes total
PDGA B-Tier
NZDG 60 Pointer

FRIDAY PRACTISE DAY TIL DARK
FRIDAY - Players meeting and quick fire evening event
SATURDAY Round 1 Tee Times from 9am
SATURDAY evening quick fire evening event
SUNDAY Round 2 Tee Times
MONDAY Round 3 Tee times - Event wrapped by 3.30pm

Golf Club Bar open
Food deals all weekend
Feature rich course - do not miss event

Most divisions will play at least 3 rounds of this f-bomb level, multi layout course.
Whilst priority will be given to the 3 day registrations, if those spots sell out we will have spaces for those that can only make two days.

GROUP A - MPO / MA1 / MP40 / MP50
3 x rounds of 18, playing from the Pro Tees
GROUP B - FPO / FA1 / MA40 / MA50 / MA60 / MA2
3 x Rounds of 18, playing from the Championship Tees
GROUP C - MA3 / FA3 / Juniors / FA40 / FA50 / FA60
3 x Rounds of 18, playing from Advanced Tees

Registration will start at $100 Am and Rec Divisions / $130 Pro Divisions / Juniors $50

More info to come.
Registrations will open for up to 16 international players in Mid December see registration schedule (in case they need to make plans with other events). After that tiered registration will start to roll out in early 2026. We do expect to sell this event out so please checking in from time to time, sign up to discshop.co.nz emails and follow us on the FB and Insta.

TJ Cappola’s remarkable portrayal, vocals and persona, supported by a live band, professional dancers, replica costumes and a state-of-the-art light show and visuals that will leave you in awe! events.id: Zwanze Day-Garage Project Wild Workshop, 7 Furness Lane Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Zwanze Day
events.venue: Garage Project Wild Workshop, 7 Furness Lane Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
GP is thrilled to be the exclusive host of Zwanze Day for New Zealand in 2026!

We’ll be tapping Cantillon’s Zwanze beers at the Wild Workshop in Wellington on 25 April, alongside a primo selection of beers from the GP cellar - an event this prestigious calls for the best, and we’re pulling out all the stops.

There are two ways you can join Zwanze Day 2026 at the Wild Workshop:

Get tickets to a guided tasting, hosted by Pete Gillespie and Dave Bell (Wild Workshop Head Brewer).

This session will run for 90 minutes from 1pm. Included in the ticket is a guided tasting of 9 beers (the two 2026 Zwanze beers, a few other Cantillon treats and some of GP’s best from the cellar), food to match and a tour of the Wild Workshop brewery. There are limited spots - get in quick.

Come by the Wild Workshop from 2.30pm for freestyle tasting.

Free entry, open for spontaneous walk-ins (get it). Pay-as-you-go to work your way through the line-up, or get a Zwanze flight, with some delish food to boot.

Zwanze Day happens but once every two years. It’s a global beer event run by renowned Belgian brewery Cantillon; they make a unique, one-off beer especially for the event, and it is tapped simultaneously at select venues across almost two-dozen countries. The Wild Workshop is Cantillon's exclusive partner in New Zealand.

This is the event for lovers of craft beer at its finest.

We’ll be tapping Cantillon’s Zwanze beers at the Wild Workshop in Wellington on 25 April, alongside a primo selection of beers from the GP cellar - an event this prestigious calls for the best, and we’re pulling out all the stops.
There are two ways you can join Zwanze Day 2026 at the Wild Workshop: Get tickets to a guided tasting, hosted by Pete Gillespie and Dave Bell (Wild Workshop Head Brewer).
Included in the ticket is a guided tasting of 9 beers (the two 2026 Zwanze beers, a few other Cantillon treats and some of GP’s best from the cellar), food to match and a tour of the Wild Workshop brewery.

events.id: Middle Age Spread - Play Reading-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Middle Age Spread - Play Reading
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
In conjunction with our 50th birthday year, we are pleased to offer a series of rehearsed play readings on select Saturdays throughout the year. These gems have been selected from over 1,149 submissions (* up to 23 April 26), spanning from 1977 to 2012. Four of these plays are from New Zealand/Aotearoa. We are also proud to introduce two additional plays for 2026.

‘Middle Age Spread’
We start our celebrations with ‘Middle Age Spread’ by Sir Roger Hall, directed by Susan Wilson. The 25th April has been purposely selected because Circa’s official opening was the 23rd April 1976, which was picked as it is the same as Shakespeare’s birthday. We will be celebrating after the play reading with birthday cakes and drinks, as part of the performance.

First performed at the original Circa Theatre in Harris Street in 1977, directed by Michael Haigh, starring Dorothy McKegg, Grant Tilly, Jane Waddell, Ray Henwood, Donna Akersten and John Reid. Three couples attend a dinner that none of them wants to be at.

Middle Age Spread ran for 15 months in London’s West End, winning the Lawrence Olivier Award for Comedy of the Year. In 1978, it became a successful NZ film directed by John Reid. This brilliant and poignant tragicomedy still remains a very significantly relevant social commentary on New Zealand Society . . . what’s changed in Aotearoa in 50 years, and then again… What hasn’t?

A brand-new, racer-led national series doesn’t work unless people actually back it… and you did.

By showing up, travelling, supporting tracks, bringing mates, crews, families and good attitudes, you helped turn an idea into something real. The True Nationals became what it is because of the people in it — and that’s worth celebrating properly.

📅 Saturday 25 April
📍 Mendoza Mechanical, Upper Hutt - WELLINGTON
🕔 Arrive from 5pm
🏆 Awards at 6pm
🔥 BBQ from ~6:30pm
(depending on how long Cindy rambles)

On the night:
🏆 True Nationals championship prize giving
🏁 Super Street season highlights
😏 A few moments that deserve revisiting
👀 Including the now-famous Naughty Boy List (10-second club)

Laid-back awards, plenty of laughs, and a chance to look back on a season that honestly shifted the needle for Super Street.

Drivers, crew, families, sponsors, track crew and Super Street supporters — you’re all welcome.

Accomodation Discount - contact Trentham Motel on and mention the Super Street Society Prizegiving for 20% off per night on Studio & King Studio units.
Let’s make this a night to remember (or not be able to remember at all!)

👉 RSVP appreciated for catering
🍔 Food provided | 🍻 BYO | 👶 Kids welcome

Built by racers. For racers.
Thanks for backing it and helping make it what it became.
— HRH Princess of the Swamp 👑

events.name: Live Music - Cammy
events.venue: Plimmerton Boating Club,66 Moana Road, Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Live Music - Cammy
Cammy is a dynamic pop and R&B performer known for his soulful vocals, smooth guitar work, and magnetic stage presence. Based in Kāpiti, he’s become a favourite across bars, restaurants, and weddings nationwide, delivering cover sets that blend the best pop hits and crowd favourites from every era.
With rich vocal tones, a lush falsetto, and an impressive range, Cammy brings a fresh energy to each performance—whether it’s a chilled afternoon session or a packed night of singalongs. His sets are built around connection, rhythm, and feel-good moments, ensuring every audience leaves with a smile and a song in their head.
Alongside his extensive cover repertoire, Cammy also performs original material, showcasing his artistry and versatility beyond the stage.

events.id: Darren Watson Plays The Shed - This Here Guitar Tour '26-46a Victoria St Alicetown,46 Victoria St, Alicetown, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Darren Watson Plays The Shed - This Here Guitar Tour '26
events.venue: 46a Victoria St Alicetown,46 Victoria St, Alicetown, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Darren Watson Plays The Shed - This Here Guitar Tour '26
Kiwi blues legend brings the special sauce!

Darren Watson –This Here Guitar Tour ‘26 - Darren is heading back to The Shed after two sold out shows late last year! Doors open 7pm for a 7.30pm. Limited seats will sell out fast.

From leading the iconic Chicago Smoke Shop to working alongside greats like Dr.John, Keb Mo, Billy Boy Arnold, and Tony Joe White, Darren Watson has been serving up the finest cuts of New Zealand blues music for decades.

His latest release, Darren Watson Sings John Hiatt, recently simmered its way up to #3 on the Official Top 20 Aotearoa Albums Chart.

Watson is hitting the road again to deliver his trademark powerful vocals, astonishing yet understated guitar playing, and soulful,"pin-drop" intensity live.

Don’t miss this tasty-serving of world-class blues and Americana.

What folks say:

“If Ry Cooder had a long-lost New Zealand cousin, it would be Darren Watson.”
Tami Neilson (Kiwi country/blues legend)

“It’s not easy writing catchy tunes that carry a message, but Darren pulls it off easily, singing in his big, soulful voice: ‘...you can hold folks up, you can knock folksdown, it matters what we do and what we say.’ Right on!”
Rick Holmstrom (Mavis Staples Band)

“... outstanding by any measure.”
Downbeat Magazine (USA)

“Watson’s Too Many Millionaires is the finest acoustic blues CD this reviewer has heard in quite some time!”
Blues Matters Magazine (UK)

“This album is so good it has almost overcome my lifelong aversion to the genre.”
Sunday Star Times (NZ)

events.id: Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop ~ Sunday 26th April-Wild Botanica Raumati South,10 Leinster Ave, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop ~ Sunday 26th April
events.venue: Wild Botanica Raumati South,10 Leinster Ave, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop ~ Sunday 26th April
Join expert forager Felicity Joy of Wild Botanica for a comprehensive Wild Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop in her wild & private garden in Raumati South.

This inspiring experience will open your eyes to the abundance of edible wild plants growing right under your nose.

We’ll unlock the secrets of foraging, connecting with the land, and turning humble "weeds" into delicious, nourishing food. You’ll gain confidence in identifying common edible plants, learn their botanical names, and understand their medicinal and nutritional benefits. We’ll also explore ethical foraging practices, how to spot toxic lookalikes, and share simple, practical ways to incorporate wild foods into your daily meals.

This isn’t just about food: it’s about sustainability, resilience, and reconnecting with the environment in a meaningful way. You'll leave with a deeper appreciation for the land, new skills to support local food security, and the knowledge to make your own simple meals from wild ingredients.

Class sizes are limited, so book early to secure your spot.
For more details and bookings, visit

www.wildbotanica.co.nz
follow @wild.botanica.nz on social media.

For questions, email .

Join my email newsletter to hear about upcoming foraging workshops: https://www.wildbotanica.co.nz/pages/newsletter

events.id: Natural Textured Rings-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Natural Textured Rings
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Make your own personalised ring - a gift for yourself or someone special! :-)

Learn the basic technique first in copper or brass, and then craft your final piece in silver!

Over this taster class, you’ll be guided through the process of heating and texturing metal, cutting and shaping with traditional jewellery tools. Use feathers, leaves, or fabrics to create a unique texture for a beautiful ring!

Learn to measure your finger, cut metal to length and texture it without a rolling mill, hammers and punches. Then use a jeweller's torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.

This 3-hour class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends and visitors to the city.

Included in the class fee are copper and brass for practising on, a sterling silver ring strip and all consumables such as sandpaper, solder and chemicals. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an adult.

events.id: Mushrooms and other fungi – guided walk-Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Mushrooms and other fungi – guided walk
events.venue: Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
A walk through the gardens and bush at Ōtari Wilton’s Bush to explore and discover the ever-changing array of mushrooms and fungi that are an essential part of its biodiversity.

Meet at Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. It is easy walking and family groups are welcome. We ask that dogs stay at home for everyones comfort and a smooth tour experience.

This is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual guided walks programme. This year, they are on the fourth Sunday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.
Join the Trust for only $20 for an individual or $30 for a double/family for a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits. www.owbt.nz/memberships-legacies/

Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

events.id: Sunday Jazz - Ol' King Cole-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Sunday Jazz - Ol' King Cole
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer, hangs, and jazz, on a Sunday, perfect!

This week featuring Ol' King Cole Quintet


Ol’ King Cole brings you the hits and celebrates the music of the great Nat ‘King’ Cole & performs the sounds of many other great tunes & artists of his era & more along the way. The group was originally inspired specifically with a focus on Nat’s early drumless trio which has now expanded for this special show and features Wellington based vocalist Wallace.

The core trio consisting of Robert Henderson on double bass along with Lockie Bennett on guitar and Louis Thompson-Munn on piano are now joined by prolific jazz drummer Mike Jensen.

A great and lively performance is always a guarantee with the spirit of the old house band alive, before TVs in bars and when people would dance the night away!

Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY

events.long_description:
Feast Your Eyes | Stand By Me
Directed by Rob Reiner

Starring Jerry O'Connell, Wil Wheaton, River Phoenix, Corey Feldman, Kiefer Sutherland

40th Anniversary

--- After learning that a stranger has been accidentally killed near their rural homes, four Oregon boys decide to go see the body. On the way, Gordie Lachance (Wil Wheaton), Vern Tessio (Jerry O'Connell), Chris Chambers (River Phoenix) and Teddy Duchamp (Corey Feldman) encounter a mean junk man and a marsh full of leeches, as they also learn more about one another and their very different home lives. Just a lark at first, the boys' adventure evolves into a defining event in their lives. ---

Upgrade your ticket to the FEAST YOUR EYES (FYE) experience which includes themed drink and dessert.

At Feast Your Eyes, cinephiles and foodies will be treated to a movie and food experience in the cinema at the perfect after dinner event!

Admit only tickets are $15, but you can upgrade to a Feast Your Eyes tickets for $45. All Feast Your Eyes tickets include a themed dessert* and cocktail to enjoy during the film.

The upstairs bar will open at 7:15pm for cocktail and dessert collection. The pre-show kicks off at 7:40pm and the feature begins at 7:50pm.

*No dietaries catered for.

Presented in 4K

events.id: Beehive to Business with Rt Hon Chris Hipkins-Wellington Chamber Of Commerce,NTT Tower, Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Beehive to Business with Rt Hon Chris Hipkins
events.venue: Wellington Chamber Of Commerce,NTT Tower, Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Beehive to Business with Rt Hon Chris Hipkins
Beehive to Business with Rt Hon Chris Hipkins

About this Event

In an election year, the big decisions are on the table. Hearing directly from political leaders helps businesses understand the signals, spot emerging shifts, and prepare for the road ahead.
We’re excited to announce the second speaker in our Business to Beehive Leaders Series: Rt Hon Chris Hipkins, Leader of the Opposition.
Ahead of the upcoming election, this series brings party leaders to our members for candid conversations about their priorities, perspectives and policy direction.
Join us on 28 April where Rt Hon Hipkins will discuss the broader themes shaping his party’s approach to New Zealand’s future and the issues they see as most important for the country and the business community.
• The party’s overall priorities heading into the election
• Key themes influencing their policy approach
• How they see opportunities and challenges for New Zealand businesses
• Their perspective on what a supportive environment for employers and industry could look like
This is the second event in a series of high value discussions designed to give our members direct access to political leaders and a deeper understanding of the landscape ahead.
Join us for an engaging and informative discussion to help you prepare for the months leading up to election day.
Agenda:
7:15am- Standing breakfast, networking
7:45am- Address
8:10am- Q&A
8:30am- Event concludes
Part of the ongoing Beehive to Business series, this event is proudly made possible through our partnership with Wellington-based consulting firm Allen + Clarke.

events.long_description:
Add dimension to your art at this textured painting event!

Join us for a creative night out and explore how lashings of thick paint and palette knives can bring your artwork to life.

Create, laugh and socialise with friends while trying something new.

This event is perfect for beginners looking to explore a fresh and exciting way to paint! No drinks are included in your ticket price, but drinks are available to purchase.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.id: Distilled Comedy-The Bond Store,21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Distilled Comedy
events.venue: The Bond Store,21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Distilled Comedy
The monthly merriment kicks off on April 29th — and autumn’s got nothing on the heat we’re bringing.

Welcome to the comedy open mic that politely asks structure to wait outside while we set fire to it.

This is the Home of The Jar — our slightly unhinged, deeply democratic system where the audience randomly selects who goes up next. No lineups. No pecking order. No sacred cows. Just brave humans, questionable decisions, and whatever name comes out of the jar. It’s equal parts comedy show, social experiment, and mild psychological thriller.

You might see a first-timer stepping up for their debut. You might see a seasoned comic testing fresh material. You might see someone absolutely smash it. You might see someone realise, in real time, that jokes are harder than they look. That’s the magic.

Come in from the cooler autumn air, grab some food, sip a cocktail, and settle in. The room fills fast, the energy builds quicker, and being there from the start means you’re fully invested when the jar starts causing trouble.

If you love comedy in its rawest, most unpredictable form — this is your night.
If you’ve ever thought, “I could probably do that” — the jar would love to meet you.

Cheap laughs. Big vibes. Absolute chaos.

events.id: STEREOFACE - RADIOHEAD TRIBUTE-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: STEREOFACE - RADIOHEAD TRIBUTE
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Stereoface brings the music of Radiohead to Wellington for the first time ever! Playing music from across the band's 30 year catalogue.
Joining us will be the Bottom Bunk Club, a folkpunk/antifolk band from Pōneke, Wellington, New Zealand. They combine the energy of a sweaty basement punk gig with the feel-good plucking and strumming of an old-time bluegrass hoedown. Imagine Woody Guthrie had a baby with 90s Green Day.
And Girls Rock the Boys hit the stage with massive vocals, huge riffs, and a no‑nonsense blast of classic rock from the likes of Boston, Journey, Zeppelin and Dio. It’s loud, it’s fierce, and it’s all delivered by a powerhouse lineup of women who take these anthems and fire them straight back at the crowd. The perfect high‑voltage jolt before the night shifts into Radiohead territory.

events.id: The Canning Line - Stand-up Comedy Show!-Double Vision Brewery, 128A Unit E Park Rd, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Canning Line - Stand-up Comedy Show!
events.venue: Double Vision Brewery, 128A Unit E Park Rd, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Canning Line Stand-Up Comedy Show returns to Double Vision Brewery, complete with a refresh!

After a successful run of sold-out shows over the last few years, The Canning Line will now be happening more often, moving into the recently revamped covered courtyard space, which is just the right mix of cozy and comfortable, and perfect for a relaxed night of laughs washed down with DVB's award-winning brews!

For each edition of the new Canning Line show, producer MC Mike Fowler will present a different "batch" of local pro and up-and-coming comedians, all bringing their best gags, stories, and new jokes for your consideration. This first April edition has an extra special spark, with every single comedian all featuring in the upcoming NZ International Comedy Festival!

Introducing Batch 001 - April 29th, 7pm

Mike Fowler
Meaghan Fisher
Austin Harrison
Joana Joy
Keegan Thomas & Aaron James Douglas
Oliver Pol
Gabby Anderson

With a reduced ticket price of only $10 and a Happy Hour at the bar from 5pm, this will be an epic night of fun and a welcome escape from world events for a few hours. See you at the end of the line!

events.id: Boney M — The Final Curtain Tour-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Boney M — The Final Curtain Tour
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s one of the most beloved bands to emerge from the golden age of disco, Boney M are back with The Final Curtain Tour, and it will feature original member Maizie Williams. The band is renowned for mixing the glitz and glamour of the 70s with catchy upbeat songs. Head to St James Theatre and enjoy all of their greatest hits. The full live band will play Rasputin, Daddy Cool, Sunny, Rivers Of Babylon, Ma Baker, Gotta Go Home, Brown Girl In The Ring and plenty more. 
Boney M recorded eight albums between 1976 and 1985, and they have gone on to sell over 150 million copies worldwide. The phenomenon continues fifty years later — here’s your last chance to sing along and relive the magic live.

Experience the sights, smells, and flavors of a traditional night market right in the heart of Lower Hutt this autumn. 🍂

The Hutt City Night Market is an international celebration of food, bringing together a delicious mix of cuisines from around the world.

Stroll through bustling stalls, grab a cozy bite (or three), bring your friends and family, stay for a picnic, and enjoy the crisp autumn air in a vibrant community setting.

@wellingtonstreeteats

See you there under the autumn skies! 🍁

events.long_description:
The Devil Wears Prada 2 : Movie Fundraiser
Join us for a special fundraising screening of The Devil Wears Prada 2 in support of Rainbow Wellington.

About this Event

Join us for a special fundraising screening of The Devil Wears Prada 2 in support of Rainbow Wellington. This community movie night is a chance to glam up (if you want to) come together, enjoy a great film, and support the important work being done to strengthen and celebrate Rainbow communities in Wellington.
Set a decade after the original movie, TDWP2 revisits the OG cast and some great outfits with Miranda Priestly struggles against Emily Charlton, her former assistant turned rival executive, as they compete for advertising revenue amid declining print media, while Miranda nears retirement.
Very limited tickets are available.
All proceeds from the event will go towards supporting Rainbow Wellington’s work advocating for and connecting LGBTQIA+ communities across the region.
Bring your friends, enjoy a night at the movies, and help make a difference in our community. 🌈

events.name: Staunch
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mum, Whaea, Mother, Ma – she’s doing what needs to be done!

Anji's been performing since she was a kid. London's West End, national & international T.V etc. etc. TV Presenter, Vocalist, Voice Over Artist, Actress, Comedienne. She's done THE LOT!
Through most of it, her Mum attended, commented, smiled and gushed with pride. Then she was gone - and that's when Anji found out so much more about her Mum than she'd ever known before! Her Mum was STAUNCH. '
With the 4th wall gone, this show has laughter, love, laments & many other fabulous things that don’t begin with L!
Enjoy Anji’s unique, inclusive, warm & welcoming style as well as the possibility of participation (voluntarily of course!)
STAUNCH - Not to be missed, never to be forgotten.

events.id: FELIX BIRD + BECAUSE ROBOTS (KOHA ENTRY)-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: FELIX BIRD + BECAUSE ROBOTS (KOHA ENTRY)
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
THURS 30 APRIL | VALHALLA | 154 VIVIAN ST
Pōneke’s mid-week sanctuary for loud guitars and local legends is back. Join us at Valhalla for a double-header featuring two of the city's most distinctive voices in indie rock.
FELIX BIRD brings his signature "Haircut" sound to the stage. Navigating the space between 90s-inflected nostalgia and modern alt-rock craftsmanship, Bird and his band deliver driving arpeggios and vulnerable, clever songwriting that has quickly made them a must-watch in the local scene.
BECAUSE ROBOTS make their second ever appearance. They will be serving up a figurative buffet of sound—expect cozy indie-rock melodies layered under beautiful, staticy grit. From lo-fi intimacy to wall-of-sound distortion, it’s a set designed for the sonic explorers.
Come for the hooks, stay for the fuzz.
Thursday, 30 April
 Valhalla, 154 Vivian Street
 Doors at 8pm, Because Robots 8:30pm, Felix Bird 9:30pm
 Koha entry (Optional donation at the door)
Felix Bird | Bandcamp | Instagram
Because Robots | Bandcamp | Instagram

events.id: Seth Boy - Gold And Flowers-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Seth Boy - Gold And Flowers
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Seth Boy and his quintet present a collection of original compositions and arrangements, painting a musical portrait of the Philippines: the Land of Gold and Flowers. Born in Quezon City, and growing up in Malaybalay, Pembo, and Catalina Village, Seth brings the perspectives and sounds of Pilipinas to his evolving practice as a jazz musician, and the influence of his moving to Aotearoa as a youth.

This concert will feature native folk melodies and rhythms, Filipino popular classics, and original tunes written about concepts relating to his heritage and upbringing.

Music from 9pm, FREE ENTRY

events.id: Fat Freddy’s Drop-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Fat Freddy’s Drop
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Doing it for the love of music, Fat Freddy’s Drop takes to the Michael Fowler Centre stage in May 2026. They’ll be there for three nights performing their ground-breaking debut album, ‘Based On A True Story’. The tour will mark the first live performances since the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, “It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.” ‘Based On A True Story’ was independently released in 2005. It became Aotearoa’s longest-charting album with 111 weeks in the Top 40. It also won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music Awards in the UK.
Twenty years later, the band’s unique funk and roots style has become a defining sound of Aotearoa. “[Based On A True Story] was the band’s first full-length studio album, and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together,” explains saxophonist Scott Towers. “We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU, and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.”

events.name: Rangatahi Market
events.venue: 72 Cuba Street, Petone, New Zealand 5012,72 Cuba St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Rangatahi Market
Happening for the first time in 2026!
Ka nui te hari, we’re excited to launch our very first Rangatahi Market at Petone Corps! 72 Cuba Street Petone
Our vision is to empower our Rangatahi. To help them feel confident, creative, and capable of sharing their talents with the world. This market is Rangatahi-led, designed for ages 10–16, giving them a space to sell their creations, showcase their skills, and gain real-life experience as young entrepreneurs. We will have Rangatahi busking as well, so if you are a talented performer, sign up! Part of this Kaupapa is that all the earnings that our Rangatahi make will go towards extra curricular activities for the year. i.e sport, music lessons, swim lessons.
We also want our Rangatahi to feel connected to their community. That’s why your presence matters. When whānau and locals come along, purchase their goodies, and show support, it helps build their confidence and strengthens the bonds within our community.
So come along, bring a picnic blanket, relax with the whānau, and enjoy a beautiful day supporting the next generation of creators and leaders.
Haere mai, tautokona mai — we can’t wait to see you there! 🌿✨

Get ready to rock as The Dawn celebrates 40 years of timeless music. This in person concert is your chance to experience the band live on stage bringing their iconic hits and unforgettable sound straight to Wellington.
Where Pinoy rock lives on The Dawn stands as one of the true pillars of the OPM rock movement a band whose music has shaped generations and continues to unite fans across the world.
Don’t miss this spectacular night filled with energy nostalgia and pure Pinoy rock spirit a once in a lifetime celebration you won’t want to miss 🎸

events.id: Hudson Freeman-San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Hudson Freeman
events.venue: San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Hudson Freeman is a Brooklyn-based Lofi-folk artist, inspired and forged by the DIY midwest. The 27-year-old Freeman continues to record and perform songs equal-parts resonant, reflective, and poignant. He has quickly emerged as one of indie's most enthralling new voices. His recent full-length, is a Folk Artist, helped solidify his presence as the kind of songwriter unafraid to tackle life's most draining contradictions: modern identity, digital disconnection, and faith.

Born to Evangelical missionaries, Hudson started writing songs upon a radical break at the age of thirteen when his family suddenly moved from the suburbs of Dallas to The Kingdom of Eswatini. The profound influence of indie big-hitters like Sufjan Stevens and Bon Iver as well as college years spent in Springfield, Missouri made a do-it-yourself midwesterner out of Hudson. Now, his new song "If You Know Me" has recently started drawing in attention from the masses, catapulting Freeman into a wider spotlight than ever. It's a moody blend of bedroom pop, folk, slowcore, and post-emo, stitched together by a singular guitar riff and mantra-style lyrics, confirming Hudson's undeniable knack for melody.

The 27-year-old Freeman continues to record and perform songs equal-parts resonant, reflective, and poignant.
He has quickly emerged as one of indie's most enthralling new voices.
His recent full-length, is a Folk Artist, helped solidify his presence as the kind of songwriter unafraid to tackle life's most draining contradictions: modern identity, digital disconnection, and faith.
Born to Evangelical missionaries, Hudson started writing songs upon a radical break at the age of thirteen when his family suddenly moved from the suburbs of Dallas to The Kingdom of Eswatini.

events.id: Cheep Frills (Live) x Rare Specimens-Cuba St Tavern,172 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Cheep Frills (Live) x Rare Specimens
events.venue: Cuba St Tavern,172 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheep Frills (Live) x Rare Specimens
Born on East London’s dancefloors and forged across Aotearoa New Zealand’s club and festival circuit, Tempo Schmempo finally lands in Te Whanganui-a-Tara for the first time, with a vibrant series of parties for the winter months.

On a bi-monthly basis, expect carefully curated lineups of the country’s finest underground electronic artists, designed to showcase the fantastic talent that thrives across the motu.

What’s more, each party will be punctuated by a double A-side release from the two artists performing on the night - to be released a week before the party.

And to top it off, we've teamed up with Duncan’s Brewing Co. to pour an official Tempo Schmempo beer on tap for the night.

For the first instalment, we’re proud to introduce Cheep Frills (Live) and Rare Specimens.

About the artists:

Cheep Frills is a genre-bending live act from Marcos Alonso and Dolphin Therapy, fusing electronic production with vibrant vocals and playful performance. Their shows deliver high-energy dancefloor stompers, cheeky reinterpretations, and unexpected sonic detours that keep crowds moving and grinning. Equal parts musically tight and joyfully loose, Cheep Frills is a guaranteed mood-lifter for any stage or setting.

As Rare Specimens, Nick Munday, Pete Green, Matt McPhee and Dan Rainey have become firm fixtures in New Zealand’s underground electronic scene, balancing playful eclecticism with dancefloor precision during their unique back to back performances. United by their love of disco, italo, acid house, Detroit techno, Balearic sounds and UK club culture, the group combines road-tested experience with finely tuned taste acquired through decades of crate-digging and DJing across the globe.

Expect only the finest musical selections, lovingly curated from dance music’s rich history — plus more shenanigans than you can shake a stick at.

See you on the dance floor.
