events.id: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Fri 1 May | Michael Fowler Centre-Michael Fowler Centre-All Events In
events.name: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Fri 1 May | Michael Fowler Centre
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Fri 1 May | Michael Fowler Centre
Doing it for the love of music, legendary band Fat Freddy's Drop are returning to the stage in 2026 to perform their ground-breaking debut album Based On A True Story.

The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu. The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.

Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.’

Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps. In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music
Awards in the UK.

‘This was the band’s first full length studio album and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together’, explains saxophonist Scott Towers. ‘We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.’

events.id: Best Foods Comedy Gala-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Best Foods Comedy Gala
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
There’s no better way to kick off a three-week festival of side-splitting laughs. The Best Foods Comedy Gala heralds the beginning of the NZ International Comedy Festival. For one night in Wellington, national and international comedians from the festival lineup gather to give a taster of their comedy. The fast-paced, ever-changing smorgasbord is hosted by Kiwi comedy legend Dai Henwood. He’ll introduce you to old hands and fresh voices. With every imaginable type of comedy covered, the energy is electric. Best of all, you won’t stop laughing all night.   
Dai Henwood has graced the Comedy Gala stage in previous years as a performer. This is his first time hosting. He joins a long line of Kiwi comic royalty in the role, including Hayley Sproull, Chris Parker, Melanie Bracewell, Justine Smith and Pax Assadi. Jay Law, Producer of the NZ International Comedy Festival, said the gala is like Christmas morning for comedy fans. “It’s a night where everyone can come together — artists, producers, audiences, locals, internationals — and celebrate how important comedy is.”

events.id: Public Service Broadcasting tickets-Meow, Te Aro, Wellington-Ticketek
events.name: Public Service Broadcasting tickets
events.venue: Meow, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Handsome Tours presents the return of London-based sonic outfit Public Service Broadcasting who return to Australia and New Zealand in May, bringing their powerful, cinematic live show to audiences across the southern hemisphere.
Since emerging into existence in 2009, Public Service Broadcasting – led by instrumentalist-auteur J. Willgoose, Esq. – have forged a singular musical identity, transforming archival narratives into rousing, emotionally resonant works that blend post-rock, electronics and orchestral grandeur.
Across a celebrated catalogue including Inform-Educate-Entertain, The Race For Space, Every Valley, Bright Magic, the spectacular BBC Proms-recorded This New Noise, and their most recent work The Last Flight, the band have explored human endeavour, collective memory and the restless drive to push beyond known limits.
Sonically rich and emotionally expansive, Public Service Broadcasting’s music evokes motion, risk and wonder – from propulsive hymns of flight to moments of haunting stillness. As ever, their meticulous storytelling and immersive live performance elevate history into something urgent and deeply human.
Renowned for their visually arresting shows and ability to turn historical narratives into shared, communal experiences, Public Service Broadcasting’s return to Australia and New Zealand promises an unforgettable live encounter – one that is at once awe-inspiring, moving and exhilarating.

events.id: Workshop Series: Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama-Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Workshop Series: Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama
events.venue: Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington
events.long_description:
Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama is a four part workshop series themed on the phases of the Māori creation story (te kore, te pō, te whaiao, and te ao mārama) as a metaphor and framework for confronting the cyclical nature of life. Through vinyasa practice, we explore and embody the phases of the creation story, drawing parallels to yogic wisdom and setting a foundation and spark of curiosity for the wānanga that follows. After a short break, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to further explore the deep wisdom of the creation story, learn more about the roles of the atua, and begin to unearth the relevance of it all to our own lives.

No prior experience of knowledge required. All are welcome - haere mai!

What to expect:
We will practice vinyasa flow together, sometimes incorporating meditation or breathwork. After a short break for showers/ lunch/coffee, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to explore the relevant aspects of the Māori creation story for that workshop.

What will people take away from the experience?
A deeper understanding and appreciation of the Māori creation story and how its wisdom can help guide us through life, learned through wānanga and discussion but also through the embodied practice of vinyasa yoga and by drawing parallels with yogic wisdom.

Why Enrol?
To enjoy challenging and fun vinyasa flows themed around the phases of the Māori creation story. To engage in a safe space to ask questions and learn more about the Māori creation story, the phases of creation, the atua, and how the story offers us a framework for moving through the cycles of human life, from darkness to enlightenment.

What to bring:
Bring an open mind, perhaps a journal to gather thoughts down after the workshop. Light kai/food/snacks , as options for buying food are quite limited on the weekends. Mat hire and complementary herbal tea are included in your ticket. Towels can be hired for $3 if needed.

Facilitated by Ani Morris (Ngāti Kahungunu, Te Āti Awa, Rangitāne, Ngāti Mutunga, Rongowhakaata, Ngāti Tūkorehe, Tūhoe, Ngāti Pāhauwera) & Jenny Oliver (RYT 200).
events.description:
All are welcome - haere mai!
After a short break for showers/ lunch/coffee, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to explore the relevant aspects of the Māori creation story for that workshop.
What will people take away from the experience?
To enjoy challenging and fun vinyasa flows themed around the phases of the Māori creation story.
To engage in a safe space to ask questions and learn more about the Māori creation story, the phases of creation, the atua, and how the story offers us a framework for moving through the cycles of human life, from darkness to enlightenment.

events.id: Plant Legends, Myths and Symbolism - Part 2 - Guided Walk-Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Plant Legends, Myths and Symbolism - Part 2 - Guided Walk
events.venue: Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
In ancient times, people were dependent on nature, so that plants played a crucial role in the lives of our ancestors. Plant legends and myths shape traditions by giving certain plants symbolic meaning. These stories influence how people used them in rituals and celebrations.

Meet at the City Lookout beside the Cable Car, Upland Road, Kelburn.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)

This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.
Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.venue: The Raumati Social Club,34 Poplar Ave, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Suny Siide Up Cocktail Party
We invite you to a night of classy vintage feel.

On May 2nd, the Raumati Social Club will be transformed into an elegant cocktail haven, an oasis of vintage décor, soft golden lighting, and timeless charm.

Picture yourself sipping crafted classic cocktails, surrounded by artful vintage furnishings, and dancing to a curated soundtrack by the head honchos of Suny Siide Up - Mapper & Dusa.

This is a 5 hour marathon of music from these two - we are sure to go on a journey, tag along!

We want to create a vibe of good music, classiness, and stepping into a retro dream. Dress up as much or as little as you feel comfortable.

Tickets are limited so get in early.

events.long_description:
Best Food Comedy Gala

Information
All Tickets are Mobile Ticket Only. Mobile Tickets are like Print-at-Home tickets but instead of having to print off the tickets yourself, you can just show the barcode on your mobile phone. It is the easiest way to access tickets to your events. For more information visit Ticketmaster.co.nz/mobileticket Please note: There is no elevator in the Opera House and access to the Dress Circle and Gallery are via stairs. The Gallery may be unsuitable for patrons with accessibility requirements or experience vertigo. For further information, please contact us on 04 916 1205. Please Note: This event is recommended 15+ years. Get ready, Whanganui-a-Tara! The Best Foods Comedy Gala is back to launch the 2026 NZ International Comedy Festival with a massive night of world-class laughs!


Nearby Attractions
New Zealand International Comedy Gala has 3 upcoming events.

A delivery fee may apply depending on the mode of delivery selected. Fees for Resale Tickets br> Resale Tickets, sold by fans and verified by Ticketmaster, may be available for this event. events.long_description:
Get ready, Te Whanganui-a-Tara - the Best Foods Comedy Gala is back to launch the 2026 NZ International Comedy Festival with a massive night of world-class laughs!

This year’s Gala will be hosted by none other than Dai Henwood, one of Aotearoa’s most beloved and quick-witted comedy icons. Dai’s hosting a line-up stacked to the rafters with comedic brilliance with a premium blend of local legends and international favourites taking over the Wellington Opera House for one unforgettable night.

Expect big laughs, big energy, and the kind of chaos only a packed-out Wellington crowd can deliver. The Gala sells out year after year, so secure your seats early or lock in the perfect treat for the comedy lover in your life.

All proceeds support the New Zealand Comedy Trust and its work developing the next generation of comedy talent across Aotearoa.

For corporate or group bookings, get in touch at tickets@comedyfestival.co.nz and we’ll sort you out.

events.id: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sat 2 May | Michael Fowler Centre-Michael Fowler Centre-All Events In
events.name: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sat 2 May | Michael Fowler Centre
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Fat Freddy's Drop: Based on a True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sat 2 May | Michael Fowler Centre
SOLD OUT! Doing it for the love of music, legendary band Fat Freddy's Drop are returning to the stage in 2026 to perform their ground-breaking debut album Based On A True Story.

The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu. The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.

Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.’

Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps. In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music
Awards in the UK.

‘This was the band’s first full length studio album and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together’, explains saxophonist Scott Towers. ‘We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.’

The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu.
Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing this album live is part of that.’ Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps.
In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music Awards in the UK.

events.name: The Funkadelics
events.venue: 118 Tory Street, New Zealand 6011,Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Funkadelics
The Undercurrent presents The Funkadelics!

The Funkadelics is a dynamic six-piece band bringing you the best of Motown and beyond, featuring creative arrangements and jazz-infused solos.

This electrifying show blends the iconic Motown hits with the improvisational spirit of jazz, creating a unique and unforgettable musical experience. Expect tight harmonies, funky basslines, and soaring saxophone solos that pay tribute to the legends of Motown while injecting a fresh, jazzy twist.

Featuring the powerhouse vocals of Jamie Musuva, backed by some New Zealand's finest musicians. You absolutely have to catch this band's live show!

Playing irresistible, toe-tapping tunes from soul legends like Otis Redding, Aretha Franklin, Wilson Pickett, James Brown, Al Green, Stevie Wonder, Chaka Khan, Curtis Mayfield, Bill Withers, and many more.

Jamie Musuva - vocals
Zak Dikvert - guitar & vocals
Miles Crayford - Hammond & keyboard
Russell Henderson - saxophones
Paul Mouncey - bass
Greg Crayford - drums

Saturday May 2
Doors 7:30pm
Music 8pm
$25

The Funkadelics performing at The Roxy: https://www.facebook.com/share/v/18B6pa9yVr/

Tickets available online and on the door!
https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/gig/101310/The-Funkadelics.utr

Snacks, a range of refreshing drinks, tea & coffee, and of course...books available for purchase!

We’ll see you there ☆

events.id: An Early Mother’s Day Treat – The Tui Express-Plimmerton Railway Station,Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: An Early Mother’s Day Treat – The Tui Express
events.venue: Plimmerton Railway Station,Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
An Early Mother’s Day Treat – The Tui Express
🌷🚂 An Early Mother’s Day Treat – The Tui Express
Sunday 3 May 2026

Our Tui Express this May runs the week before Mother’s Day, making it the perfect opportunity to treat Mum to a special day out a little early.

👉 Book here: https://fareharbor.com/embeds/book/mainlinesteamheritagetrust/items/?flow=1555439&full-items=yes&back= https://www.mainlinesteam.co.nz/

Join us as we travel by train to Mangatainoka and the famous Tūī Brewery, enjoying the scenery along the Kapiti Coast and through the Manawatū countryside.

Travel in comfort aboard our premium train, and soak up the atmosphere of classic rail travel.

When we arrive at Mangatainoka, a short bus transfer (or an easy 10-minute walk) will take you to the Tūī Brewery, where you can enjoy a gourmet BBQ lunch in the historic Flagon Room, including a complimentary drink. Each guest also receives a Mangatainoka Passport with vouchers to redeem at the brewery.

🚉 Station stops include
Plimmerton
Paraparaumu
Ōtaki
Levin
Palmerston North

It’s a great way to celebrate Mum with a relaxing day out and a unique rail experience.

🎟 Seats are limited

❤️ Mainline Steam Heritage Trust

events.id: Money Moves - Financial Literacy for Wāhine Keynote-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,55 Cable Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Money Moves - Financial Literacy for Wāhine Keynote
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,55 Cable Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Money Moves - Financial Literacy for Wāhine Keynote
Hosted by W`ahine Wanawana - Ara Ki Angitu Charitable Trust & R&P Insurance

About this Event

Koha only event - please add your koha amount in the space when you are purchasing your ticket.

This keynote event has been built to inspire Wāhine on their financial journey, to build confidence and community in a room full of other Women. Get the gals together and join us for an epic day. Check out R&P Insurance Instagram or website to see the highlights of last year's events in Christchurch.

W`ahine Wanawana - Ara Ki Angitu is a charitable trust set up by R&P Insurance to invest in our community through financial education and connection.

Our Speakers:
Brooke Roberts - Co founder of Sharesies
Sarah Brown - I am not your rehab
Tegan Yorwarth - Mai FM
Te Kahukura Boynton - Māori Millionaire
Courtney Manu - Content creator @themanuss
Rochelle and Philly - R&P Insurance

Our MC - Janelle Riki from Riki Consultancy

Please arrive 10.45am for a strict 11am start. Please use the main entrance to Te Papa and we will direct you to Rangimarie Rooms 1&2 for the event.

You will have time to mingle with each other and our speakers and light refreshments will be provided. We love children but this event is 16+ only and each attendee needs to be registered.

This event is the inspiration, following this we will announce the "practical" side - our financial foundations workshops also to be held in person in Pōneke.

events.venue: St John's in the City Presbyterian Church, 170 Willis Street., Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Bible can feel big, complex, or hard to navigate, but it doesn’t have to. This winter, St John’s in the City is hosting The Bible Course, an eight‑week journey that helps you see how the whole story of Scripture fits together and why it matters for life today.

This is the first time The Bible Course has been run in New Zealand, and we’re delighted to partner with Bible Society NZ as their pilot group ahead of a national launch during Bible Month in July. Over 140,000 people in the UK have already taken part, finding it accessible, engaging, and confidence‑building.

Whether you’ve read the Bible for years or are just beginning to explore Christian faith, you’re warmly invited.

Each Sunday evening includes:
- A shared meal to begin the night together
- Childcare for preschool and primary-aged children
- A 20‑minute video teaching session
- Time in small groups to talk, ask questions, and explore the themes
- A physical guidebook to use throughout the course (optional purchase: $10)

Spaces are limited, registration is essential

Over 140,000 people in the UK have already taken part, finding it accessible, engaging, and confidence‑building.
Whether you’ve read the Bible for years or are just beginning to explore Christian faith, you’re warmly invited.

events.id: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based On A True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sun 3 May | MFC-Michael Fowler Centre-All Events In
events.name: Fat Freddy's Drop: Based On A True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sun 3 May | MFC
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Fat Freddy's Drop: Based On A True Story 21st Anniversary Tour | Sun 3 May | MFC
Doing it for the love of music, legendary band Fat Freddy's Drop are returning to the stage in 2026 to perform their ground-breaking debut album Based On A True Story.

The 51 dates across Australia, Aotearoa, Europe & UK will be Freddy’s first shows following the sudden death of founding band member Chris ‘MU’ Faiumu. The band is united in its decision to continue with this long-planned tour.

Lead singer Dallas Tamaira says, ‘It’s important for us to honour the intentions we had with MU and playing
this album live is part of that.’

Based On A True Story was independently released in 2005, an album of 10 fresh slices of future funk, with surprises, fakes & freaky sidesteps. In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music
Awards in the UK.

‘This was the band’s first full length studio album and its success was the springboard for what has been an incredible journey together’, explains saxophonist Scott Towers. ‘We want to pay tribute to the music, to MU and the band he established. And, of course, to play to our fans around the world who have supported us through everything.’

In a historic run, the album became Aotearoa’s Longest-Charting Album with 111 weeks in the Top 40, won eight NZ Music Awards and took out Giles Peterson’s Worldwide Music Awards in the UK.

events.id: Baxter Dury tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Baxter Dury tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Live Nation
Following the release of his latest album, UK alternative artist Baxter Dury is bringing his Allbarone Tour 2026 to Australia and New Zealand. With genre-blurring music that blends post-punk, indietronica and art-rock, his sound leans on sleek synths and a cool, understated aesthetic rooted in a distinctly British sensibility. ‘Allbarone’ can be described as a nine-track tour-de-force, stripping everything away and building Baxter’s most melodically direct, futuristic collection yet.
The Australian leg of the tour will kick off at Melbourne’s Forum on April 28, before heading to Roundhouse in Sydney on April 29, and concluding at The Tivoli in Brisbane on May 1. Dury will then head to New Zealand for performances at The Hollywood Avondale in Auckland on May 3 and Wellington’s Meow Nui on May 4.

events.id: Communication English - Level 3-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Communication English - Level 3
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Communication English - Level 3
By the end of the course, you will: 
• Have increased your confidence in speaking English
• Be able to understand native speakers more
• Have improved your vocabulary and grammar
• Understand and use informal spoken English
• Understand and describe more about local systems and customs
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents but to enrol you must contact the CEC office (04 385 8919) for an interview. There are limited free places available and you may need to go on a waiting list. You'll also need to come to our office and take a placement test to make sure that the course is the right level for you.
There may be some places on the course for people who are not NZ residents or citizens but you must pay the course fee of $1049. 
This course is subsidised by the Tertiary Education Commission and you must provide evidence of your NZ residency or citizenship (if applicable).
Please note that there is no student parking at the High School during the day. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

English in the Community courses help you to learn more English, get to know your community and meet new people. The groups aim to help you use what you already know, learn more from your tutor and the other students and feel more at home in your local community.

events.id: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
This is an introduction to the wonderful, organic material that is clay. You'll receive a comprehensive overview to working with clay which will cover all steps from clay preparation and re-cycling through to glazing the finished work. You'll learn to throw basic forms with group demonstration and one-to-one instruction.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link. 
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
•  Wedge and re-cycle clay 
•  Demonstrate the basics of working on a pottery wheel 
•  Centre clay on the wheel 
•  Open a centred piece of clay on wheel 
•  Pull clay up to form basic beaker/cylinder shape 
•  Demonstrate the correct use of prepared glazes 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
Learn how to prepare and alter clay:  pinch, coil, slab, extruder and roller use.  Understand shrinkage and clay care, joining techniques, drape and press moulding, mark making, slip making and application - as well as tetrahedron, cone and cylinder production
The techniques learnt during the making will provide an excellent springboard to creating a wide array of ceramic objects in the future.
The emphasis will be on learning techniques and honing those skills and by the end of the 8 sessions you will have completed three works which will be fired and glazed.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Throwing: Next Step-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Throwing: Next Step
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Next Step
A follow-on course for those who have some experience of throwing on the wheel and would like some guidance to take their skills to the next stage.
You’ll learn how to “turn” your pots with more confidence and be able to make a variety of small to medium-sized projects. Basic glazing of your pots will also be covered.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Turn a pot
• Make a lidded container
• Make a rounded vase
• Form a reasonable sized bowl
• Do basic glazing
All materials included in course fee. Wear old clothes and footwear suitable for a workshop environment and bring an old towel.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Epic Event Structures-Marion Street-All Events In
events.name: Epic Event Structures
events.venue: Marion Street
events.long_description:
Epic Event Structures
Epic Event Structures is part of NZS Group - New Zealand's leading scaffolding and height access service provider.

About this Event

Auckland event structures are essential to the success of many events because they provide a flexible and useful way to design areas that may accommodate a variety of gatherings. The correct event structure may improve the whole experience by offering cover, protection, and a visually appealing atmosphere for everything from weddings and business gatherings to major festivals and fairs. These constructions are made to keep people safe and guarantee that the event goes on without any problems, whether it's raining in the winter or sunny in the summer. They can take many different shapes, such as marquees, tents, pavilions, and specially constructed buildings, all of which are intended to provide cover while maintaining an open and breezy atmosphere. Contact us today to discuss your event needs and let our team provide you with the perfect solution to make your event a success.
Visit:- https://epic.co.nz/



https://vimeo.com/1121832687

events.name: Assertiveness - Stand Up For Yourself
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Gain practical skills to help you get what you want out of life and improve your self-esteem.

An opportunity to learn practical skills to assist you in communicating in an assertive, healthy manner, as well as improving your self-esteem. We'll work both individually and as a group using this environment to help us grow as we learn.

By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
- Speak in a language that commands respect and learn how to say “no”
- Use strategies to allow you not to be manipulated by other people
- Affirm yourself and others
- Express increased self-esteem
- Deal assertively with angry feelings and understand your own and others’ anger
- Interact more effectively in your personal relationships and the workplace


Duration 4 weeks, Tue 5:30 PM – 8:00 PM

Wellington High School
Presented by Lesli Mancktelow
$149.00 incl. GST
NZ citizens and residents: $79.00 incl. GST

An opportunity to learn basic practical skills to assist you in learning to communicate in an assertive healthy manner. Come prepared to participate in class, as well as be working on assigned activities between classes. We'll work both individually and as a group using this environment to help us grow as we learn.
  By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
Use a strategy to identify your own feelings, and to express what you would like
Have an increased understanding of your own and other peoples’ anger, dealing with that in a more assertive manner
Use strategies to allow you not to be manipulated by other people
Speak in a way that commands respect and to begin to learn how to say “no”
Begin interacting more effectively in your personal relationships and the workplace
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking - More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cooking - More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Description
Come along and learn how to create wonderful Vietnamese dishes bursting with fresh flavours.

Depending on what's seasonally available, dishes taught may include some of the following: green papaya prawn salad (Nộm tôm đu đủ xanh), lemongrass chicken stir fry with steamed rice (Cơm Gà rang sả) , grilled pork noodle and fried rolls (bún nem thịt nướng) and beef noodle soup (Phở bò).

All ingredients provided but bring an apron, dish cloth, tea towels and containers to take food home.

Note: Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking Thai Food With Aranya-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cooking Thai Food With Aranya
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Thai food is some of the most popular and flavoursome in the world!
Join Chef Arayna as she teaches you how to make a range of delicious Thai food.

Description
Thai cuisine is an exciting explosion of tastes and textures. This highly aromatic food has become popular around the globe. Each week you will be shown different Thai dishes and you will get to make these. This course will provide you with tips, tricks and knowledge and it will be fun, easy and yummy!

Not suitable for vegetarians. All ingredients included in course fee – please bring to each session your apron, a couple of tea towels, a dishcloth and containers to take leftovers home

By the end of the course you'll be able to

Make a range of Thai dishes at home with more confidence
Use ingredients spices and herbs like Thai people do!
Be able to explain some simple Thai eating etiquette

Note: Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

events.id: Night Photography: In the Dark-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Night Photography: In the Dark
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Night photography is, by its nature, magical. In the photographer’s hands, the camera becomes a magic box that allows the night to be caught in a way the human eye could never see.

This course gives you the opportunity to explore and sharpen both your technical and creative skills, so you have the ability and vision to capture the enchanting world that resides in the darkness.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Identify the equipment needed to make photographs at night
- Identify how to use technical photographic tools in a nighttime context
- Explore techniques that allow you to develop a more creative approach to picture-making including: light painting, intentional camera movement, light trails and working with water and reflections in your images
- Recognise the importance of light and colour in night photography and develop your ability to use it to make dynamic images

You must bring a tripod and, if you have one, a neutral density filter and cable release remote. You need a fully manual DSLR or mirrorless camera and an intermediate understanding of camera functions. Not suitable for point and shoot cameras.


5 weeks, 5:30PM- 7:30 PM
Wellington High School
Presented by David Jensen
$165 incl. GST

events.id: Sewing - All Levels-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sewing - All Levels
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sewing - All Levels
All levels welcome! Beginning sewers will learn basic skills and techniques and those with some experience will improve their skills and learn tips and tricks.
Beginners - If you're a beginner, you'll start by making a lined bag, which teaches all the basic skills involved. After that you'll move on to a project of your choice, in consultation with the tutor.  Please bring: 1 metre of firm cotton fabric for your bag outer and 1 metre of a lighter cotton fabric for the lining. You'll also need to bring scissors, a Bernina bobbin (this can be purchased from the CEC office on the day of the course for $7), pins, thread to match your outer fabric, an 18 cm zip and tape measure to the first session.
 If you're a returning student, or have already mastered the basic skills, you'll work on your own project so please bring along whatever you're currently working on (or something new) and your sewing kit. 
Please note PDF patterns are not suitable for this course unless you are very experienced in using them;  we strongly recommend patterns from the following companies:  Simplicity, Butterick, New Look or Quicksew.
By the end of the course, you'll have been introduced to basic skills in dressmaking, if beginners, or further skills for the more experienced.
You'll be able to:
• Use all sewing machine equipment and demonstrate basic sewing techniques (basic seams and finishes)
• Begin to read a commercial pattern and select appropriate materials for task
• Cut out and construct a garment using techniques learned
• (In addition), use a variety of advanced sewing techniques e.g. rolled hems, various zip insertions (invisible and standard), make buttonholes or line garments. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Book Group: Doctor Zhivago by Boris Pasternak-The Library, 1/53 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Book Group: Doctor Zhivago by Boris Pasternak
events.venue: The Library, 1/53 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
A sweeping tale of love, loss and the upheaval experienced during the turbulent years of the Russian Revolution. The story centres on Doctor Yuri Zhivago, a physician and poet, whose life is changed forever by the October Revolution and subsequent civil war. Boris Pasternak’s 1957 novel was an international bestseller and, of course, became an epic film that is one of the highest-grossing of all time.

Do you keep meaning to read more of the great classics of literature? At Wellington Classic Literature Club, we read novels that have stood the test of time, whether they were written in 1780, 1880 or even 1980. We meet on the first Tuesday of the month.

RSVP
Bookings essential. Get your free ticket by clicking 'Register' above. Limited places.

If you've signed up, but things change and you're not going to be able to make it along, please email classiclitwgtn@gmail.com as we may be able to make more spaces available for others.

VENUE
We meet in the ‘Games Room’, The Library bar's private area overlooking Courtenay Place. After you enter the bar, head to your right (up the step) and through a smaller space with booths to the large carpeted space beyond.

ABOUT US
Wellington Classic Literature Club has been running for more than a decade - since the start of 2013 - and we have a great bunch of regulars as well as readers who join us for particular books or simply when it suits.

events.id: Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery Making - Precious Stones in Cast Rings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Create sculptural rings studded with dazzling precious gemstones! Combine wax working and stone setting methods to produce a set of rings cast in your choice of copper, brass, bronze, white bronze and silver - beginners welcome!

All materials are included in the course fee.

Description

Expanding on the Lost Wax Cast Rings class, students in this course get to add precious gemstones to their cast jewellery process!

Experiment and create sculptural cast metal rings set with beautiful stones in a variety of colours.
This beginner-friendly jewellery class is also fun and engaging for returning students to diversify their skills and set stones in new ways.

Students will learn to work with a range of jeweller waxes, including hard wax, modelling wax, sheet wax, melting wax and more to create wax 'masters', setting stones in wax, as well as turning raw metal casts into shining, ready-to-wear jewellery.

Students choose their favourite wax masters to be cast professionally outside of class time, transforming the designs into their choice of metals, and are returned to students to polish and finish their casts in class.

The course fee includes a range of small precious gemstones, casting 2 rings in either copper, brass, yellow bronze, white bronze, and 2 rings cast in Sterling Silver.

The bold, organic forms and textures of the lost wax processes offer a wide range of creative possibilities beyond the scope of fabrication methods alone (soldering metal sheet and wire). Setting faceted stones in wax offers a simpler and more experimental way of adding stones to jewellery compared to fabrication settings.

All materials are included in the course fee. Please bring pen and paper, and your reading glasses (if you use these), and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.

By the end of the course, students will be able to:

Design and create unique sculptural ring designs

Carve a hard jeweller’s wax ring master design to fit a chosen finger size for casting

Sculpt soft jeweller’s wax master design to fit a chosen finger size for casting

Combine hard and soft jeweller’s wax to create unique designs ready for casting

Set faceted gemstones in wax for ‘cast-in-place’ setting methods

File, sand and polish metal casts into finished jewellery

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Duration 6 weeks, Tue 6:00 PM – 8:30 PM
Thorn Richards

events.id: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Te Reo Māori - Introductory 2
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $110 with ID, all others pay $205).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Discuss our responsibilities as a bi-cultural nation under ‘Te Tiriti o Waitangi’ and identify underlying Māori values such as: whanaungatanga, pepehā and tikanga
• Discuss the significance of whakapapa as a fundamental Māori world view and confidently describe your family and relationships through this lens
• Form simple sentences to describe parts of your face and body
• Discuss protocol on the marae
• Recognise the importance and use of karakia and be able to recite one correctly
• Recognise the cultural values inherent within different whakataukī (proverbs) and kīwaha (idioms) and be able to correctly recite one of each
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.

This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
 ‘Tuning in to Teens’ is led by educational psychologist Vernice Young, this evidence based programme focuses on Emotion Coaching, helping parents:

events.id: All About Our Ancient Bats-Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: All About Our Ancient Bats
events.venue: Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
A creature of the forest floor and the night sky, the New Zealand short-tailed bat is found nowhere else on Earth and is clinging on to survival in a handful of scattered sites.
Join Dave Hunt as he explores the current conservation status of these extraordinary ancient bats, and the work underway to protect and manage their fragile populations.

Located in Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. Gold coin donation. No need to book, just turn up!

This seminar is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual seminar series. This year, they are on the first Tuesday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.


Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

Image credit: New Zealand lesser short-tailed Bat, Mystacina tuberculata tuberculata, collected 20 June 1998, The Valley, Codfish Island, New Zealand. Gift of the Department of Conservation, 1998. CC BY 4.0. Te Papa (LM001330)

Located in Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre.

events.id: Drawing For Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Drawing For Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Always wanted to know how to draw but never had the chance to learn? Join our experienced tutor and get confident with the basics of drawing.

Description A back-to-basics programme for those who always wanted to know how to draw but never had the chance to learn.
Bring along your own visual diary (A4 or larger) All other course materials will be provided.

By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
- Use line to draw and gain familiarity with using a pencil and other drawing mediums, including oil pastel, coloured pencil and charcoal
- Develop observational skills to aid the drawing process and practise line drawing techniques
- Use tone and shading to describe form and light
- Create a final drawing of your choice

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Wellington High School
Presented by Iain Watt

$145 incl. GST

By the end of the course, you'll be able to communicate in basic sign language and will be able to:

- Demonstrate strategies for: getting attention, asking for clarification, correction and repetition

events.id: Scottish Country Dancing-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Scottish Country Dancing
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Scottish Country Dancing
Why do Scottish Country Dancing? Great music and amazing ‘brain gym’ for body and mind! Scottish Country Dancing is a very social form of dancing, danced in "sets" usually with four "couples" - unlike Highland Dancing which is usually solo dancing. Have fun and meet new people, while getting fit!
It’s suitable for all levels of fitness, and is a great way of meeting and socialising with new people. Scottish country dancing is a common pastime for thousands of dancers in many countries, whether you’re in Japan, Germany, the US or Scotland itself.
Wear soft flat shoes and loose clothing and bring a bottle of water. No partner is needed – we change partners for every dance!
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Name and perform to beginner level the five major steps of Scottish Country Dancing
• Name and perform ten simple formations
• Participate in five simple Scottish Country dances, in strathspey (slow) and reel/jig (fast) tempo
• Clap rhythm to reel, jig and strathspey tempo
• Have the confidence and skills to join a club to progress your social dancing 
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Zach & Lesa Bought a Gun || NZ Intl Comedy Festival
A classic double-header performed by two of the most [figure out technically accurate adjective] comedians in NZ, Zach Mandeville and Lesa MacLeod-Whiting.

events.id: Split Enz — Forever Enz Tour-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Split Enz — Forever Enz Tour
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
During their recording career, Split Enz released a string of hits across the late 70s and early 80s. Those songs quickly gained notoriety and defined an era for many New Zealanders. Now, for the first time in nearly 20 years, the band has reunited for a New Zealand tour. Featuring original members Tim Finn, Neil Finn, Eddie Rayner and Noel Crombie, the Split Enz — Forever Tour will remind audiences of the band’s huge creative talent. Songs include I Got You, I See Red, Message To My Girl, Six Months In A Leaky Boat, History Never Repeats, One Step Ahead, and plenty more. 
Formed in New Zealand in 1972 before relocating to Australia in 1975, Split Enz is widely regarded as one of the most important antipodean groups of the last five decades. Their number one, 1980 album release, “True Colours”, achieved multiple-platinum status in Aotearoa and was certified five times platinum in Australia, selling over 250,000 copies. In 2005, Split Enz were inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame. Says founding member Tim Finn — “Aotearoa, rugged individual! We’re looking forward to climbing into our new Noel Crombie suits and seeing you all soon.”

events.id: Thundercat tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Thundercat tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Bench Music
Known for his jazz-fusion sensibility and elastic funk, Thundercat is bringing his 2026 tour to Wellington this May, performing live in support of his new album Distracted. See his show at Meow NUI in Wellington on Wed, 6 May 2026.
In an age of endless notifications and fractured attention, Thundercat turns the chaos inward on Distracted, his long-awaited new album and one of his most resonant statements yet. Six years on from It Is What It Is, the record captures the restless aftermath of grief and modern overload, balancing existential weight with Thundercat’s unmistakable wit and warmth. It’s an album that laughs through the noise, folding jazz-fusion virtuosity, elastic funk and late-night introspection into songs that feel like unfiltered inner monologues: tender, funny, and painfully human.
That philosophy carries directly into the live experience. Onstage, Thundercat treats Distracted not as a lecture on modern life, but as a shared release, stretching songs, breaking the fourth wall, leaning into humour and spontaneity as a form of survival.
Thundercat's New Zealand shows promise moments of presence inside the noise: music that drifts, refocuses, and reminds you why joy still matters. It’s playful, loose, deeply human, and a rare chance to experience Distracted and all of Thundercat the way it was meant to be felt, not scrolled past.
Thundercat - 'I Did This To Myself (feat. Lil Yachty)' (Official Audio)
Thundercat - 'I Wish I Didn't Waste Your Time'
Thundercat - 'No Moe Lies (feat. Tame Impala)' (Official Visualizer)

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Leadlighting/Stained Glass
Bring along your project ideas and learn the techniques of glass cutting and making stained glass panels. Suitable for both beginners and those with previous knowledge.
NOTE: If this is your first Leadlighting course, you MUST attend the first session when key instruction is given. If you do not attend the compulsory first session, you will forfeit your course fees and will not be able to transfer to a future session of this course
(Unsuitable for pregnant people due to presence of lead in some materials)
 Your materials costs are dependent on the size of your project and the coloured glass content (likely minimum cost around $170). Please wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.
By the end of the course, you'll: 
• Have designed and constructed a small rectangle/window, lampshade or light catcher 
• Be able to cut glass and assemble leadlighting panels 
• Be able to solder, seal and finish a leadlighting project 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
We will prepare simple, traditional Japanese home-style meals consisting of rice, soup, and a main dish (Ichijyu-issai). In each class, we'll make a different-flavoured miso soup along with a popular Japanese dish -- for example, teriyaki chicken or tonkatsu (crumbed pork cutlet).
  We'll introduce you to Japanese food that's easy to prepare, tasty, and made with simple fresh ingredients plus we'll show you how easy it is to incorporate tasty and healthy Japanese food into your daily diet.
All ingredients are included in the fee. You just need to bring an apron, a dish cloth, a tea towel, and a container to take your food home in. Don't forget your smartphone if you want to take photos!
By the end of the course, you’ll:
become more familiar with using Japanese ingredients
feel more confident to cook authentic Japanese-style dishes at home 
have the know-how to make delicious, well-balanced, and nourishing meals for the whole family.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus. 
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Creative Writing - Express Yourself-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Creative Writing - Express Yourself
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Writing - Express Yourself
Creative writing is a powerful tool for personal growth and is an art form that belongs to all people. Everyone has their own unique writer’s voice, so if you’ve always wanted to explore your creativity through writing, without pressure, join this weekly workshop.
  No previous writing experience needed - express yourself! Bring pen and paper and/or your laptop.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
• Unlock your hidden creativity
• Explore your writer’s voice
• Experience personal insights
• Develop self-awareness through writing
• Produce text with confidence
• Write on 
This course is designed to help you improve your skills, to develop your unique writer's voice and to encourage you to take risks on the page. It is not a course where you'll be asked to read your work out loud, critique other writers' work, or hand in your writing to the tutor. This is a safe place where you will be supported to do what writers do best - write.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Life Drawing - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Life Drawing - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Life Drawing - Beginners
This course will help artists and designers at all levels to develop their life-drawing skills. Starting with an exploration of structure, form, shape and proportion, we will progress through foreshortening while creating illusions of depth and atmosphere using light sourcing. You will also learn to draw using a range of media.
Please bring an A2 sketch pad, 2B and 4B pencils and an eraser.  Cost of model included in course fee.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
- Draw the human form
- Comprehend structure and shape
- Demonstrate fundamental drawing skills
- Be familiar with proportion
- Use drawing media to create tonal range
Please note this course uses live models to draw from. It may include some nudity, including draped models and possibly completely unclothed models. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Smartphone videography is an increasingly important and useful skillset. Whether you fancy yourself as a social media influencer or you want to make and edit content for your business for life – this course will teach you how to plan video content, use a variety of camera techniques and employ basic video principles. We will also explore editing apps and how to produce polished videos for platforms like YouTube, Tik Tok and Facebook.

events.id: Make Your Own Roman Blinds-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Make Your Own Roman Blinds
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Make Your Own Roman Blinds
Roman blinds are a perennial favourite because they use a lot less fabric than the usual pencil pleated or gathered curtains - as well as making your rooms look streamlined and uncluttered.
During the four weeks you will learn how to accurately measure your window to enable you to create very own customized roman blind. 
Some hardware included in course fee but a list of all other materials (fabric, etc) will be sent out to you when the course is confirmed.
Please choose a narrow window for your first project - max 150cms wide.
By the end of the course you'll be able to
Accurately measure your window 
Calculate how much fabric to purchase
Measure and cut out your curtain
Use appropriate machine skills to sew your curtain fabric
Use finishing skills to apply rings and cords to your curtain
Identify appropriate method to hang Roman Blind
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
ROCK TENORS - ANTHEMS RELOADED TOUR
GET READY KAPITI!...

UNLEASHING THE GREATEST ROCK ANTHEMS OF ALL TIME!

Rock Tenors are about to detonate their brand new show of pure rock energy at Te Raukura ki Kāpiti!

The Anthems Reloaded Tour is a full-throttle celebration of the greatest rock hits of all time, delivered with theatrical firepower and vocal brilliance by five of Aotearoa’s finest. Backed by a live, hard-hitting rock band, this is not just a concert—it’s a full-scale theatrical rock experience.

Soaring harmonies, epic guitar solos, and anthems that defined generations, all reimagined with dramatic vocal flair and explosive energy.

Touring 23 iconic venues nationwide, the Anthems Reloaded Tour is set to ignite your city and pay homage to the worlds greatest rock anthems from artists such as Queen, Bon Jovi, Aerosmith, AC/DC, Black Sabbath, Iron Maiden, White Snake, KISS, TOTO, Guns'N'Roses, Red Hot Chilli Peppers, The Animals, Beatles, Radiohead and many more.

Whether you're a die-hard rocker or a first-time Tenors fan, this is your chance to witness the ultimate fusion of vocal mastery and rock spectacle.

ANTHEMS WILL RISE. CROWDS WILL ROAR. THE ROCK TENORS ARE RELOADED.

For those who have done Spanish Introductory 1 (16 hours) or have equivalent knowledge (e.g. who may have picked up some basic survival language through travel).

events.id: John Maus NZ Tour 2026 | WELLINGTON-Meow,17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: John Maus NZ Tour 2026 | WELLINGTON
events.venue: Meow,17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
John Maus NZ Tour 2026 | WELLINGTON
BANISHED MUSIC & STRANGE NEWS PRESENT
JOHN MAUS ~ NEW ZEALAND TOUR 2026

WEDNESDAY 6 MAY - MEOW, WELLINGTON
THURSDAY 7 MAY - DOUBLE WHAMMY, AUCKLAND

Tickets from Banished Music

The musical career of John Maus has been riddled with iconic moments. Now, to celebrate the release of his brand new album Later Than You Think, the synth-tastic American songwriter with the unmistakable baritone is headed to New Zealand for two extraordinary shows next May. Brought to you by Banished Music and Strange News, Mr Maus is set to make good on a long-held promise.

In 2019, after a series of personal tragedies and the stresses of touring his high-intensity show left Maus feeling unable to perform here, he flew home urgently to recover, with shows here cancelled at short notice. Now, with five years of contemplation, retreat and recuperation, and time spent crafting his new record, John Maus is set to make his return.

Throughout his long career, Maus considered himself to have been “constantly fighting the status quo: the Mickey Mouse Club, Coca-Cola, and all the institutions that reduce culture to its lowest common denominator”. Then, in 2020, he was infamously photographed at the January 6 riots, and was himself promptly “cancelled”.

In hindsight, he says, he now knows he should have been a lot clearer about his ethos and political beliefs in the wake of it all. “I thought my legacy would speak for itself,” Maus told Stereogum in a recent interview. Stating he was only at the protest to shoot footage for a new documentary film alongside film-maker Alex Moyer, these days he realises he ought to have stated his position a lot more bluntly. “I should have been clearer that I’m absolutely against Trumpism,” he says. “It wasn’t as forthright a denunciation as it should have been.”

Powered by “genuine emotion and radical sincerity”, the new album includes I Hate Antichrist, a song Maus describes as a sort of sequel to Cop Killer, and features a secular Gregorian chant that embraces French philosopher Alain Badiou’s anti-xenophobic sentiment that “All the people who are here are from here”.

From the death of ego and descent into madness, this is a party we’re all invited to.
events.description:
John Maus NZ Tour 2026 | WELLINGTON
Brought to you by Banished Music and Strange News, Mr Maus is set to make good on a long-held promise.
Now, with five years of contemplation, retreat and recuperation, and time spent crafting his new record, John Maus is set to make his return.
Stating he was only at the protest to shoot footage for a new documentary film alongside film-maker Alex Moyer, these days he realises he ought to have stated his position a lot more bluntly.
“I should have been clearer that I’m absolutely against Trumpism,” he says.

events.id: THUNDERCAT Wellington - May 6th, Meow Nui (SOLD OUT)-Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6011,92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: THUNDERCAT Wellington - May 6th, Meow Nui (SOLD OUT)
events.venue: Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6011,92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
THUNDERCAT Wellington - May 6th, Meow Nui (SOLD OUT)
Grammy-nominated Bassist, Producer, and Vocalist THUNDERCAT Brings His New Album Distracted To Auckland And Wellington This May.

Known for his jazz-fusion sensibility and elastic funk, Thundercat is bringing his 2026 tour to Auckland and Wellington this May, performing live in support of his new album Distracted. The New Zealand leg of his tour will include a stop at The Powerstation in Auckland on Monday, May 4, 2026, followed by a show at Meow NUI in Wellington on Wednesday, May 6, 2026.

In an age of endless notifications and fractured attention, Thundercat turns the chaos inward on Distracted, his long-awaited new album and one of his most resonant statements yet. Six years on from It Is What It Is, the record captures the restless aftermath of grief and modern overload, balancing existential weight with Thundercat’s unmistakable wit and warmth.

It’s an album that laughs through the noise, folding jazz-fusion virtuosity, elastic funk and late-night introspection into songs that feel like unfiltered inner monologues: tender, funny, and painfully human.

That philosophy carries directly into the live experience. Onstage, Thundercat treats Distracted not as a lecture on modern life, but as a shared release, stretching songs, breaking the fourth wall, leaning into humour and spontaneity as a form of survival.

Thundercats New Zealand shows promise moments of presence inside the noise: music that drifts, refocuses, and reminds you why joy still matters. It’s playful, loose, deeply human, and a rare chance to experience Distracted and all of Thundercat the way it was meant to be felt, not scrolled past.

Six years on from It Is What It Is, the record captures the restless aftermath of grief and modern overload, balancing existential weight with Thundercat’s unmistakable wit and warmth.
That philosophy carries directly into the live experience.
Onstage, Thundercat treats Distracted not as a lecture on modern life, but as a shared release, stretching songs, breaking the fourth wall, leaning into humour and spontaneity as a form of survival.

events.name: Kyla Cobbler — Gone Rogue
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Known for raw storytelling, razor-sharp crowd work, and unfiltered honesty, Kyla Cobbler commands the stage with total confidence. The comic from Cork in Ireland brings her show ‘Gone Rogue’ to Wellington. In it, Kyla covers everything from the chaos of Barcelona dating life, navigating a friend crush, and the power plays of a barbecue. 
Kyla is one of the fastest-rising names in comedy. She first gained attention through her viral Instagram reels before stepping on stage for her first open mic in 2021. Five years later, Kyla has built a global following, with close to 700K Instagram followers and 100M+ video views. 
Kyla brings quickfire wit and a knack for spinning real-life chaos into killer stories. Get ready for a performance that is electric, unpredictable, and unforgettable.

DJ Music Production - An Introduction
Welcome to the world of Electronic Dance Music (EDM). This introductory course will show you the "ins and outs" of producing your own music. Using Fruity Loops SL as the main production program, you'll learn how to compose, arrange, record, edit, mix and master quality music.
While it's designed to complement the "How to be A DJ" course, you can still enrol if you have no experience. 
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Use the basics of the Fruity Loops SL software
- Navigate audio and midi tracks
- Create drum Lines and sequences
- Play basslines and samples
- Render tracks and save formats e.g. mp3, wav etc
- Use basic mixdown and master of music
The course will be held at DJ Shan’s studio at Shop 21, 73 Cuba Street, Wellington.  Bring headphones, a USB and a laptop, if you have one. Limited to eight participants.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Photography: Unlocking the Secrets to Captivating Images
Elevate your photography from ordinary to extraordinary and ensure your work stands out in the digital age. Creating captivating photographs and understanding what makes a photograph engaging is something every photographer should master. Camera settings are essential, but once you know them, you need to learn how to take photographs that stir emotions, impact your audience, and leave a lasting impression. After all, isn’t that the true purpose of photography? People don’t want to see the same landscapes or flowers they’ve seen thousands of times. But how do you turn what you love into something that makes people go, "Wow"?
Captivation in photography means stopping a viewer in their tracks, making them look, feel, and connect. It’s about delivering a photograph that enthrals, evokes profound emotions, and shifts perspectives.
In a world brimming with images, only a few have the power to truly captivate and linger in our memories.
 This pioneering course is your gateway to mastering the art of creating captivating photographs at a time when AI-generated images are saturating our visual landscape. Create something genuine and memorable—images that spark curiosity, evoke emotions, and inspire changes in perception. It's time to define your unique style, refine your vision, and make your mark.
 Course Outcomes
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
Identify the foundational elements that transform an ordinary image into a captivating one.
Create photographs that make people say "Wow" by applying techniques that add a spark of wonder to your shots.
Infuse your photographs with curiosity, making each frame a source of intrigue and interest.
Understand why some images linger in memory and how to make your photographs unforgettable.
Develop a unique style and vision that sets your work apart in the crowded digital landscape.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Garden Design: Landscaping-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Garden Design: Landscaping
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Garden Design: Landscaping
In this fun and interactive class you will be inspired to find answers to develop a simple makeover or design a grand new plan for your own outdoor area. Find solutions to problems and with creativity, develop your own design to suit your site and character.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
 
• Anticipate what hardscaping is required and draw up a full plan for a garden / outdoor space.
• Identify and take into account your preferred style, wish list and work to site conditions (including location and aspect of the property.)
• Accurately draw your detailed design on paper and to scale.
• Use your drawing to build the garden yourself or use it to consult a builder/landscaper to do the hardscaping on your behalf.
• Identify what materials will produce the style / preferred end result
• Work to a budget or know what range of budget to anticipate
 
Please note that this course DOES NOT include:
 
• Planting design, although inclusion of trees will be considered in part of this course. Planting design is expected to be available in a later course.
• Building the garden
• Learning skills to build your garden
• Recommendation of contractors and suppliers for hardscaping

Please bring some scrap paper, a notebook, pens, pencils and an eraser.
 
It is highly recommended you take photos of the area. Bring these in to discuss in class as “personal case studies”.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sofie Hagen: I Think Some of This is My Fault in WELLINGTON-The Fringe Bar,28 Allen Street, Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Sofie Hagen: I Think Some of This is My Fault in WELLINGTON
events.venue: The Fringe Bar,28 Allen Street, Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sofie Hagen: I Think Some of This is My Fault in WELLINGTON
Sofie won Best Newcomer at the Edinburgh Festival in 2015 and she thinks you all stopped talking about it too soon.

Remember TV? Yeah, she did that too. You may know her from that, but it's more likely you know her from the internet, where she's been both funny and angry at times.

Sofie is from Denmark, but learned English at the age of 4, just so she could go to New Zealand in 2026 and do stand-up.

Despite her excellent foresight, she never saw it coming that the fascists would be taking over. (Otherwise, she would have learned Russian, am I right?) Sofie has always tried to do the right thing, but now she has to ask herself: Did she do the right thing? And what will she say, with her last remaining freedom of speech? It better be good.

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Improv for the Stage
Laugh, play characters, get them into trouble (and out again!) and see how your skills translate into confident performance.
This is the WIT Level 2 course and will take your improv that next step beyond the confidence-building of our Joy of Improv, and on into collaborative spontaneous theatre creation.
Suitable for those who have completed a foundation course either with WITor another improv troupe or have some theatrical experience or training.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Create characters and stories in open scenes
• Play and perform several classic improv games
• Stay positive and enjoy failure
• Use improvisation terminology to discuss story, character, comedy, theatre and think about performance (your own and others)
The course will end with a show for invited friends and family.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Photography - Beginners
If you want to get the best results from your camera, you need to learn the fundamental skills and theory of good photography. 
Bring your fully manual-capable (D)SLR or mirrorless camera and owner's manual (if you have one) to the first session.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Operate your camera in manual mode with more confidence 
• Use your camera controls such as shutter speed and aperture with confidence 
• Determine a correct exposure 
• Present and discuss images in a group environment 
• Demonstrate technical concepts through their practical application
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
